A JSTree version of my Perl.Wiki

CPAN::MetaCurator V 1.22. 2026-06-09T16:14:44

My home page, for downloading Perl.Wiki.html

Notes:
⊕ Nodes just under the root ('MetaCurator') are called Topics. Paragraphs is the TiddlyWiki equivalent.
⊕ Tokens like [[XS]] indicate a TiddlyWiki paragraph name, not a Perl module or topic.
⊕ Almost all leaf node are links. Use a middle-button click to open the link in another tab.
⊕ The code does not yet cleanly handle special cases such as the FAQ, pre-formatted paras.
⊕ Also, cases where the para is just a 'See also' are not yet clean.

⊕ My other module, CPAN::MetaPackages, builds & ships an SQLite db containing 02packages.details.txt.
⊕ Most recent version of 02packages.details.txt used: Last-Updated: Mon, 18 May 2026 22:29:02 GMT .
⊕ Acronyms: 259. Known modules: 264312. Leaves: 8164. Topics: 207. Unknown modules: 4907

  • MetaCurator
    • ABeCeDarian
      • ChartingAndPlotting - For Charts::ECharts or Imager, i.e. for all things to do with images - It means in abcd order, i.e. alphabetical, so I can put it first in the list of topics :-).
      • CommandLineStuff
      • CryptoStuff - For random stuff
      • [[FAQ]] - For escaping & quoting special characters
      • FeaturesAndPragmas - For a must-read document
      • FileHandling - For Path::Class, Path::Class::Tiny, Path::Tiny
      • PdfStuff - For html2pdf etc
      • StringStuff
      • SwitchStatements - For Multi::Dispatch, Sub::MultiMethod
      • TestingHelp - For Test::Script etc
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/robert_acock/2026/01/ready-set-compile-you-slow-camel.html
      • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/serialisation-in-perl/ - Serialization of data to ram/disk
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CSVUtils - CLI utilities related to CSV.
      • The module contains 64 scripts
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-SQLiteUtils - Utilities related to SQLite.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Maker-Bootstrapper - Scaffold a new CPAN distribution in one command.
      • Contains CPAN::Maker::ConfigReader etc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cron-Toolkit - Quartz-compatible cron parser with unique extensions and over 400 tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CXC::Exporter::Util - Tagged Based Exporting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::KSUID - KSUID are a globally unique identifier similar to UUIDs but designed to be naturally sorted by generation timestamp and to have representations that are easily postable across systems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::OptList::Object - Data::OptList, but object-oriented.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Path::XS - Fast path-based access to nested data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::SQLite - Self-contained RDBMS in a DBI Driver.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::SQLite::Constants - Common SQLite constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::AutoUpgrade::NativeStrings - Automatically upgrade Perl native strings to utf8 before sending them to the database.
      • Primarily for DBD::Oracle, DPD::Pg and DBD::SQLite
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dev-Util - Base modules for Perl Development.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Bug - Transparent inline debugging probe (pure Perl).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/e - Beast mode unleashed.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Stuffer - A more casual approach to creating and sending Email:: emails.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Sticker - Read, Write file meta-data.
      • Includes sticker which sticks tags onto files, populates a database with file meta-data
      • See also Data::TagDB in DatabaseAndSQL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Ixchel - Automate various sys admin stuff.
      • Contains about 50 modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-Util-Find - List utilities related to finding items.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Codes - A distribution of many modules to handle locale codes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Melian - Perl client to the Melian cache.
      • More details in CachingStuff
      • https://gotify.net/ - Gotify client for Perl.
      • I feel this may become extremely popular
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-IPData - Perl client for the ipdata.co IP geolocation and threat intelligence API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Server - Extensible, general Perl server engine.
      • Has a massive amount of flexibility
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Finance-Human - Human-readable numbers for accounting and finance.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::InsideOut - Comprehensive inside-out object support module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::InsideOut::Metadata - Introspection for Object::InsideOut classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Path-Tiny - Provides methods like slurp_utf8 and spew_utf8 to read and write files using the correct encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAX - Perl Adaptive eXecution compiler and standalone binary packager.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Reuse - Reuse and mass produce PDF documents.
      • This module has an important See Also
      • https://poppler.freedesktop.org/ - Poppler is a PDF rendering library based on the xpdf-3.0 code base.
      • Appears to be installed as part of Debian
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Community - Community-inspired Perl::Critic policies.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::DiscouragedModules - Various modules discouraged from use.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::PreferredAlternatives - Various modules with preferred alternatives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Random-Simple - Simple, usable, real world random numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rose-DB - A DBI wrapper and abstraction layer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rose-DB-Object - Extensible, high performance object-relational mapper (ORM).
      • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Tutorial - A guided tour of the basics of Rose::DB::Object
      • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Loader, Rose::DB::Object::Manager, etc
      • See also https://dev.to/dragostrif/rosedb-orm-and-perl-3dig
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Server::Starter - A superdaemon for hot-deploying server programs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Wizard - Composable SQL query builder with expression trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Escape - Backslash escapes, quoted phrase, word elision, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::ShellQuote - Quote strings for passing through the shell.
      • Does not decode ANSI escape codes
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Most-Explain - Enhanced diagnostics for Test::More with structured diffs and helpful hints.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Stencil - Fast XS list/table renderer with escaping, formatting, and transform chaining.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table::Manifold - Render tables in manifold formats.
      • Its See Also lists about similar 40 modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table::Tiny - Generate simple text tables from 2D arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Subs - Execute selected subs concurrently in worker threads.
      • The POD is very sophisticated
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Moment - Represents a date and time of day with an offset from UTC.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TOML - Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language is a file format for configuration files.
      • Supports the following data types: string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, and table
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Registry - A glorified hashref for looking up type constraints.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Type-Tiny - Tiny, yet Moo(se)-compatible, type constraint.
      • Contains Types::Standard and many other modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Common - The one stop shop.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/utf8-all - Turn on Unicode - all of it.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::CheckUTF8 - Checks if scalar is valid UTF-8.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Unicode-UTF8 - Encoding and decoding of UTF-8 encoding form.

      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asynchronous_I/O - Non-Blocking Interface (or sometimes Non-Blocking Invocations)..
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intrusion_detection_system - Network intrusion detection systems.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noise_Protocol_Framework - Noise Protocol Framework.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nostr - Notes and Other Stuff Transmitted by Relays.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Name_server - Name server.
      • http://nws.noaa.gov/ - National Weather Service (USA).
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cisco_NX-OS - A network operating system for the Nexus-series Ethernet switches and MDS-series Fibre Channel storage area network switches made by Cisco Systems.
      • Aka NXOS
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Optimal_asymmetric_encryption_padding - Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OAuth - Open Authentication (OATH).
      • See also TOTP
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Obsessive%E2%80%93compulsive_disorder - Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder.
      • See ERP
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenDocument - OpenDocument Format.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenID#OpenID_Connect_(OIDC) - OpenID Connect.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Common_data_model#Health - Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Network_File_System - Open Network Computing.
      • See also RPC
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OPC_Unified_Architecture - Open Platform Communications Unified Architecture.
      • Aka IEC 62541
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAPI_Specification - OpenAPI Specification, previously known as the Swagger Specification, is a specification for a machine-readable interface definition language for describing, producing, consuming and visualizing web services.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - Observe, Orient, Decide, Act.
      • It Is a framework to understand decision-making in adversarial situations
      • https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8361091/ - Open Database API for Materials Design.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OSLV-Monitor - OS-Level Virtualization.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc2898 - Password-based cryptography key derivation functions.
      • Obsoleted by PKCS
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlBuildSystem - Perl Build System.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Process_design_kit - Process Design Kit.
      • See EDA above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDL - The Perl Data Language.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peer_exchange - Peer Exchange.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PharmGKB - The Pharmacogenomics Knowledgebase.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc8018 - Public-Key Cryptography Standards.
      • Replaces rfc2898
      • https://metacpan.org/release/CHOROBA/Treex-PML-2.29 - Prague Markup Language.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/POE - A framework for cooperative, event driven multitasking and networking.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Principle_of_least_astonishment - Principle of least astonishment.
      • Aka POLS - Principle of least surprise
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/POSIX - Portable Operating System Interface.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-quantum_cryptography - Post-quantum cryptography.
      • https://webdyne.org/00_introduction/ - A html template suffix used by WebDyne.
      • See [[HTTPServers]]
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_field_theory - Quantum Field Theory.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Named_graph - A specialized type of database designed to store and query 'quads'. It is essentially an evolution of a triplestore, which is used for RDF data.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quartz_(scheduler) - The Quartz Scheduler is a powerful, open-source job scheduling library for Java applications.
      • For usage in Perl, see RRD::Fetch in DatabaseAndSQL
      • RRD itself is defined below
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Latin_phrases_(full)#quod_vide - which see. Used after a term, phrase, or topic that should be looked up elsewhere in the current document.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retrieval-augmented_generation - Retrieval-augmented generation.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resource_acquisition_is_initialization - Resource Acquisition Is Initialization.
      • https://dbpedia.org/page/RAR_(file_format) - Roshal ARchive - A proprietary archive file format that supports data compression, error correction and file spanning.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Role-based_access_control - Role-Based Access Control.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Remote_administration - Remote administration includes any method of controlling a computer or other Internet-connected device, such as a smartphone, from a remote location. Think Minecraft server.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Registration_Data_Access_Protocol - Registration Data Access Protocol.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7483
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resource_Description_Framework - Resource Description Framework.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N-Triples
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N-Triples#N-Quads
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RDM_(lighting) - Remote Device management.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_RPC - Remote Procedure Call.
      • See also ONC
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resource_Public_Key_Infrastructure - Resource Public Key Infrastructure.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RPM_Package_Manager - Originally Red Hat Package Manager, now a recursive acronym for RPM Package Manager.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RRDtool - Round-Robin Database.
      • For usage in Perl, see RRD::Fetch in DatabaseAndSQL
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RSS - RDF Site Summary or Really Simple Syndication.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/ - Request For Comment.
      • https://requesttracker.com/rtir/ - Request Tracker for Incident Response.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CSS::SAC - Simple API for CSS.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Source_Code_Control_System - Source Code Control System.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_configuration_management - Software Configuration Management.
      • https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/ - Simple DirectMedia Layer.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single_instruction,_multiple_data - Single Instruction, Multiple Data) registers, a feature of Intel chips.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single_instruction%2C_multiple_data - Single instruction, multiple data.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3261 - Session Initiation Protocol.
      • https://slurm.schedmd.com/ - Simple Linux Utility for Resource Management.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-Monitoring,_Analysis_and_Reporting_Technology - Self-Monitoring, Analysis, and Reporting Technology.
      • https://gemini.google.com/app/9185e03e7c67f3b2 - State, Operator, And Result or Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SPARQL - SPARQL Protocol and RDF Query Language.
      • - System Package Data Exchange.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sender_Policy_Framework - Sender Policy Framework.
      • https://danielmiessler.com/blog/spqa-ai-architecture-replace-existing-software - State, Policy, Questions, Action.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg12 - Sparse Post Quantum Ratchet.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SSL - Secure Sockets Layer.
      • Obsoleted by TSL
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ext4 - (part of) Fourth extended filesystem.
      • See also EXT4
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Streaming_Text_Oriented_Messaging_Protocol - Streaming Text Orientated Messaging Protocol.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SWF - ShockWave Flash.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interactive_Advertising_Bureau - Transparency and Consent Framework.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc9293 - Transmission Control Protocol.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg20 - Trusted Execution Environments aka Secure Enclaves.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TeX - TeX is a popular means of typesetting complex mathematical formulae.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TOML - Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language is a file format for configuration files.
      • Supports the following data types: string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, and table
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Token-Oriented_Object_Notation - Token-Oriented Object Notation for Perl.
      • http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6238 - Time-Based One-Time Password Algorithm.
      • See also OATH
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third-party_management - Third-party risk management.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Triplestore - A triplestore or RDF store is a purpose-built database for the storage and retrieval of triples through semantic queries.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transport_Layer_Security - Transport Layer Security.
      • Successor to SSL
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turtle_(syntax) - Terse RDF Triple Language.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::DB2 - IBM's DB2 Universal Database.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc768 - User Datagram Protocol.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/User_Datagram_Protocol
      • https://unicode.org/reports/tr9/#:~:text=A%20Unicode%20Standard%20Annex%20(UAX,version%20of%20the%20Unicode%20Standard. - Unicode Standard Annex.

      • Aka Unicode
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universal_Decimal_Classification - Universal Decimal Classification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sekhmet - Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier.
      • https://www.cognex.com/resources/symbologies/1-d-linear-barcodes/upc-a-barcodes#:~:text=The%20UPC-A%20barcode%20is,been%20in%20use%20since%201974 - Stands for Universal Product Code, Version A.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986 - Uniform Resource Identifiers (absolute and relative).
      • A compact sequence of characters for describing a range of Uniform Resource Identifiers through variable expansion
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6570
      • See also URL below
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986 - Uniform Resource Locator.
      • These are a subset or URI above
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3629 - A transformation format of ISO 10646.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4122 - A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Variable-length_quantity - Variable-Length Quantity.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vonage - Vonage Holdings Corp is an American cloud communications provider operating as a subsidiary of Ericsson.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WebDAV - Web Distributed Authoring and Versioning.
      • https://encyclopedia2.thefreedictionary.com/WebP - Bitmapped graphics file format from Google.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Geodetic_System#WGS84 - World Geodetic System.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Meteorological_Organization - World Meteorological Organization.

      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4506 - External Data Representation Standard.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4122 - Extensible Message Passing Protocol.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XPDL - The XML Process Definition Language.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cross-site_scripting - Cross Site Scripting.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zero-configuration_networking - Zero-configuration networking.
      • Similar to DNS-SD

      • BusinessApps - For App::karr - [[Acronyms]].

      • https://github.com/ggerganov/ggml - For ggml source code

      • https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro
      • https://itsfoss.com/ollama/ - Ollama lets you run various open source LLMs locally
      • https://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html - For Mojolicious re MCP::*
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Claude-Shell - AI-powered interactive shell using Claude Agent SDK.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-MCP - Cheap Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AI-ActivationFunctions - Activation functions for neural networks in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AI::Chat - Interact with AI Chat APIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-ggml - Find or build the ggml tensor library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Chit - Chat with AI from the command line.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AI-Ollama-Client - Client for AI::Ollama (not on CPAN).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Raider - Autonomous CLI agent that can browse directories, edit files, and run bash commands.
      • Uses AI engine Langertha
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Claude::Agent - Perl SDK for the Claude Agent SDK.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Claude-Agent-Code-Refactor - Automated code refactoring with review-fix loops.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Claude-Agent-Code-Review - AI-powered code review using Claude.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Eliza-Chatbot - Eliza chatbot.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Langertha - The clan of fierce vikings with ðª and ð¡ï¸ to AId your rAId.
      • This module is designed to facilitate interactions with various AI models
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Langertha-Knarr - LLM Proxy with Langfuse Tracing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Langertha::Raider - Autonomous agent with conversation history and MCP tools.
      • See App::Raider
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Langertha-Skeid - Dynamic routing control-plane for multi-node LLM serving with normalized metrics and cost accounting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LLM-SimpleClient - Perl module for making single API requests to LLM providers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lugh - Pure C LLM Inference Engine for Perl (built on ggml).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MCP - Model Context Protocol Perl SDK.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MCP-Run - MCP server with a command execution tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MCP::Server - MCP server implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MCP::Server::Run - MCP server with a command execution tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MCP-Wiki - Markdown wiki MCP server with TOC extraction and git history.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-Crawl4AI - IO::Async Crawl4AI client with an async strategy chain.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-Firecrawl - IO::Async Firecrawl v2 client with flow helpers.
      • See also WWW::Firecrawl below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Gemini - A small gemini client.
      • See also TheRFCGuide for rfc2045
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenAPI-Client-OpenAI - A client for the (Microsoft) OpenAI API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sim-AgentSoar - SOAR-inspired explicit search with a pluggable LLM worker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sim-Agent - Deterministic, file-backed orchestration of LLM workers and critics via a small S-expression DSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Chroma - Chromadb client.
      • Chroma is the AI-native open-source vector database. Chroma makes it easy to build LLM apps by making knowledge, facts, and skills pluggable for LLMs
      • https://docs.trychroma.com/docs/overview/getting-started
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WebService::Ollama - Ollama client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wordsmith-Claude - AI-powered text rewriting with style.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Crawl4AI - Perl client and fallback orchestrator for Crawl4AI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::Firecrawl - Firecrawl v2 API bindings (self-host first, cloud compatible).
      • https://www.firecrawl.dev/
      • See also Net::Async::Firecrawl above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-VastAI - Perl client for the Vast.ai REST APIs.
    • AlgorithmicStuff
      • EventStuff - DatabaseAndSQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Action::Retry - Module to try to perform an action, with various ways of retrying and sleeping between retries.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Action::Retry::Strategy::Constant - Constant sleep time strategy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Action::Retry::Strategy::Fibonacci - Fibonacci incrementation of sleep time strategy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Action::Retry::Strategy::Linear - Linear incrementation of sleep time strategy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff - Various backoff strategies for retry.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::Constant - Backoff using a constant delay.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::Exponential - Backoff exponentially.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::Fibonacci - Backoff using Fibonacci sequence.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::LILD - Linear Increment, Linear Decrement (LILD) backoff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::LIMD - Linear Increment, Multiplicative Decrement (LIMD) backoff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::MILD - Multiplicative Increment, Linear Decrement (MILD) backoff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::MIMD - Multiplicative Increment, Multiplicative Decrement (MIMD) backoff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Backoff::RetryTimeouts - A backoff-style retry algorithm with adjustable timeout support.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-X - Solve exact cover problems with Algorithm-X and Dancing Links.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::DLX V 0.03 - Solve exact cover problems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::AlgorithmBackoffUtils - Utilities related to Algorithm::Backoff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Retry::Backoff - Retry a piece of code, with backoff strategies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Retry-Policy - Simple retry wrapper with exponential backoff and jitter.
    • AnimationStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-FfmpegUtils - Utilities related to ffmpeg.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Automate::Animate::FFmpeg - Create animation from a sequence of images using FFmpeg.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Scan-ClamAV - Connect to a local Clam Anti-Virus clamd service and send commands.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/YaraFFI - Minimal Perl FFI bindings for the YARA malware scanning engine.
      • https://yara.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

      • Regexp::Long::Common
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Apache::Session - A persistence framework for session data.

      • Written to support Lemonldap::NG. See SystemMonitoring

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Apache2-API - Apache2 API Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Avro - The official Perl API for the Apache Avro project's serialization and RPC framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Common::Log::Parser - Parse the common log format lines used by Apache.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/sealed - Monkey-patch :Sealed registry handler() into ModPerl::RegistryCooker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-API-REST - Framework for RESTful APIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Kafka-Producer-Avro - Apache Kafka message producer based on librdkafka, Avro serialization and Confluent Schema Registry validation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ThreatDetector - Apache log parser for threat detection & classification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thrift - Apache Thrift is a software framework for scalable cross-language services development..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Thrift::XS - Faster Thrift binary protocol encoding and decoding.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mac::FSEvents - Monitor a directory structure for changes.

    • ArchiveFiles
      • FileHandling - [[Acronyms]].
      • Games - for more SCS stuff
      • IoLayers
      • TheRFCGuide
      • ftp://ftp.info-zip.org/pub/infozip/doc/ - For zip file details
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress/view/lib/IO/Compress/FAQ.pod
      • https://pkware.cachefly.net/webdocs/casestudies/APPNOTE.TXT - For zip file details
      • https://sourceware.org/bzip2/ - For info re bzip2
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlop#I%2FO-Operators - For I/O operators
      • https://oldhome.schmorp.de/marc/liblzf.html - For lzf
      • https://www.winzip.com/en/support/aes-encryption/ - AES Encryption Information
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::ultraviolet - Alien package for Python uv.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-SevenZip - Read/write 7z, zip, ISO9960 and other archives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-CAR - Content Addressable Archive (CAR) reader and writer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Extract - A generic archive extracting mechanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/NICOMEN/Archive-Lha-0.11 - Read and extract .LZH/.LHA archives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-Libarchive-Compress - Recursively archive a directory (using libarchive).
      • Contains many modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-Libarchive-Peek - Peek into archives without extracting them.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-Tar-Stream - Pure Perl IO-friendly tar file management.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::SCS - SCS archive controller.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Tar - Module for manipulations of tar archives.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Tar::Wrapper - API wrapper around the 'tar' utility.
      • This module differs from Archive::Tar in 2 ways
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Unzip::Burst - Featureless but fast ZIP extraction.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Zip - Provide an interface to ZIP archive files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Zip::SimpleZip - Create Zip Archives.
      • Supports deflate, store, bzip2, Zstandard (Zstd), Xz and lzma compression
      • Contains Archive::Zip::SimpleUnzip
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::Zip::SimpleUnzip - Read Zip Archives.
      • Supports deflate, store, bzip2, Zstandard (Zstd), Xz and lzma compression
      • Contained in Archive::Zip::SimpleZip
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-zipdetails - Display the internal structure of zip files.
      • The zipinfo program that comes with the info-zip distribution (http://www.info-zip.org/) can also display details of the structure of a zip file
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/gb64 - Fast Pure Perl Base64 Encoding and Decoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Compress-Raw-Bzip2 - Low-Level Interface to bzip2 compression library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Compress-Raw-Lzma - Low-Level Perl Interface to lzma compression library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Compress-Raw-Zlib - Low-Level Interface to zlib or zlib-ng compression library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Compress-Stream-Zstd - Perl interface to the Zstd (Zstandard) (de)compressor.
      • Works with IO::Compress::Zstd
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Compress::Zlib - Interface to zlib compression library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Compress::Zstd - Perl interface to the Zstd (Zstandard) (de)compressor.
      • No dictionary support but support ticket has sample code
      • But see Compress::Stream::Zstd
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Compression-Util - Implementation of various techniques used in data compression.
      • Contains
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Unpack2 - A strong bz2/gz/zip/tar/cpio/rpm/deb/cab/lzma/7z/rar/... archive unpacker, based on mime-types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FU::Util - Miscellaneous Utility Functions.
      • Supports booleans, brotli, file descriptor passing, gzip, HTTP dates and json
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Gzip-Faster - Simple and fast gzip and gunzip.
      • This module provides an interface to libdeflate. Libdeflate is a newly-written compression library which offers the same deflate form of compression as zlib, as used in gzip, libpng and many similar utilities. This distribution includes libdeflate within itself, so it is not necessary to have that on your system - Libdeflate compression library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gzip::RandomAccess - Extract arbitrary bits of a gzip stream.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress/view/lib/IO/Compress/FAQ.pod - Is an interface to zlib compression library.
      • Contains streamzip to create a zip file from stdin
      • Contains zipdetails to display the internal structure of zip files
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Base - Base Class for IO::Compress modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Brotli - Write Brotli buffers/streams.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Bzip2 - Write bzip2 files/buffers.
      • See also IO::Uncompress::Bunzip2
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Deflate - Write RFC 1950 files/buffers.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1950
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions about IO::Compress.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Gzip - Write RFC 1952 files/buffers.
      • See also IO::Uncompress::Gunzip
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1952
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress-Lzf - Write lzf files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress-Lzma - Write lzip files/buffers.
      • Contains IO::Compress::Lzma and IO::Compress::Xz and the 3 uncompressers too
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Lzma - Write lzma files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress-Lzop - Write lzop files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::RawDeflate - Write RFC 1951 files/buffers.
      • See RfGuide for rfc1951
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Xz - Write xz files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Compress::Zip - Write zip files/buffers.
      • Supports store, deflate, bzip2, lzma, xz, zstd
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Compress-Zstd - Write zstd files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::Bunzip2 - Read bzip2 files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::Gunzip - Read RFC 1952 files/buffers.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1952
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::RawInflate - Read RFC 1951 files/buffers.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1951
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::UnLzma - Read lzma files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::UnXz - Read xz files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uncompress::Unzip - Read zip files/buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Zlib - IO:: style interface to Compress::Zlib.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAR - Perl Archive Toolkit.
      • This module lets you use special zip files, called Perl Archives, as libraries from which Perl modules can be loaded
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAR::Packer - PAR Packager.
      • This module implements the App::Packer::Backend interface, for generating stand-alone executables, perl scripts and PAR files
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sereal - Fast, compact, powerful binary (de-)serialization.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sereal-Decoder - Fast, compact, powerful binary deserialization.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sereal-Encoder - Fast, compact, powerful binary serialization.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Stream - Provide input & output encoding & decoding.
      • Used by Hypersonic
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/streamzip - Create a zip file from stdin.
      • Supports store, deflate, bzip2, lzma, xz, zstd
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XS::libbrotli - Brotli compression library for XS..
    • ArchOS
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cpan2arch - Generate PKGBUILD from CPAN metadata.
    • ArrayHandling
      • DataValidation - DataTraversal.
      • HashHandling - For Data::MultiValuedHash
      • NewOperators
      • [[XS]]
      • List::MoreUtils
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Autojoin - Arrayrefs that stringify as join(", ", @$it).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Circular - Provide an array data structure that can go around in circles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Compare - Perl extension for comparing arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Diff - Find the differences between two arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Intersection - Exports an array intersection function.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Iterator - A simple class for iterating over Perl arrays.
      • Other modules discussed in the POD: Class::Iter, Class::Iterator, Data::Iter, Data::Iterator::EasyObj and Tie::Array::Iterable
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Iterator::Circular - This iterator will loop continuously as long as next or get_next is called.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Join - Performs SQL-like joins on arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Merge::Unique - Merge those arrays uniquely.
      • See also List::MoreUtils
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Array-Objectify - Objectify an array.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::PseudoScalar - Arrays that behave like scalars.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Array-Transpose - Transposes a 2-Dimensional Array.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::LnArray - The great new Data::LnArray.
      • Arrays with lots of access methods
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-LnArray-XS - Arrays as just above.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Table - Data type related to database tables, spreadsheets, CSV/TSV files, HTML table displays, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HATX - A fluent interface for Hash and Array Transformations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::MoreUtils - Provide the stuff missing in List::Util.
      • Note: List::Util::XS is available - This is a core module.
      • use List::Util 'any' or, better, 'first' (but don't use first() for finding 0 or undef!)
      • And then 1 of
      • if(any { $_ eq $thing } @values) ...
      • if(any { $_ == $thing } @values) ...
      • It's not limited to just string or number comparison of course; you can
      • Or
      • If you don't like the XSUB call overhead of List::Util::any, there's
      • use List::Keywords 'any';
      • if(any { $_ eq $thing } @values) ...
      • Or
      • if (grep { $_ eq $myval } @arr)
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::UtilsBy - Higher-order list utility functions.
      • Amazing!
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-MatrixBool - Matrix of Booleans.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-MatrixReal - Matrix of Reals.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::Zip - Infix operator to compose two lists together.
      • Provides 2 functions: mesh and zip, both of which are in List::Util
      • Also, List::UtilsBy contains zip_by and unzip_by

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SpeL-Wizard - Engine to build audio files from the spel files generated by SpeL.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SWF - An autoloadable interface module for Ming - a library for generating ShockWave Flash format movies.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SWF-File - Create SWF file.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/VideoLAN-LibVLC - Wrapper for libvlc.so.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-NKC2MARC - Tool to fetch record from National library of the Czech Republic to MARC file.

      • Contains Business::ISBN10 and Business::ISBN13
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-ISBN-Data - Data pack for Business::ISBN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-UDC - Class to work with Universal Decimal Classification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-MARC-Field008 - Data object for MARC field 008.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-MARC-Leader - Data object for MARC leader.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::MARC::Validator::Report - Data object for MARC validator report.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Qiime2-Artifact - A parser for Qiime2 artifact files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC - Perl extension to manipulate MAchine Readable Cataloging records.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::Charset - Convert MARC-8 encoded strings to UTF-8.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Convert-Wikidata-Object - Bibliographic Wikidata object defined by MARC record.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Field008 - Class for parsing and serialization of MARC field 008.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Field008-L10N - Localization framework for MARC::Field008.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Field008-Print - MARC 008 field class for print.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::File::USMARC - USMARC-specific file handling.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Leader - MARC leader class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Leader-L10N - Localization framework for MARC::Leader.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::Leader::Print - MARC Leader class for print.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Leader-Utils - Utilities for MARC::Leader.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::Lint - Perl extension for checking validity of MARC records.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::Record - Perl extension for handling MARC records.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MARC::Record::Stats - Scans one or many MARC::Record and gives a statistics on the tags and subtags.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Validator - Set of plugins for MARC validation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MARC-Validator-Filter - MARC validator filter plugins.

      • Uses Net::Z3950::SimpleServer
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Z3950::SimpleServer - Simple Perl API for building Z39.50 servers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/NKC::Transform::BIBFRAME2MARC - bibframe2marc transformation class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/NKC::Transform::BIBFRAME2MARC::Utils - Utilities for bibframe2marc transformations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NKC-Transform-MARC2BIBFRAME - marc2bibframe transformation class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/NKC::Transform::MARC2RDA - marc2rda transformation class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::Scraper::ISBN - Retrieve information about books from online sources.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ZOOM - Perl extension implementing the ZOOM API for Information Retrieval.
      • Contains Net::Z3950::ZOOM

    • BsdWare
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/BSD::Resource - BSD process resource limit and priority functions.
      • In rare cases these use different options or work differently than standard Linux tools, which can lead to confusion if they and the Linux versions are both on your PATH. I personally deleted them when I hit this problem (or rather, it hit me) - Power Tools (BSD utilities in pure Perl).

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Money - Money/currency with formatting and overloading..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-MoneyCurrency - Get information for different currencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/EAI::Wrap - Framework for easy creation of Enterprise Application Integration tasks.
      • Functionality: DB, DateUtil, FTP and File
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Calendar - Represents the trading calendar.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Finance::Currency::Convert - Convert currencies and fetch their exchange rates (with Finance::Quote).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Currency-Convert-WebserviceX - Lightweight currency conversion using WebserviceX.NET.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Exchange - Represents a financial stock exchange object..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Finance::GDAX::API::Currency - Currencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-MIFIR-CONCAT - Provides CONCAT code generation out of client data according to MIFIR rules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Quote - Get stock and mutual fund quotes from various exchanges.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Tax-Aruba - A package that deals with tax calculations for Aruba.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Finance-Tiller2QIF - Convert Tiller CSV exports to QIF for import into Financial software like GnuCash, KMyMoney, Quicken, HomeBank, Money Manager Ex and many others.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lib-Pepper - Perl bindings for the Pepper payment terminal library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Currency - Module for dealing with currency code sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Currency::Format - Perl functions for formatting monetary values.
      • Masser is a perl-based fastcgi-driven application framework - https://masser.sourceforge.net/.
      • Note: masser::home is not a Perl module
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Currency - Exact Currency Math with Formatting and Rounding.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NBI-Slurm - NBI Slurm module.
      • SLURM stands for Simple Linux Utility for Resource Management
      • Debian via apt has available 7 basic components of SLURM

      • https://stripe.com/au
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDK-Utils - Provides utility functions for managing and parsing PDK configuration contexts.

      • https://developer.paypal.com/docs/api/orders/v2/

    • CachingStuff
      • Bread::Board - Curio.
      • MooX::Singleton
      • ParallelProcessing
      • PipeLines
      • CHI::Driver::SharedMem
      • https://github.com/xsawyerx/melian/ - For Melian the sever
      • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/caching-in-perl/ - A great HOWTO
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/BerkeleyDB - Perl extension for Berkeley DB version 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Very fast (data ends up mostly in shared memory cache) but acts as a database overall, so data is not automatically expired
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache%3A%3ACache - The Cache interface.
      • But see CHI, Cache::FastMmap below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cache-FastMmap - Uses an mmap'ed file to act as a shared memory interprocess cache.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::FileCache - Iimplements the Cache interface.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Lots of features, slow
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::File::Simple - Dead simple file based caching meachanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::Memcached::Fast - Perl client for memcached, in C language.
      • The API is largely compatible with Cache::Memcached, the original pure Perl client
      • See Debian.Wiki.html's Installation para for installation of memcached
      • See http://www.memcached.org for the memory cache daemon
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::Memcached::Fast::Safe - Cache::Memcached::Fast with sanitizing keys and fork-safe.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cache-Memcached-PDeque - Implements a priority deque using memcached as storage.
      • This is an implementation of a double-ended queue, with support for priorities
      • Implements the Cache interface
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::MemoryCache
      • But see CHI., Cache::FastMmap below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::Mmap - Shared data cache using memory mapped files.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Similar to Cache::FastMmap, in pure perl. slows down with larger pages
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cache::SharedMemoryCache - Extends the MemoryCache class.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Lots of features, VERY slow. Uses IPC::ShareLite which freeze/thaws ALL data at each read/write
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CHI - Unified cache handling interface.
      • The successor to Cache::Cache
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-Simple-Cached - Cache messages to an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Simple::Readonly::Cached - Cache messages to an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-BitSet-Shared - Shared-memory fixed-size bitset for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Buffer - Read/write buffer class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Buffer-Shared - Type-specialized shared-memory buffers for multiprocess access.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Deque-Shared - Shared-memory double-ended queue for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Graph-Shared - Shared-memory directed weighted graph for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HashMap-Shared - Type-specialized shared-memory hash maps for multiprocess access.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Heap-Shared - Shared-memory binary min-heap (priority queue) for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Log-Shared - Append-only shared-memory log (WAL) for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-PubSub-Shared - High-performance shared-memory pub/sub for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Pool-Shared - Fixed-size shared-memory object pool for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Queue-Shared - High-performance shared-memory MPMC queues for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-ReqRep-Shared - High-performance shared-memory request/response IPC for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-RingBuffer-Shared - Shared-memory fixed-size ring buffer for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Stack-Shared - Shared-memory LIFO stack for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Sync-Shared - Shared-memory synchronization primitives for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBI - Database independent interface for Perl.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Use your favourite RDBMS. can perform well, need a DB server running. very global. socket connection latency
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Meta-Cache - Cache open file descriptors and stat meta data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Cache::Transparent - Cache the result of http get-requests persistently.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::MM - Perl interface to Ralf Engelschall's mm library.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Hash implementation is broken, data is not automatically expired, slow
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LCC - Content Provider Modules for the Local Content Cache system.
      • See also https://sourceforge.net/projects/lococa/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Legba - Global slot storage implemented in XS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LRU-Cache - LRU cache with O(1) operation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Melian - Perl client to the Melian cache.
      • A quote from Gemini: Melian (the cache server by Sawyer X and Gonzalo Diethelm) is a fantastic addition to the "High Performance" section of your wiki. It uses a custom binary protocol over UNIX or TCP sockets for microsecond-level lookups - great companion to the modern Perl stack
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Memoize - Make functions faster by trading space for time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Memoize::Expire - Plug-in module for automatic expiration of memoized values.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MLDBM::Sync - Safe concurrent access to MLDBM databases.
      • (Cache::FastMmap says) Acts as a database, data is not automatically expired, slow
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Clacks - Fast client/server interprocess messaging and memcached replacement.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Resource-Silo - Lazy declarative resource container for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Mechanize-Cached - Cache response to be polite.
    • CaptchaStuff
      • https://developers.google.com/recaptcha/docs/v3 - https://cloud.google.com/security/products/recaptcha.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Captcha-Stateless-Text - Stateless, text-based CAPTCHAs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Captcha::reCAPTCHA - A Perl implementation of the reCAPTCHA API version 0.99.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Captcha::reCAPTCHA::V2 - A Perl implementation of reCAPTCHA API version 2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Captcha-reCAPTCHA-V3 - A Perl implementation of reCAPTCHA API version v3.
    • ChartingAndPlotting
      • Chart - BarCodes.
      • Chart::Clicker
      • Chart::GGPlot
      • Chart::Plotly
      • Color::Library
      • Convert::Color
      • Imager::Plot
      • VS::Chart
      • ChartingAndPlotting
      • [[Color]]
      • [[Graphs]]
      • PdfStuff
      • PdlLanguage
      • https://echarts.apache.org/
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ggplot2
      • https://github.com/aluntzer/gtknodes/
      • https://plot.ly/javascript/reference/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::CFITSIO - Build and Install the CFITSIO library.
      • See also https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/docs/software/fitsio/fitsio.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-GtkNodes - Find or install GtkNodes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-BlurFill - A simple command line tool to create a blurred background image.
      • Command line access to a Dancer app
      • curl -OJ -X POST http://localhost:5000/blur -F "image=@input.jpg"
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::gimpgitbuild - Build GIMP from git.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ImageMagickUtils - Utilities related to ImageMagick .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Image-Generator - Perl class for image-generator application.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::imgsize - Show dimensions of image files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cairo - Perl interface to the cairo 2d vector graphics library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chart::ECharts - Apache ECharts wrapper for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::LibMagic - Determine MIME types of data or files using libmagic.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GD - Interface to Gd Graphics Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GD::Polyline - Polyline object and Polygon utilities (including splines) for use with GD.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GD::Simple - Simplified interface to GD library.
      • Tutorial on usage at http://perlmaven.com/drawing-images-using-gd-simple
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GD::SVG - Seamlessly enable SVG output from scripts written using GD.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gimp - Write GIMP extensions/plug-ins/load- and save-handlers in Perl.
      • Git repo for the module is at https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/gimp-perl
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Gnuplot-Builder - Object-oriented gnuplot script builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-Framebuffer - A Simple Framebuffer Graphics Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-Penplotter-GcodeXY - Produce gcode files for pen plotters from Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-D3 - A simple Perl module for generating bar charts using D3.js.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-APNG - Generate Animated PNG (APNG) files from individual PNG images.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Base - Base class for loading, manipulating and saving images.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Base-GD - Draw images with GD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Bitmap2Paths - Perl extension to convert line-drawing (and font's) bitmaps to paths (of vector formats).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Image::Dot - Create 1x1 pixel image files (pure-perl).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Image::Epeg - Thumbnail jpegs at lightning speed.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-ExifTool - The command line tool exiftool has many, many options and supports a vast array of types.
      • https://exiftool.org/
      • http://exiftool.sourceforge.net/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-ExifTool/ - ExifTool tag name documentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Info - Extract meta information from image files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-JPEG-Size - Find the size of JPEG images.
      • https://imagemagick.org/Usage/ - sudo apt-get install imagemagick.
      • Site has a list of exploits and many examples
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Magick-Safer - Wrap Image::Magick Read method to check magic bytes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Image::Magick::Thumbnail::PDF - Make thumbnail of a page in a pdf document.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Image::MetaData::JPEG - Perl extension for showing/modifying JPEG (meta)data.
      • But see Image::ExifTool
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Image::PNG::Libpng - Perl interface to the C library libpng.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-PNG-QRCode - ABANDONED - Make PNG images containing QR codes from your text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-Synchronize - Synchronize filesystem modification timestamps of images, movies, and related files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-ThumbHash - A very compact representation of an image placeholder.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Imager - Perl extension for Generating 24 bit Images.
      • To get my demo test.imager.tiles.pl of Imager running, I had to do
      • sudo aptitude install libpng-dev
      • sudo aptitude install libfreetype6-dev
      • sudo aptitude install libfont-freetype-perl
      • Probably the 1st and 3rd only are required
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Imager-File-GIF - Read and write GIF files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Imager-File-HEIF - Read and write HEIF files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Imager-File-QOI - Read and write QOI image files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Imager-File-TIFF - Read and write TIFF files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Imager::Font::FT2 - Font support using FreeType 2.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Imager::QRCode - Generate QR Code with Imager using libqrencode.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Matplotlib-Simple - Simple plotting.
      • Note: POD is useless
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PGPLOT - Allow subroutines in the PGPLOT graphics library to be called from Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PGPLOTx-Constants - Constants for use with PGPLOT.
      • See also Types::PGPLOT below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PGPLOT-Device - Autogenerate PGPLOT device names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Prima - A Perl graphic toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Prima::Cairo - Prima extension for Cairo drawing.
      • Math::Image::CalcResized - Calculate dimensions of image/video resized by ImageMagick-like geometry specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Snig - (Simple|Static|Small|S..) Image Gallery.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SVG - Perl extension for generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SVG::Barcode - Base class for SVG 1D and 2D codes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SVG-Barcode-UPCA - Generator for SVG based UPCA barcodes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SVG-Timeline - Create SVG timeline charts.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SVG-TrafficLight - Perl extension to produce SVG diagrams of traffic lights.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::PGPLOT - Type::Tiny compatible types for the PGPLOT library.
      • See also PGPLOTx::Constants above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WebGPU::Direct - Direct access to the WebGPU native APIs.
    • ChemistryStuff
      • http://opensmiles.org/opensmiles.html - http://www.perlmol.org/.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::Domain - Class for domains in macromolecules.
      • Chemistry::File - Molecule file I/O base class - Molecule file I/O base class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chemistry-File-InChI - InChI identifier reader.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::File::SMARTS - SMARTS chemical substructure pattern linear notation parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::File::SMILES - SMILES linear notation parser/writer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::MacroMol - Perl module for macromolecules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::Mol - Molecule object toolkit.
      • Contains Chemistry::Atom, Chemistry::Bond, Chemistry::Tutorial
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chemistry-OpenSMILES - OpenSMILES format reader and writer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::Pattern - Chemical substructure pattern matching.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::Reaction - Explicit chemical reactions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chemistry::Ring - Represent a ring as a substructure of a molecule.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chemistry-Mol/view/Tutorial.pod - PerlMol Quick Tutorial.

      • [[POSIX]]
      • Proc::ProcessTable::Colorizer
      • Proc::Spawn
      • Spawn::Safe
      • System::Command
      • - .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Ghost - Yet another view to daemon processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ProcUtils - Command line utilities related to processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Command::Run - Execute external command or code reference.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Command-Runner - Run external commands and Perl code refs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CTK - CTK ToolKit library (CTKlib).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CTK::Daemon - Abstract class to implement Daemons.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Daemon::Control - Create init scripts in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Daemon::Daemonize - An easy-to-use daemon(izing) toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Daemon::Device - Forking daemon device construct.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Daemon::Generic - Framework to provide start/stop/reload for a daemon.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Daemon - A simple http server class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Forks::Super - Extensions and convenience methods to manage background processes.
      • Provides new definitions for the Perl functions fork, wait, and waitpid with richer functionality
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Pipely - Portably create pipe() or pipe-like handles, one way or another.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Cmd - Finding and running system commands made easy.
      • system() and background procs w/ piping, redirs, ptys (Unix, Win32)
      • Can use IPC::Run
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-LockTicket - Use Storable library to IPC token to prevent parallel access to any resources. Including your custom data to transfer asynchronously.
      • But see Storable::Improved
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-MicroSocket - Minimal request/response or pub/sub mechanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Run - system() and background procs w/ piping, redirs, ptys (Unix, Win32).
      • IPC::Run is a much more complete solution for running child processes (than the next)
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Run3 - Run a subprocess with input/output redirection.
      • POD contains important comparison with various similar modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Shareable - Use shared memory backed variables across processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::Daemonize - Role for daemonizing your Moose based application.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Daemon - Perl extension for portable daemons.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Application - Daemon class based on Proc::Application.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Background - Much simpler than the above.
      • Supports Linux and MS Windows
      • Refers to the previous module
      • Refers to the Win32 module below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Daemon - Run Perl program(s) as a daemon process.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Proc::Fork - Simple, intuitive interface to the fork() system call.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Proc::Forking - Provides a set of tool to fork and daemonize.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Forkmap - Map with forking.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Govern - Run child process and govern its various aspects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Proc::ProcessTable - Perl extension to access the unix process table.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Proc::Simple - Launch and control background processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Cmd - Run a system command or spawn a system processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Win32-Console-DotNet - Win32::Console .NET interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Win32::IPC - Base class for Win32 synchronization objects.
      • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/archive/blogs/twistylittlepassagesallalike/everyone-quotes-command-line-arguments-the-wrong-way - Everyone quotes command line arguments the wrong way.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Workflow::Inotify - Script to daemonize a Linux::Inotify2 handler.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Zleep - Fork a new process, sleep for the number of ms and then execute the codeblock.
    • ClipBoard
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ClipboardUtils - CLI utilities related to clipboard.
      • It iincludes 5 command line utilities for clipboard manipulation - Copy and paste with any OS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Clipboard
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Clipboard-Any - Common interface to clipboard manager functions.
    • CodingStyle
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eshu - HTMLHandling - For HTML::Valid.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CriticDB - Manage a database of Perl::Critic violations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Code::TidyAll - Engine for tidyall, your all-in-one code tidier and validator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Eshu - Indentation fixer for C, Perl, XS, XML, HTML, CSS, JavaScript and POD source files.
      • use Test2 - use standard.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/standard - A Perl parser. Provided 'use standard'.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/mb - Can easy script in Big5, Big5-HKSCS, GBK, Sjis(also CP932), UHC, UTF-8, ....
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic - Critique Perl source code for best-practices.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Community - Community-inspired Perl::Critic policies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-Grape - Additional Perl::Critic policies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-PJCJ - Perl::Critic policies for code style consistency.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-PolicyBundle-SNEZ - Extra Perl::Critic policies.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::DiscouragedModules - Various modules discouraged from use.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Policy::Community::PreferredAlternatives - Various modules with preferred alternatives.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::Policy::ControlStructures::ProhibitReturnInDoBlock - Do not "return" in "do" block.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-Policy-ControlStructures-ProhibitReturnInMappingBlock - Do not "return" in mapping blocks (map, grep, sort).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-Policy-Security-RandBytesFromHash - Flag common anti-patterns for generating random bytes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-Policy-Variables-ProhibitLoopOnHash - Don't write loops on hashes, only on keys and values of hashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Critic-Pulpo Type::Tiny - Some add-on perlcritic policies.
      • Contains Types::Standard
      • See DataTypes
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Critic::StricterSubs - Perl::Critic plugin for stricter subroutine checks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Tidy - Parses and beautifies perl source.
    • Color
      • CommandLineStuff - ChartingAndPlotting.
      • [[Graphs]]
      • PdfStuff
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X11_color_names
      • https://imagemagick.org/script/color.php
      • https://no-color.org/ - For env var NO_COLOR
      • https://www.pantone-colours.com/ - Pantone Colors
      • https://www.w3.org/TR/css-color-3/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::AutoColor - Automatic color names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ANSI-Palette - ANSI Color palettes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ANSIColorUtils - Utilities related to ANSI color.
      • https://imagemagick.org/script/color.php - ImageMagick Color Names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Color::ANSI::Util - Routines for dealing with ANSI colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Color::Library - An easy-to-use and comprehensive named-color library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Color::Rgb - Simple rgb.txt parsing class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Color-RGB-Util - Utilities related to RGB colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Color-Spectrum - Just another HTML color generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Colouring::In - Color or colour.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Colouring::In::XS - Color or colour.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Displaycolour - Work with display colours.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames - Defines RGB values for common color names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::CSS - CSS color names and equivalent RGB values.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::GrayScale - RGB values of shades of gray and base colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::HTML - HTML color names and equivalent RGB values.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::Mozilla - Mozilla, Netscape 6.0, Galeon (anything using Gecko) color names and equivalent RGB values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-ColorNames-Pantone - RGB values of Pantone colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::SVG - SVG color names and equivalent RGB values.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorNames::WWW - WWW color names and equivalent RGB values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-ColorNamesLite-All - Lots of color names and equivalent RGB values (lite version).
      • There is also Graphics::ColorNamesLite::All
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-ColorNamesLite-EN
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorObject - Convert between color spaces.
      • Part of https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSIColor-Concise
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-Colourset - Create sets of colours.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::ColorUtils - Easy-to-use color space conversions and more.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graphics-Toolkit-Color - Color palette creation helper.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSI-Sprintf - Sprintf with ANSI colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::ANSIColor - Color screen output using ANSI escape sequences. Built-in.
      • It only acts on the terminal formatting escape sequences that end in "m" and leaves all other ones alone
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSIColor-Concise - Produce ANSI terminal sequence by concise notation .
      • Contains Graphics::ColorObject
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSIColor-Gradients - Curated ANSI 256-color palette library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::Chrome - DSL for colors and other terminal chrome.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::ExtendedColor - Color screen output using 256 colors.
    • CommandLineStuff
      • DataTraversal - For Term::TablePrint - ChildProcesses.
      • DataTypes - For Rope
      • ModuleBuilding
      • ParallelProcessing
      • PoeStuff
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting - For string handling
      • WindowManagement
      • XWindowsStuff
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X11_color_names
      • https://no-color.org/ - For env var NO_COLOR
      • https://turso.tech/ - For Turso DB
      • libtickit
      • The Terminal Interface Construction Kit
      • A high-level C library used for building interactive, full-screen terminal programs (TUIs)
      • It is designed to be a more modern and flexible alternative to the classic ncurses library
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion-Emitter - Event dispatcher.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion-Run - Role for console commands.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libtickit - Alien wrapping for libtickit.
      • This module behaves like ExtUtils::PkgConfig
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::Turso::CLI - Install and find Turso CLI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ansicolumn - ANSI sequence aware column command.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ansifold - Fold command handling ANSI terminal sequences.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-BashHistoryUtils - CLI utilities related to bash history file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CaseUtils - CLI utilities related to case (uppercase/lowercase).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Cmd - Write command line apps with less suffering.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cat-v - cat-v command implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-chot - Command Heuristic Omni-Tracer.
      • But see App::PMUtils
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-lms - A utility to locate and display command or library files.
      • But see App::PMUtils
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-nup - N-up output wrapper for optex -Mup.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-oo_modulino_zsh_completion_helper - Provides zsh completion for OO-Modulinos.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-optex - General purpose command option wrapper.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-optex-up - Optex module for multi-column paged output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-optex-rpn - Optex module for Reverse Polish Notation calculation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-optex-xform - Data transform filter module for optex.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::papersway - PaperWM-like scrollable tiling window management for Sway/i3wm.
      • https://github.com/paperwm/paperwm
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PickArgs - Pick one or more items from command-line arguments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::PMUtils - Command-line utilities related to Perl modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ptimeout - Module implementing ptimeout.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-SpeedTest - Command-line interface to speedtest.net.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ARGV-OrDATA - Let the diamond operator read from DATA if there's no ARGV.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B - Returns a double-quote-surrounded escaped version of STR which can be used as a string in C source code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B - Returns a double-quote-surrounded escaped version of STR which can be used as a string in Perl source code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/BATsh - Bilingual Shell: cmd.exe and bash in one script (self-contained).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chandra - Perl bindings to webview-c for creating cross-platform GUIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Check-Term-Color - Check terminal color functionality.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CLI-Cmdline - Minimal command-line parser with short/long options and aliases in pure Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CLI::Framework - Build standardized, flexible, testable command-line applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CLI-Helpers - Subroutines for making simple command line scripts.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CLI-Simple - A minimalist object oriented base class for CLI applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/cpanm-shcompgen - Install modules from CPAN (and activate shell completion).
      • See shcompgen below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Curses - Terminal screen handling and optimization.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Curses::UI - A curses based OO user interface framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Curses-UI-POE - A subclass makes Curses::UI POE Friendly.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Expect - Automate interactions with command line programs that expose a text terminal interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-App - Write and test your script with ease.
      • Helps you structure your scripts and integrates Getopt::Long with a very simple API
      • Makes it very easy to test your script, since the script file can be sourced without actually being run
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::ArgvFile - Interpolates script options from files into @ARGV or another array.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-Class - Extended dictionary version of Getopt::Long.
      • Fancy!
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::EX - Getopt Extender.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::EX::Colormap - ANSI terminal color and option support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::EX::Config - Getopt::EX module configuration interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-EX-Hashed - Hash object automation for Getopt::Long.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-EX-i18n - General i18n module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::EX::LabeledParam - Labeled parameter handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::EX::termcolor - Getopt::EX termcolor module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-Guided - getopts implementation that follows POSIX utility guidelines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::Lazier - Lazy Getopt-like command-line options and argument parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::Long - Extended processing of command line options.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-Long-Bash - Bash option parsing that does what you mean.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::Long::More - Like Getopt::Long, but with more stuffs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::Std - Process single-character switches with switch clustering. Built-in.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-Type-Tiny - Clean Getopt::Long wrapper with Type::Tiny support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::Yath - Option processing yath style.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Cmd - Giving an easy Moo style way to make command organized CLI apps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Options - Explicit Options eXtension for Object Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OptArgs2 - Command-line argument and option processor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlVision - Text-mode User Interface Widgets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Progress-Any - Record progress to any output.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Progress::Any::Output::TermProgressBarColor - Output progress to terminal as color bar.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Progress-Any-SIGHandler - Output progress to terminal as simple message.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Ragnetto-Console - ANSI terminal control utility.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Reply - Read, eval, print, loop, yay!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rope-Cmd - Command Line Applications via Rope.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/shcompgen - Generate shell completion scripts.
      • See cpanm-shcompgen above
      • https://metacpan.org/release/SBECK/Shell-Cmd-3.05 - Run shell commands with enhanced support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Shell::Perl - A read-eval-print loop in Perl.
      • See also Devel::REPL
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::ShellQuote - Quote strings for passing through the shell.
      • Does not decode ANSI escape codes
      • Quite a lot of ANSI-compliant devices *also* support the 8-bit Fe/C1 versions of escape sequences (e.g. \e[ can be abbreviated to \x9b)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-Choose - Choose items from a list interactively..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::Choose::LineFold - Control the number of occupied columns of a character string on a terminal with a monospaced font.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-Choose-LineFold-XS - XS acceleration for Term-Choose-LineFold.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-Choose-Util - TUI-related functions for selecting directories, files, numbers and subsets of lists..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-Form - Read lines from STDIN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ProgressSpinner - Terminal Progress bars!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TermReadKey - A perl module for simple terminal control.
      • Contains GetTerminalSize
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ReadLine - Perl interface to various readline packages. If no real package is found, substitutes stubs instead of basic functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::ReadLine::Gnu - Perl extension for the GNU Readline/History Library.
