      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ReadLine-Repl - A batteries included interactive Term::ReadLine REPL module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::UI - Term::ReadLine UI made easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tickit - Terminal Interface Construction KIT.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TUI-Vision - Perl TUI Framework (Turbo Vision 2.0 Port).
      • TUI::Vision is the successor namespace to the TV:: (TVision?) module family
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TVision - Perl glue to the TurboVision library.
      • A modern port of Turbo Vision 2.0, the classical framework for text-based user interfaces. Now cross-platform and with Unicode support
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Win32API-Console - Win32 native Console API.
    • ConfigFiles
      • EditorConfigFiles - DataTraversal.
      • [[SSH]]
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • http://standards.freedesktop.org/desktop-entry-spec/desktop-entry-spec-latest.html - For .Desktop files
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Advanced::Config - Perl module reads configuation files from various sources.
      • Advanced::Config is an enhanced implementation of a config file manager that allows you to manage almost any config file as a true object with a common interface
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Cme - Configuration data checker or editor based on Config::Model.
      • Contains the cms command
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Env - Manage application specific environments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AppConfig - Perl5 module for reading configuration files and parsing command line arguments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Clearcase - Object oriented interface to Clearcase.
      • IBM DevOps Code ClearCase (also known as IBM Rational ClearCase) supports software configuration management (SCM)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Colon-Config - XS helper to read a configuration file using ':' as separator.
      • App::Options - Combine command line options, environment vars, and option file values (for program configuration). Also discusses a range of other config-type modules - Hint: Start with Config::IOD::Reader below.
      • Config::ROFL - Yet another config module. Uses Config::ZOMG
      • Config::ZOMG - Yet another layer over Config::Any
      • Config::Any - Load configuration from different file formats, transparently
      • Config::Dot
      • ConfigReader::Simple - https://metacpan.org/pod/ConfigReader::Simple
      • Config::Structured - Provides generalized and structured configuration value access
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Structured
      • Config::Simple::Conf
      • Config::Parser
      • Config::INI::Tiny
      • Config::INI::RefVars - INI file reader, allows the referencing of INI and environment variables within the INI file
      • Config::Param - All you want to do with parameters for your program (or someone else's)
      • Config::Structured - Provides generalized and structured configuration value access
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Structured
      • Parse::PlainConfig - Provides hierarchical config file structures
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::PlainConfig
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Conf-Libconfig - Perl extension for libconfig.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Abstraction - Configuration Abstraction Layer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Any - Load configuration from different file formats, transparently.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-App - Cascading merged application configuration.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Auto - Magical config file parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-General - Generic Config Module.
      • Has many features
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::IniFiles - A module for reading .ini-style configuration files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::INI::Reader - A subclassable .ini-file parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-INI-Reader-Multiline - Parser for .ini files with line continuations.
      • This module is a round-trip parser for IOD configuration format - Read and write IOD/INI configuration files.
      • If you only need to read IOD configuration files, you might want to use Config::IOD::Reader instead
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::IOD
      • See IOD below
      • It is a minimalist alternative to the more fully-featured Config::IOD - Read IOD/INI configuration files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::IOD::Reader
      • See IOD below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model - A framework to validate, migrate and edit configuration files.
      • I hope you never need code this complex
      • See also Config::Model::Tester
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Model::CursesUI - Curses interface to edit config data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model-Itself - Model (or schema) editor for Config::Model.
      • A graphical config editor for /etc/ssh/sshd_config, /etc/ssh/ssh_config and ~/.ssh/config - https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model-OpenSsh.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model-Systemd - Editor and validator for systemd configuration files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model-TkUI - Tk GUI to edit config data through Config::Model.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-MorePerl - Convenient and flexible config loader in perl format.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Resolver - Recursively resolve placeholders in a data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Simple - Simple configuration file class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Tiny - Read/Write .ini style files with as little code as possible.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Tiny::Ordered - Read/Write ordered .ini style files with as little code as possible.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Writer - A module to write configuration files in an easy and safe way.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Env-Assert - Ensure that the environment variables match what you need, or abort.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Env-AsYaml - Is intended to be a tool for examination of the environment.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Env::Dot - Read environment variables from a .env file.
      • Parse prefixed environment variables and dotenv (.env) files into Perl - https://metacpan.org/pod/ENV::Util.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-DesktopEntry - Object to handle .desktop files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IOD - IOD (INI On Drugs) file format specification.
      • See Config::IOD and Config::IOD::Reader above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perinci::CmdLine::Util::Config - Utility routines related to config files.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TOML - Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language is a file format for configuration files.
      • Supports the following data types: string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, and table
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TOML::Tiny - A minimal, pure perl TOML parser and serializer.
    • ConstantStuff
      • EnumStuff - DataTypes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Common::CodingTools - Common constants and functions for programmers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Const::Exporter - Declare constants for export.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Constant::Export::Lazy - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module by defining functions that are only called as needed. The interface is rather complex.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Constant::Exporter - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module, although you can specify tags.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Const-Fast - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, and hashes.
      • But see Const::XS for speed tests
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Const::Fast::Exporter - .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Const-XS - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, hashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exporter::Constants - This module only allows you to declare function symbol constants, akin to the constant module, without tags.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::SetOnce - Write-once, read-many attributes for Moose.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::Types::Ro - Moose type constraints for read-only containers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooX-Const - Syntactic sugar for constant and write-once Moo(se) attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Readonly - Facility for creating read-only scalars, arrays, hashes.
      • But see Const::XS for speed tests
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Readonly::Tiny - Simple, correct readonly values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Readonly-Values-Boolean - Boolean Constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Readonly-Values-Days - Days Constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Readonly-Values-Months - Months Constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Trigger::Lock - A coderef for use in Moose triggers that will lock hashrefs and arrayrefs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Const - Types that coerce references to read-only.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::ReadOnly - Type constraints and coercions for read-only data structures and locked hashes.
    • CPAN
      • ParsingStuff - CpanFile.
      • PerlDocumentation
      • PerlInternals
      • UsernamePassword
      • http://cpantesters.org/ - For the CPAN Testers wiki
      • https://docs.docker.com/get-started/get-docker/ - For Docker
      • https://docs.docker.com/compose/install - For Docker Compose
      • https://fastapi.metacpan.org
      • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-api
      • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-docker
      • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-web
      • https://keepachangelog.com/
      • https://metacpan.org
      • https://perlmaven.com/pinto-tutorial
      • https://pinguinorodriguez.cl/blog/bundling-vendored-modules/
      • http://fast-matrix.cpantesters.org/?dist=libwww-perl - Pick a distro, any disto. Try DBix-Tree, or....
      • https://cpanscan.com/ - Nice!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CPAN-Dependents - Recursively find all reverse dependencies for a distribution or module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::CPAN::Get - Tool to fetch module from CPAN archive.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::CPAN::Mini::Visit - Explore each distribution in a minicpan repository.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CPAN-Search - Base class for cpan-search script.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CpanDak - Cpanm, with some sort of distroprefs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cpangitify - Convert cpan distribution from BackPAN to a git repository.
      • See also Git::CPAN::Patch
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CPANIDX - Queryable web-based CPAN Index.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::CPANAuthors - We are CPAN authors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-CPANAuthors-InMemoriam - Remembering our fallen heroes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::CPANAuthors::Not - We are not CPAN authors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cpm - A fast CPAN module installer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cpx - Install and execute CPAN package binaries.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/lcpan - Manage your local CPAN mirror.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-makefilepl2cpanfile - Convert Makefile.PL to a cpanfile automatically.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-pause - A CLI for PAUSE.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::PAUSE::CheckPerms - Check if PAUSE permissions are consistent for all modules in a dist.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PAUSE-Comaint - Make someone co-maint of your module on PAUSE/CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sourcepan - Modulino implementation of soucepan.
      • Contains sourcepan, to fetch source archives from CPAN
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Changes - Changes file management.
      • Subclasses: Changes::Change, Changes::Group, Changes::NewLine, Changes::Release, Changes::Version
      • Uses GnuPG as gpg - Load (and optionally decrypt via GnuPG) user/pass identity information.
      • Use with gpg-agent
      • For GitHub API access, an identity is a login/token pair
      • For PAUSE access, an identity is a user/password pair
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN - Query, download and build perl modules from CPAN sites.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Changes - Parser for CPAN style change logs.
      • Contains CPAN::Changes::Spec
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Changes::Parser - Parse a CPAN Changelog file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Changes-Parser-KeepAChangeLog - Parser for Keep a Changelog formatted files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Common::Index - Common library for searching CPAN modules, authors and distributions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::DistnameInfo - Extract distribution name and version from a distribution filename.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Distroprefs - Read and match distroprefs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Faker - Build a bogus CPAN instance for testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Index::API::File::Whois - Interface to 00whois.xml.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-IndexPod - Index the POD from an unpacked CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Info-FromURL - Extract/guess information from a URL.
      • Contains CPAN::Author::FromURL, CPAN::Dist::FromURL, CPAN::Module::FromURL, CPAN::Release::FromURL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-InGit - Manage custom CPAN trees to pin versions for your projects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Maker - Create a CPAN distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Maker-Bootstrapper - Scaffold a new CPAN distribution in one command.
      • Contains CPAN::Maker::ConfigReader etc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Meta-X - Catalog of custom (x_*) keys in CPAN distribution metadata being used in the wild.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-MetaCurator - Manage the cpan.metacurator.sqlite database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-MetaPackager - Manage the cpan.metapackager.sqlite database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Mini - Create a minimal mirror of CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Mini::FromList - Create a minimal CPAN mirror from a list of modules you specify.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Mini-Inject - Inject modules into a CPAN::Mini mirror..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Mirror-Tiny - Create partial CPAN mirror (a.k.a. DarkPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Nearest - Find the nearest module to a given name.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::PackageDetails - Create or read 02packages.details.txt.gz.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::ParseDistribution - Index a file from the BackPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Perl-Releases - Mapping Perl releases on CPAN to the location of the tarballs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Perl-Releases-MetaCPAN - Mapping Perl releases on CPAN to the location of the tarballs via MetaCPAN API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Plugin-Sysdeps - A CPAN.pm plugin for installing system dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Reporter - Adds CPAN Testers reporting to CPAN.pm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Repository - API to access a directory which can be served as CPAN repository.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Repository::Packages - Parse 02packages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Repository::Perms - Parse 06perms.
      • See Parse::CPAN::Perms below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Requirements-Dynamic - Dynamic prerequisites in meta files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-RPM - A Perl module packager.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Search-Lite - Setup and maintain a searchable CPAN database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Search-Tester - Interface to search CPAN module tester.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Source - CPAN source list data aggregator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-SQLite - Maintain and search a minimal CPAN database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Tarball-Patch - Patch a CPAN tarball, via CPAN::Distroprefs mechanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Testers-Schema - Schema for CPANTesters database processed from test reports.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Testers-ParseReport - Parse reports to www.cpantesters.org from various sources.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::UnsupportedFinder - Identify unsupported or poorly maintained CPAN modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Uploader - Upload things to the CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-02Packages-Search - Search packages in 02packages.details.txt.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPANPLUS - API & CLI access to the CPAN mirrors.
      • Has many sub-modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPANPLUS::Backend - Programmer's interface to CPANPLUS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPANPLUS::Config - Configuration defaults and heuristics for CPANPLUS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPANPLUS::Configure - Configuration for CPANPLUS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPANPLUS-Dist-Debora - Create Debian or RPM packages from Perl modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPANPLUS::Module - CPAN module objects for CPANPLUS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DarkPAN-Utils - A set of utilities for working with a DarkPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Git::CPAN::Patch - Patch CPAN modules using Git.
      • See also App::cpangitify
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Menlo - A CPAN client.
      • https://github.com/metacpan/metacpan-docker - Running the MetaCPAN stack with Docker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MetaCPAN-API - A comprehensive, DWIM-featured API to MetaCPAN - Deprecated.
      • See MetaCPAN::Client
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MetaCPAN-Client - A comprehensive, DWIM-featured client to the MetaCPAN API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MetaCPAN-Pod-HTML - Format Pod as HTML for MetaCPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MetaCPAN-Role - A collection of roles useful to MetaCPAN project.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Metadata - Gather package and POD information from perl module file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Metadata::Changes - Manage machine-readable Changes/CHANGES/Changelog.ini files.
      • Contains Ron's simple home-grown change file manager called ini.report.pl
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-API-CPAN - A client to issue queries to the MetaCPAN REST API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OrePAN2 - Yet another DarkPAN manager.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OrePAN2::S3 - This script is used to add distributions to your own DarkPAN repository housed on Amazon's S3 storage.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OrePAN2::Server - DarkPAN Server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Package::DeprecationManager - Manage deprecation warnings for your distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Authors - Parse 01mailrc.txt.gz.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Meta - Parse META.yml and META.json CPAN metadata files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Packages - Parse 02packages.details.txt.gz.
      • But see https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Packages
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Perms - Parse 06perms.txt.gz.
      • See CPAN::Repository::Perms above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Packages::Fast - Parse CPAN's package index.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CPAN::Whois - Parse CPAN's authors/00whois.xml file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAUSE::Packages - Interface to PAUSE's packages file (02packages.details.txt).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAUSE::Permissions - Interface to PAUSE's module permissions file (06perms.txt).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAUSE-Users - Interface to PAUSE's users file (00whois.xml).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Release-Checklist - A QA checklist for CPAN releases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WorePAN - Creates a partial CPAN mirror for tests.
    • CpanFile
      • LicenceStuff - [[CPAN]].
      • SecurityPolicy
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Audit - Audit CPAN distributions for known vulnerabilities.
      • This module contains cpan-audit, which uses a cpanfile or a cpanfile.snapshot file
    • cron
      • WebServices - [[Acronyms]] - For XPDL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/BPM::Engine - Business Process Execution Engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/BPM::XPDL - XPDL access.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Chronic - A constraints-based, opportunistic, application-level scheduler.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cron-Toolkit - Quartz-compatible cron parser with unique extensions and over 400 tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Schedule-Activity - Generate random activity schedules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Schedule::Cron - cron-like scheduler for Perl subroutines. Use it to create a daemon.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/BBLACKM/Perl-Schedule-Easing-0.1.0 - Scheduled ramp-up and linear activation of events.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Schedule::LongSteps - Manage long term processes over arbitrary large spans of time.
    • CryptoStuff
      • IoLayers - [[Acronyms]] - For UUID.
      • [[UUID]]
      • KubernetesStuff
      • Mojolicious::Plugin::Credentials
      • TheRFCGuide
      • [[XML]] - For XML::Sig::OO
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/leon_timmermans/2024/02/using-peppers-with-cryptpassphrase.html
      • https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/core/choosing-prng-algorithm.html - For choosing a PRNG
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EdDSA#Ed25519
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RSA_cryptosystem
      • https://github.com/alvoskov/SmokeRand - For PRNG testing
      • https://letsencrypt.org/
      • https://openquantumsafe.org/
      • https://security.metacpan.org/docs/guides/random-data-for-security.html
      • https://www.nntp.perl.org/group/perl.perl5.porters/2024/11/msg269033.html
      • https://www.pcg-random.org/ - For an overview of the PCG family
      • https://www.vaultproject.io/
      • https://zephyrtronium.github.io/articles/randomness.html - For choosing a PRNG
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libsecp256k1 - Perl builder for libsecp256k1.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alt::Digest::MD5::OpenSSL - Alternative Digest::MD5 based on OpenSSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-bcrypt - A command-line tool to deal with bcrypt password hashing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Bitcoin-PaperWallet - Generate printable cold storage of bitcoins.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::xsum - Compute and check file checksums/digests (using various algorithms).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::Passphrase - Hashed passwords/passphrases as objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Authen-Passphrase-Argon2 - Store and check password using Argon2.
      • This is an attempt to make Crypt::Argon2 compliant with Authen::Passphrase
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bitcoin-Crypto - Bitcoin cryptography in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bitcoin-Secp256k1 - Perl interface to libsecp256k1.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bytes-Random-Secure - Perl extension to generate cryptographically-secure random bytes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bytes-Random-Secure-Tiny - A tiny Perl extension to generate cryptographically-secure random bytes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Circle-Chain - The Circle::Chain SDks.
      • See also Circle::User, Circle::Wallet, Circle::Block, Circle::Node modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Circle::Miner - The miner module for circle chain sdk..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Age - Perl implementation of age encryption (age-encryption.org).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Argon2 - Perl interface to the Argon2 key derivation functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Bear - BearSSL for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::CBC - Encrypt Data with Cipher Block Chaining Mode.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Credentials - Manage credential files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Curve25519 - Generate shared secret using elliptic-curve Diffie-Hellman function.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::DES - Perl DES encryption module.
      • Warning: Do note that DES only uses 8 byte keys and only works on 8 byte data blocks.
      • If you're intending to encrypt larger blocks or entire files, please use Crypt::CBC in conjunction with this module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-DES_EDE3 - Triple-DES EDE encryption/decryption.
      • This is a wrapper around Crypt::DES
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Diceware - Random passphrase generator loosely based on the Diceware algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::DSA - DSA Signatures and Key Generation - Deprecated.
      • Crypt::DSA is an implementation of the DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) signature verification system
      • The heavy-duty mathematics underneath are provided by the Math::Pari library
      • DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) signatures are no longer considered to be adequate for security
      • Possible replacement is Crypt::DSA::GMP
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-DSA-GMP - DSA Signatures and Key Generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Fernet - Perl extension for Fernet (symmetric encryption).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-HSM - A PKCS11 interface for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::JWT - JSON Web Token (JWT, JWS, JWE) as defined by RFC7519, RFC7515, RFC7516.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Komihash - Komihash implementation in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::LE - Let's Encrypt (and other ACME-based) API interfacing module and client..
      • See also Net::ACME2
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Liboqs-Sign - Post-Quantum Digital Signatures via liboqs.
      • This module is a drop-in replacement for Crypt::PQClean::Sign
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-MatrixSSL3 - Perl extension for SSL and TLS using MatrixSSL.org v3.9.3.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Misc - Miscellaneous functions related to (or used by) CryptX.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-NaCl-Sodium - NaCl compatible modern, easy-to-use library for encryption, decryption, signatures, password hashing and more.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-NaCl-Tweet - XS bindings for TweetNaCl.
      • A Perl wrapper around OpenSSL's AES library - https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::OpenSSL::AES.
      • See also Crypt::CBC
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-EC - Perl extension for OpenSSL EC (Elliptic Curves) library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-PBKDF2 - Wrapper for PBKDF2 keys derivation function of the OpenSSL library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-PKCS10 - Perl extension to OpenSSL's PKCS10 API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-PKCS12 - Perl extension to OpenSSL's PKCS12 API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::OpenSSL::Random - OpenSSL/LibreSSL pseudo-random number generator access.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-RSA - RSA encoding and decoding, using the openSSL libraries.
      • Prefer Crypt::PK::RSA. See Net::SAML2
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL-BaseFunc - Base Functions, using the OpenSSL libraries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenSSL3 - A modern OpenSSL wrapper.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-OpenToken - Perl implementation of Ping Identity's "OpenToken".
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase - A module for managing passwords in a crypto-graphically agile manner.
      • Contains Crypt::Passphrase::Pepper::Simple
      • But see Crypt::Passphrase::Bcrypt::AES
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Argon2 - An Argon2 encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • It is the recommended password encoder as of 2023
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Argon2::HSM - HSM encrypted Argon2 hashes for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Bcrypt::AES - A peppered AES-encrypted Bcrypt encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • But see Crypt::Passphrase::Bcrypt::AES
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::HSM - A hasher using hardware for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Linux - An Linux crypt encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • Recommended if you need to be compatible with standard Linux system passwords
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Passphrase-PBKDF2 - A PBKDF2 encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • Only recommended when FIPS-compliance is required
      • This is the only portable pure-perl backend
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Passphrase-SaltedHash - Validate against Crypt::SaltedHash hashes with Crypt::Passphrase .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Scrypt - A scrypt encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • If one wants a memory-hard password scheme Argon2 or Yescrypt is recommended instead
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::System - An system crypt() encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • Note that the supported algorithms depend entirely on your platform
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Passphrase::Yescrypt - A yescrypt encoder for Crypt::Passphrase.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PasswdMD5 - Provide interoperable MD5-based crypt() functions.
      • Crypt::PasswdMD5 in the random_md5_salt function uses the built-in rand function to generate a salt
      • The built-in rand function is not appropriate for cryptographic use, as it is predictable and seeded by only 32 bits
      • Use Crypt::SysRandom or Crypt::URandom to generate random bytes instead of calling rand
      • Or use Crypt::URandom::Token to generate the salt directly without having to convert the bytes into base-64 characters
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PBKDF2 - The PBKDF2 password hashing algorithm..
      • See PBKDF2::Tiny below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Perl - Cryptography in pure Perl.
      • Can create and sign in X509 in pure Perl. Slow without the XS addons
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PK::Ed25519 - Digital signature based on Ed25519.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PK::RSA - Crypt::PK::RSA - Public key cryptography based on RSA.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-PostgreSQL - Module for generating encrypted password for PostgreSQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PQClean::Sign - Post-Quantum Cryptography with keypair.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::PRNG - Cryptographically secure random number generator.
      • Part of CryptX
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Protocol - No POD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Random-Source - Get weak or strong random data from pluggable sources.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Rhash - Compute message digests and magnet links.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-RIPEMD160 - Perl extension for the RIPEMD-160 Hash function.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-RSA-Blind - Blind RSA signatures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-SaltedHash - Perl interface to functions that assist in working with salted hashes - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-ScryptKDF - Scrypt password based key derivation function.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::SecretBuffer - Prevent accidentally leaking a string of sensitive data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-SMIME - S/MIME message signing, verification, encryption and decryption.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Sodium-XS - Perl XS bindings for libsodium.
      • https://doc.libsodium.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-SysRandom - Perl interface to system randomness.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-SysRandom-XS - Perl interface to system randomness, XS version.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-TimestampedData - Read and write TimeStampedData files.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc5544
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::URandom - Provide non blocking randomness.
      • This is a pure-Perl module that reads data from /dev/urandom. It also uses Win32::API to read random bytes on Windows
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-URandom-MonkeyPatch - Override core rand function to use system random sources - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::URandom::Token - Generate secure strings for passwords, secrets and similar.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Crypt::Util - A lightweight Crypt/Digest convenience API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-xxHash - xxHash implementation for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crypt-Yescrypt - A Perl interface to the yescrypt password hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CryptX - This is a huge crypto toolkit.
      • Crypt::PRNG::ChaCha20 - Cryptographically secure PRNG based on ChaCha20 (stream cipher) algorithm. Used in UUID
      • Random numbers: CryptX::PrNG::Fortuna
      • App::xsum
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Dancer2::Plugin::CryptPassphrase - Use Crypt::Passphrase with Dancer2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Entropy - Entropy (randomness) management - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::CryptColumn - Automatically hash password/passphrase columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::DigestColumns - Automatic digest columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::EncodedColumn - Automatically encode columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::InflateColumn::Authen::Passphrase - Inflate/deflate columns to Authen::Passphrase instances.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::PassphraseColumn - Automatically hash password/passphrase columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest - Modules that calculate message digests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::BLAKE2 - Message digest algorithm (which is an excellent security improvement: Bruce Scheiner).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::CRC - Generic CRC functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Digest-HMAC - Keyed-Hashing for Message Authentication.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::MD5 - Perl interface to the MD5 Algorithm.
      • But see Alt::Digest::MD5::OpenSSL above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::Merkle::SHA256 - Pure-Perl SHA-256 Merkle Tree with Audit Proofs.
      • Merkle tree aka Hash Tree
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Digest-Perl-MD5 - Perl implementation of Ron Rivest's MD5 Algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Digest-prvhash64 - Variable length hashing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::SHA - Perl extension for SHA-1/224/256/384/512. Built-in.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::SHA::PurePerl - Perl implementation of SHA-1/224/256/384/512.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Digest-SHA3 - Perl extension for SHA-3.
      • Prefer this to Digest::SHA
      • It supports many types of digests
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Digest::xxHash - xxHash Implementation For Perl.
      • But see Crypto::xxHash above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Digest - Calculate digests of files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Filter-Crypto - Create runnable Perl files encrypted with OpenSSL libcrypto.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Filter::Crypto::Decrypt - Perl source code filter to run encrypted Perl files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-SOPS - Perl implementation of Mozilla SOPS encrypted file format.
      • See http://www.openssl.org - External link to Open SSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::ISAAC - Perl interface to the ISAAC PRNG algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::ISAAC::XS - C implementation of the ISAAC PRNG algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::MT - The Mersenne Twister PRNG.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::MT::Auto - Auto-seeded Mersenne Twister PRNGs.
      • https://github.com/gitpan/Math-Random-MTwist - The Mersenne Twist pseudorandom number generator.
      • https://rt.cpan.org/Ticket/Display.html?id=171333
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::PCG32 - Minimal PCG random number generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Random-Secure - Cryptographically-secure, cross-platform replacement for rand().
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Random::Xorshift - A fast PRNG.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Random-Xoshiro256 - XS wrapper for xoshiro256** PRNG.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Monero-Keys - Module to generate Monero cryptocurrency compatible keys. Monero pulic key generation algorithm varies from standard Ed25519.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::ACME2 - Client logic for the ACME (Let's Encrypt) protocol.
      • See also Crypt::LE
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Random - Get random data from online sources.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SSLeay - Perl bindings for OpenSSL and LibreSSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PBKDF2::Tiny - Minimalist PBKDF2 (RFC 2898) with HMAC-SHA1 or HMAC-SHA2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Random-Any - Try to use Data::Entropy::Algorithms::rand(), fallback on builtin rand().
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Random-Simple - Simple, usable, real world random numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rand-Urandom - Replaces rand() with /dev/urandom.
      • This is a pure-Perl module that makes syscalls to getrandom(2), but falls back to reading from /dev/urandom
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Solana-SPLAddress - Perl extension for creating deterministic Solana token addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::GetRandom - This is an XS module that calls getrandom(2) directly. It has a slightly different interface but is faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-GetRandom-PP - Pure Perl interface to getrandom(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Vigil-Crypt - Encryption and Hashing wrapper for ChaCha20-Poly1305 and Argon2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Vigil-Token - Cryptographically secure, URL-safe token generator for OTPs, sessions, and custom codes.
    • CssStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CSS::Minifier - Perl extension for minifying CSS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CSS::Minifier::XS - XS based CSS minifier.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CSS::SAC - SAC CSS parser.
    • DatabaseAndSQL
      • AlgorithmicStuff - [[Acronyms]] - For GDB & GQL re Neo4j.
      • CommandLineStuff - For Alien::Turso::CLI
      • ConfigFiles
      • CryptoStuff - For DBIx::Class::CryptColumn, DBIx::Class::DigestColumns, DBIx::Class::EncodedColumn, DBIx::Class::PassphraseColumn, etc
      • DataValidation
      • EventStuff - For EV::*
      • FileHandling
      • HashHandling - For Pivot::ArrayOfHashes
      • ModuleBuilding - For Alien::DuckDB
      • UsernamePassword
      • YamlStuff
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/usna78/2024/05/mariadb-10-and-sqltranslatorproducer.html
      • https://clickhouse.com/docs/interfaces/http
      • https://clickhouse.com/docs/interfaces/formats#native
      • https://duckdb.org/ - For DuckDB's feature-rich SQL dialect
      • https://man.he.net/man1/dig - For dig, similar to App::dnsq
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ORLite - For an important discussion of SQLite
      • https://redis.io/
      • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/dbix-class-async-evolution/ - For async techniques
      • https://turso.tech/ - For Turso DB
      • To run it you need to set three environment variables as below
      • dbi.schema.pl takes an option parameter of image type, which defaults to svg
      • Edit the code if you wish to change the output dir's name
      • The last line, the copy, is for my setup. $DR points to a RAM disk. Debian automatically creates a RAM disk, so I configure the free web server Engine X, aka nginx, to use it. See my other TiddlyWiki, Debian.Wiki.html, for details about nginx
      • And yes, you can use empty strings for the username and password
      • Lastly, $ is just the command line prompt
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-DuckDB - Find or build DuckDB.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::hiredis - Alien wrapper for hiredis.
      • https://github.com/redis/hiredis
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libpq - Find or build libpq PostgreSQL client library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-MariaDB - Find or build libmariadb client library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::sqlite_vec - Sqlite-vec SQLite extension for vector search.
      • https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::Redis - Non-blocking Redis client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-DBBrowser - Browse SQLite/MySQL/PostgreSQL databases and their tables interactively.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::dbinfo - Get/extract information from database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-dnsq - A full-featured dig-like DNS query tool.
      • Sqitch is a database change management application - Sensible database change management.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Sqitch
      • http://sqitch.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::SQLiteUtils - Utilities related to SQLite.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-dbitemplater - A utility for running a SQL query via DBI and using the output in a template.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Async::Redis - Async Redis client using Future::IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/BerkeleyDB - Perl extension for Berkeley DB version 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Catmandu::DBI - Catmandu tools to communicate with DBI based interfaces.
      • This is (presumably) unrelated to Catmandu in DataTraversal
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ClickHouse-Encoder - Fast XS encoder for ClickHouse Native format.
      • See also EV::ClickHouse
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Couch-DB - CouchDB database client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CXC-DB-DDL - DDL for table creation, based on SQL::Translator::Schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-ObjectDriver - Simple, transparent data interface, with caching.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Record::Serialize - Flexible serialization of a record.
      • A module which encodes data in various ways and can further transmit the result
      • This module is primarily useful for output of sets of uniformly structured data records. It provides a uniform, thin, interface to various serializers and output sinks. Its raison d'etre is its ability to manipulate the records prior to encoding and output
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-SimpleKV - A simple key-value database with memory cache and disk persistence.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-TagDB - Work with Tag databases.
      • See also File::FStore, File::Sticker in FileHandling
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TagDB::Tag - Work with Tag databases.
      • DBIx::Placeholder::Named - DBIx::Simple[::Concrete, Interpol].
      • PGObject::Util::DBAdmin
      • DBIx::Raw
      • DBIx::Connector::Retry[::MySQL]
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Database::Abstraction - Database abstraction layer.
      • Abstract class giving read-only access to CSV, XML and SQLite databases via Perl without writing any SQL
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Database::ManagedHandle - Manage database handles safely for long running processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Database::Temp - Create an ad-hoc database which drops itself automatically.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DB-Berkeley - XS-based OO Berkeley DB HASH interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DB-Handy - Pure-Perl flat-file relational database with DBI-like interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DB::Object - An SQL API much like DBI, but with the added benefits that it formats queries in a simple object oriented, chaining way.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DB_File - Perl5 access to Berkeley DB version 1.x.
      • Awating DBD::AnyData2 - DBI access to XML, CSV and other formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Avatica - Driver for Apache Avatica compatible servers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-DB2 - DataBase Driver for IBM's DB2 Universal Database (UDB).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-DuckDB - DuckDB database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Firebird - DBI driver for Firebird RDBMS server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-KB - Kingbase database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-libsql - DBI driver for libsql databases.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::Oracle - Oracle database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Patroni - DBI driver for PostgreSQL with Patroni cluster support.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Pg - PostgreSQL database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-PgAsync - A PostgreSQL database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::SQLite - Self-contained RDBMS in a DBI Driver.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::SQLite::Constants - Common SQLite constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Sybase - Sybase database driver for the DBI module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-Unify - DBI driver for Unify database systems.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBD-XMLSimple - Access XML data via the DBI interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBI::Shell - Interactive command shell for the DBI.
      • Contains dbish
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Abstract - DBI SQL abstraction.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Admin-TableInfo - A wrapper for all of table_info(), column_info(), *_key_info().
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Auto-Migrate - Wrap your database connections and automatically apply db migrations..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::AutoUpgrade::NativeStrings - Automatically upgrade Perl native strings to utf8 before sending them to the database.
      • Primarily for DBD::Oracle, DPD::Pg and DBD::SQLite
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::BatchChunker - Run large database changes safely.
      • For DML
      • See also DBIx::BulkLoader::Mysql, DBIx::Class::BatchUpdate, DBIx::BulkUtil
      • See also DBIx::OnlineDDL below for DDL
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::Storage - Generic Storage Handler.
      • See DBIx::QuickORM as a similar alternative, where the docs discuss and criticize DBIx::Class
      • Or consider simple alternatives like DBIx::Simple or DBIx::Squirrel
      • Or see the great article on DBIx::Class::Result::PrettyPrint at https://peateasea.de/introducing-dbix-class-resultset-prettyprint/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Async - Asynchronous database operations for DBIx::Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-EncodedColumn - Automatically encode columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Helpers - Simplify the common case stuff for DBIx::Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Helper-ColumnNames - Retrieve column names from a resultset.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-InflateColumn-Serializer - Inflators to serialize data structures for DBIx::Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::ParseError - Extensible database error handler.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::ParseError::Parser::MySQL - Parser for MySQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::ParseError::Parser::PostgreSQL - Parser for PostgreSQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::ParseError::Parser::SQLite - Parser for SQLite.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::Schema - Composable schemas.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Schema-Loader - Create a DBIx::Class::Schema based on a database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Class::Storage::DBI - DBI storage handler.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Storage-DBI-MariaDB - Storage::DBI class implementing MariaDB specifics.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-Storage-DBI-mysql-Retryable - MySQL-specific DBIC storage engine with retry support.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Connector - Fast, safe DBI connection and transaction management.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Custom - DBI extension to execute insert, update, delete, and select easily.
      • It extends Object::Simple
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::DataModel - UML-based Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Diff::Schema - Compare schema of two DBI databases.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Fast - DBI fast & easy (another one...).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Lite - Chained and minimal ORM.
      • This module tries very hard to do a lot of work for you
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Migration - Seamless DB schema up- and downgrades.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-DBO - An OO interface to SQL queries and results.
      • Easily constructs SQL queries, and simplifies processing of the returned data
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-NamedParams - Use named parameters instead of '?'.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::OnlineDDL - Run DDL on online databases safely.
      • See also gh-ost
      • See also https://docs.percona.com/percona-toolkit/pt-online-schema-change.html
      • See also https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.4/en/innodb-online-ddl.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Otogiri - A lightweight medicine for using database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-ParseError-MySQL - Error parser for MySQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Query - Simplified abstracted chained DBI subclass.
      • A subclass of DBI
      • Can use Perl data structures described by SQL::Abstract::Complete
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Quick - Object Relational Mapping for the lazy programmer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::QuickDB - Quickly start a db server.
      • PostgreSQL and MySQL are the initially supported drivers
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-QuickORM - Actively maintained Object Relational Mapping that makes getting started Quick and has a rich feature set.
      • An alternative to DBIx::Class, but not a drop-in replacement.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-RunSQL - Run SQL from a file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::SearchBuilder - Encapsulate SQL queries and rows in simple Perl objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Skinny - Simple DBI wrapper/ORMapper.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Squirrel - A DBI extension.
      • The DBIx::Squirrel package extends the DBI by providing a few extra conveniences that are subtle and additive in nature, and, hopefully, quite useful
      • Looks good
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Sunny - DBIx::Sunny is a simple DBI wrapper. It provides better usability for you.
      • DBIx::Sunny supports only SQLite and MySQL
      • Supports nested transaction management using DBIx::TransactionManager
      • See also SQL::Maker::SQLType
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Maker::SQLType
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Wizard - Lightweight, chainable, zero-configuration ORM for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBM::Deep - A pure perl multi-level hash/array DBM that supports transactions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DiaColloDB - Diachronic collocation database, top-level.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Fey - Better SQL Generation Through Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Fey-ORM - A Fey-based ORM.
      • https://github.com/github/gh-ost - Is a triggerless online schema migration solution for MySQL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GraphViz2::DBI - Visualize a database schema as a graph.
      • Uses DBIx::Admin::TableInfo
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async-Pg - Async PostgreSQL client for IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/KiokuDB - Object Graph storage engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LMDB_File - Tie to LMDB (OpenLDAP's Lightning Memory-Mapped Database).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MMapDB - A simple database in shared memory.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mojo::Redis - Redis driver based on Mojo::IOLoop.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MongoDB - Official MongoDB Driver for Perl (EOL).
      • End of life: August 13, 2020
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::Storage - A serialization framework for Moose classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MorboDB - In-memory database, mostly-compatible clone of MongoDB.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MySQL::Hi - Credentials for MySQL/MariaDB from config files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Neo4j-Bolt - Query Neo4j using Bolt protocol.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Neo4j-Client - Build and use the libneo4j-omni library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Neo4j-Driver - Neo4j community graph database driver for Bolt and HTTP.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Async::Redis - Talk to Redis servers via IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OOPS - Object Oriented Persistent Store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OPTIMADE-PropertyDefinitions - Open Database API for Materials Design.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ORLite - Extremely light weight SQLite-specific ORM.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ORLite::Migrate - Extremely light weight SQLite-specific schema migration.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Paws::DynamoDB::Response::Parser - Convert Paws DynamoDB response objects to Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pg-Explain - Object approach at reading explain analyze output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pg-SQL-PrettyPrinter - Pretty printing SQL queries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PGXN-API - Maintain and serve a REST API to search PGXN mirrors.
      • https://pgxn.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PGXN::Site - Maintain and serve a PGXN web site for talking to Postgres.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PomBase-Chobo - Reading OBO files into a Chado database using PostgreSQL COPY.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pongo - A Perl MongoDB interface using XS and the MongoDB C driver.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Protocol::Redis - Redis protocol parser/encoder with asynchronous capabilities..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Protocol::Redis::XS - hiredis based parser compatible with Protocol::Redis.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Quote::LineProtocol - Helper module for Lineprotocol quoting.
      • https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2/reference/syntax/line-protocol/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Redis - Perl binding for Redis database.
      • Redis is an in-memory data store
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Redis-Cluster-Fast - A fast Perl binding for Redis Cluster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Redis::Fast - Perl binding for Redis database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Redis::hiredis - Interact with Redis using the hiredis client.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/RedisDB - Perl extension to access redis database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rose-DB - A DBI wrapper and abstraction layer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rose-DB-Object - Extensible, high performance object-relational mapper (ORM).
      • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Tutorial - A guided tour of the basics of Rose::DB::Object
      • Contains Rose::DB::Object::Loader, Rose::DB::Object::Manager, etc
      • See also https://dev.to/dragostrif/rosedb-orm-and-perl-3dig
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RRD-Fetch - Fetch information from a RRD file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Abstract - Generate SQL from Perl data structures.
      • Contains SQL::Abstract::ExtraClauses aka SQL::Abstract::Plugin::ExtraClauses etc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Abstract::Classic - Generate SQL from Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Abstract::Complete - Generate complete SQL from Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Abstract::More - Extension of SQL::Abstract with more constructs and more flexible API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Abstract-Plugin-TableAlias - Automagical table aliasing.
      • This module is an extension of the SQL::Abstract::Plugin::ExtraClauses plugin
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Beautify - Beautify SQL statements by adding line breaks indentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Concat - SQL concatenator, only cares about bind-vars, to write SQL generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Inserter - Efficient buffered DBI inserter and fast INSERT SQL builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Interp - Interpolate Perl variables into SQL statements.
      • Includes DBIx::Interp
      • POD discusses Class::DBI, DBIx::Simple, FU::SQL, SQL::Abstract, SQL::Interpol, SQL::KeywordSearch, SQL::Preproc and SQL::String
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Maker - Yet another SQL builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Object - Yet another SQL condition builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::QueryMaker - Helper functions for SQL query generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Parser - Validate and parse SQL strings.
      • This module is part of SQL::Statement
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Statement
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Shell - Command interpreter for DBI shells.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-SimpleOps - SQL Simple Operations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQL::Tokenizer - A simple SQL tokenizer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Translator - Manipulate structured data definitions (SQL and more).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SQLite::Work - Report on and update an SQLite database.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/GETTY/SQLite-VecDB-0.001 - SQLite as a vector database using sqlite-vec.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SQL-Wizard - Composable SQL query builder with expression trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tangram - Store pure objects in standard relational databases.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TDB_FileX - Perl access to the trivial database library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TMDB - Perl wrapper for The MovieDB API.
      • TheSchwartz::Simple - Lightweight TheSchwartz job dispatcher using plain DBI - A reliable job queue.
      • TheSchwartz::JobScheduler - Lightweight TheSchwartz job dispatcher with maintained database connections
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::PGXN - Interface to PGXN mirrors and the PGXN API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Yote-SQLObjectStore - Rooted tree based Object Store atop SQL.
      • See also Yote::Spiderpup
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Yote-YapiServer - HTTP server for Yote API.
      • Built on top of Yote::SQLObjectStore
    • DataTraversal
      • CommandLineStuff - ArrayHandling.
      • Data::Fake
      • Data::Grid
      • Data::Table::Text - Write data in tabular text format. Very flexible. Vast list of methods. Aimed at Amazon Web Services
      • Data::Transmute
      • DataTypes
      • Data::Validation
      • DebuggingStuff - For Data::Dump* modules
      • HashHandling
      • [[HTMLHandling]] - For HTML::PopupTreeSelect, HTML::TreeStructured
      • LinguaStuff
      • ModuleBuilding - For Role stuff
      • ParallelProcessing
      • ParsingStuff
      • RedBlackTrees
      • RestfulInterface
      • TheRFCGuide
      • SpreadsheetStuff
      • StringStuff
      • TabularData
      • TestingHelp - For generating random data
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • Text::Table::Manifold - Has many features & its See Also lists about similar 40 modules
      • ToonLanguage
      • [[UTF8]]
      • WebServices - For Data::JPack
      • [[XS]]
      • https://github.com/FasterXML/smile-format-specification/blob/master/smile-specification.md - For Smile format specification
      • https://openrefine.org/ - OpenRefine is a powerful free, open source tool for working with messy data
      • https://sedgewick.io/ - For info about Prof Robert Sedgewick who often worked with trees
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::CPANModules::PickingRandomLinesFromFile - List of modules to pick random lines from a file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-CPANModules-PickingRandomItemsFromList - List of modules to pick random items from a list.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Numerical::Sample - Draw samples from a set.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-CurveFit - Nonlinear Least Squares Fitting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-SlidingWindow - Fixed-capacity rolling window (overwrite-oldest).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-SlidingWindow-Dynamic - Generic, dynamically sized sliding window.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-abgrep - Print lines matching a pattern. Has many options.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Bin-Search - Base class for bin-search tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::BloomUtils - Utilities related to bloom filters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-bookmarks - Export bookmarks from browsers and files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ccdiff - Colored Character diff.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-csvtool - Implements the csvtool core commands.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::CSVUtils - CLI utilities related to CSV.
      • The module contains 64 scripts
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-DataBrowseUtils - CLI utilities related to browsing data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-DPath - A Cmdline tool around Data::DPath (see below).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-DataSizeSpeedUtils - Utilities related to data size/speed.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple - Extensible grep with lexical expression and region control.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-charcode - Greple module to annotate unicode character data.
      • Greple module -Mcharcode (or -Mcc for short) displays information about the matched characters. It can also visualize Unicode zero-width combining or hidden characters, which can be useful for examining text containing visually indistinguishable or imperceptible elements
      • The POD page https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Greple::charcode shows various outputs worthy of close study
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-pw - Module to get password from file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-stripe - Greple zebra stripe module. Produces coloured background bars of output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-subst - Greple module for text search and substitution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-tee - Module to replace matched text by the external command result.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-wordle - Wordle module for greple.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-xlate - Translation support module for greple.
      • This module is amazing. Check the demo
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Greple::xlate
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Greple::xp - Extended pattern module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-nauniq - Non-adjacent uniq.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::PickRandomLines - Pick one or more random lines from input.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-remotediff - Remote diff over rsync.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sdif - sdif (side-by-side) and family tools, cdif (visual char diff) and watchdiff (repetition of diff).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-td - Does for tables what jq does for JSON. Has a huge range of options.
      • Can be used in conjunction with csv2td from App::CSVUtils
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-uricolor - Colorize URIs with ANSI colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::wordlist - Grep words from (or test them against) WordList::*.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-wordlist-wordle - A wordlist wrapper to help solve Wordle.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Archive::BagIt - The main module to handle bags.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Array-Contains - Check if an array contains a specific element.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Sample::Partition - Sample elements from an array by equal partitions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Sample::SimpleRandom - Sample elements randomly (with or without replacement).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Sample::SysRand - Systematic random sampling from an array.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Array::Sample::WeightedRandom - Sample elements randomly, with weights (with or without replacement).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/assign - Destructuring Assignment Syntax for Perl.
      • Instead of assigning a value to a variable, you can now assign to a data structure (array-ref or hash-ref) that specifies how to unpack a data structure into all the variables you need
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Attribute-Handlers - Simpler definition of attribute handlers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/autobox - Call methods on native types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/autobox::Lookup - Easy autoboxed lookup.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/autobox::Text - Turns baubles into trinkets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/autobox-Time-Piece - On-the-fly date conversion.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/BsDiPa - Create or apply binary difference patch.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Catmandu - A data toolkit.
      • This is (presumably) unrelated to Catmandu in DatabaseAndSQL
      • http://metacpan.org/dist/Class-XPath - Adds xpath matching to object trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Clone - Recursively copy Perl datatypes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Clone::AsUTF8Bytes - Recursively copy Perl data converting to UTF-8 bytes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Codec::CBOR - DAG-CBOR encoder and decoder.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Connector - A generic connection to a hierarchical-structured data set.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/csvgrep - Search for patterns in a CSV and display results in a table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CXC-Data-Visitor - Invoke a callback on every element at every level of a data structure..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Alias - Comprehensive set of aliasing operations.
      • See also Data::Reuse
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Clone - Polymorphic data cloning.
      • Does data cloning, i.e. copies things recursively
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Compare - Compare perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Diff - Data structure comparison module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Difference - Compare simple hierarchical data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-DPath - DPath is not XPath!.
      • With this module you can address points in a datastructure by describing a 'path' to it using hash keys, array indexes or some wildcard-like steps. It is inspired by XPath but differs from it
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-DRef - Delimited-key access to complex data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Edit::Xml - Edit data held in the XML format.
      • This intriguing module has a huge POD full of example code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Edit-Xml-Reuse - Reuse Xml via the Dita conref facility.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::FastPack - FastPack Record format, Parsing and Serialising.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Filter::Abstract - Generate Perl filter subs from data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HexDump - Hexadecimal Dumper.
      • Contains links to 8 other dumpers
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-JavaScript - Dump perl data structures into JavaScript code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::JavaScript::Anon - Dump big dumb Perl structs to anonymous JavaScript structs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::JavaScript::LiteObject - Lightweight data dumping to JavaScript.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::LNPath - Lookup on nested data via path.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Match - Complex data structure pattern matching.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::MessagePack::Stream - Yet another messagepack streaming deserializer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::MessagePack::Unpacker - Messagepack streaming deserializer - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Mirror - A simple way to efficiently retrieve data from the World Wide Web.
      • Handles JSON objects, YAML documents, XML instances and CSV files
      • Can create objects, files, file handles and strings
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Munge - Various utility functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::NestedKey - Object-oriented handling of deeply nested hash structures.
      • Uses dot-separated keys to access nested hashes
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Page - Help when paging through sets of results.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Pageset - Page numbering and page sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Pageset-Exponential - Page numbering for very large page numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Path::XS - Fast path-based access to nested data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Peek - A collection of low-level debug facilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Pretty - Data Dump Beautifier.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Printer - Colored & full-featured pretty print of Perl data structures and objects.
      • Does some things very well, but apparently does not handle expressions being passed to p(...)
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Printer::Filter - Create powerful stand-alone filters for Data::Printer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Printer-Filter-EscapeNonPrintable - Escape missing ASCII nonprintable characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Printer::Filter::JMAP - A Data::Printer filter for JMAP::Tester-related objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Printer-Filter-StructDumb - A Data::Printer filter for Struct::Dumb.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Record::Serialize - Flexible serialization of a record.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Record::Serialize::Encode::csv - Encode a record as csv.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Record-Serialize-Encode-dbi - Store a record in a database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Record::Serialize::Role::Encode - Encode Role.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Record::Serialize::Role::Sink - Sink Role.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Reuse - Share constant values with Data::Alias.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Rmap - Recursive map, apply a block to a data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::RenderAsTree - Render any data structure as an object of type Tree::DAG_Node.
      • See Tree::Simple::View & its See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Roundtrip - Convert between Perl data structures, YAML and JSON with unicode support (I believe ...).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Serializer - Modules that serialize data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Section - Read multiple, named, chunks of data out of your __DATA__ section.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Section-Pluggable - Read structured data from __DATA__.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Section::Simple - Read multiple, named, chunks of data out of your __DATA__ section.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Section-Writer - Write __DATA__ section files for Data::Section, Data::Section::Simple or Mojo::Loader::data_section.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Show - Dump data structures with name and point-of-origin.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Smile - Encoder/decoder for Smile data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Smile-XS - XS encoder/decoder for Smile data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Table - Data type related to database tables, spreadsheets, CSV/TSV files, HTML table displays, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Table::Excel - Convert between Data::Table objects and Excel (xls/xlsx) files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Table::Gherkin - Lean Gherkin data table parser.
      • https://cucumber.io/docs/gherkin/reference/#data-tables
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TableData::Lookup - Lookup value in a table data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TableData::Object - Manipulate table-like data structure via table object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TableData::Pick - Pick randomly one or more table rows, with some options.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TableData::Rank - Add a rank column to a table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-TableReader - Extract records from "dirty" tabular data sources.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Tools - Provides set of basic functions for data manipulation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Transfigure - Performs rule-based data transfigurations of arbitrary structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Transform - Performs rule-based data transformations of arbitrary structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Traverse - Callback-based depth-first traversal of Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TreeDumper - Improved replacement for Data::Dumper. Powerful filtering capability.
      • See Tree::Simple::View & its See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Unixish - Implementation for Unixish, a data transformation framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Unixish::pick - Pick one or more random items.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Visitor - Visitor style traversal of Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Visitor::Lite - An easy implementation of Data::Visitor::Callback.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Visitor::Tiny - Recursively walk data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Walk - Traverse Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Walker - A tool for navigating through Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Datafile-Array - Pure-Perl utilities for reading and writing delimited data files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Datafile-Hash - Pure-Perl utilities for datafiles and INI-style config files with multi-level sections.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Array - DBI Wrapper with Perl style data structure interfaces.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-MaterializedPath - Efficiently retrieve and search trees with DBIx::Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-HTML - Just another HTML table generating DBI extension.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Tree - Generate a tree from a self-referential database table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Tree-MaterializedPath - Fast DBI queries and updates on "materialized path" trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Walk - Walk a complex object or reference.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Directory-Diff - Recursively find differences between similar directories.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Doubly - Thread-safe doubly linked list.
      • This module provides access to a C-based doubly linked list
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Doubly::Linked - Doubly linked lists.
      • But see Doubly just above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Doubly-Linked-PP - Linked lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-LoadLines - Load lines from files and network.
      • Handles URLs, encoding and line terminators
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Random - Perl module for random selecting of a file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::RandomLine - Retrieve random lines from a file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Random::Pick - Pick random lines from a file, without duplicates.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Forest - A collection of n-ary tree related modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Google::ProtocolBuffers - Simple interface to Google Protocol Buffers.
      • http://code.google.com/p/protobuf/
      • http://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Path - A simple way to return a path of HoH.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Heap-PQ - Binary heap (priority queue).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Pager - Select a pager (possibly perl-based) & pipe it text if a TTY.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator - A general-purpose iterator class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::DBI - An iterator for returning DBI query results.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::Diamond - Iterate through the files from ARGV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Iterator-Flex - Iterators with flexible behaviors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::IO - Filesystem and stream iterators.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::Misc - Miscellaneous iterator functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::Simple - Simple iterator and utilities.
      • POD says: About 10 times faster than Iterator::Util
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Iterator::Util - Essential utilities for the Iterator class.
      • Uses Iterator
      • But see Iterator::Simple
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-AllUtils - Combines List::Util, List::SomeUtils and List::UtilsBy in one bite-sized package.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::BinarySearch - Binary Search within a sorted array..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::BinarySearch - Pure-Perl Binary Search functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::BinarySearch::XS - Binary Search a sorted array with XS routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::Compare - Compare elements of two or more lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::Gen::Cookbook - Provides functions for generating lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::Insertion - Binary search a list for insertion point.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-Lazy - Generate lists lazily.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-Regexp - Convert list of strings to a regular expression.
      • Includes regexp-opt
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::Util - A selection of general-utility list subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-Util-Find - List utilities related to finding items.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/List-Uniqnum - A rendition of List::Util::uniqnum minus the bugs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Loop-Util - Loop helper keywords.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LTSV-LINQ - LINQ-style query interface for LTSV files.
      • https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/linq/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Finance-Human - Human-readable numbers for accounting and finance.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Iterator - The great new Number::Iterator!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Iterator-XS - Iterate numbers faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/NumSeq::Iter - Generate a coderef iterator from a number sequence specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Iterate - Iterators for objects that know the next element.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Meta - Library to manage data and meta data as one object but keeping it separate.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OLE::Storage - An Interface to Structured Storage Documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OLE-Storage_Lite - Simple Class for OLE document interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OMOP::CSV::Validator - Validates OMOP CDM CSV files against their expected data types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Package::Stash - Routines for manipulating stashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::CSV - Highly flexible CSV parser for large files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parser-FIT - A parser for garmin FIT (Flexible and Interoperable Data Transfer) files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Quantum::Superpositions - QM-like superpositions in Perl.
      • Not for the eigen-challenged
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Skew - Set up a pool of items to return one of, randomly - with some more likely than others.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Skew::Test - Handy means for testing (and fine tuning) Random::Skew.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Search-Tokenizer - Decompose a string into tokens (words).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Junction - Perl6 style Junction operators in Perl5.
      • Essentially a fork of Perl6::Junction without the name Perl6
