      • Perl6::Junction - Perl6 style Junction operators in Perl5
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Scalar-List-Utils - A distribution of general-utility subroutines.
      • Contains Sub::Util
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sim::OPT - OPT is an optimization and parametric exploration program favouring problem decomposition.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Storable - Persistence for Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Storable::Improved - Storable improved with core flaws mitigated.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-Table - Format a header and rows into a table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-TablePrint - Print a table to the terminal and browse it interactively.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Aligner - Its single function align() is used to justify strings to various alignment styles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::ANSITable - Create nice formatted tables using extended ASCII and ANSI colors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::ASCIITable - Create a nice formatted table using ASCII characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-ASCIITable-EasyTable - Create ASCII tables from an array of hashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::AutoCSV - Helper module to automate the use of Text::CSV.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::CSV - Comma-separated values manipulator (using XS or PurePerl).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::CSV::Encoded - Encoding aware Text::CSV.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::CSV_XS - Comma-separated values manipulation routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::FormatTable - Format text tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::NumericData - Many tools for parsing and writing of textual numeric data files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Pipe - Common text filter API.
      • This is a clever module for pipelining textual transformations
      • It has about 30 sub-modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::SimpleTable - Simple eye candy ASCII Tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table - Organize Data in Tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table::Manifold - Render tables in manifold formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table::Tiny - Generate simple text tables from 2D arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Treesitter - Perl binding for tree-sitter.
      • Tree-sitter aims to be: General enough to parse any programming language
      • https://github.com/tree-sitter
      • https://github.com/tree-sitter-perl/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Treesitter-Bash - Parse Bash with Text::Treesitter and extract executable commands.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Table-Read-RelationOn-Tiny - Read binary "relation on (over) a set" from a text table.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Pick - Randomly pick (and remove) an element from a set.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree - An N-ary tree.
      • Contains Tree::Binary2
      • Has an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tree-Binary - An Object Oriented Binary Tree for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Binary::Dictionary - A dictionary API to a binary tree.
      • Not related to Tree::Binary, with a primitive POD
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Binary::XS - Perl extension for manipulating binary tree structure.
      • Not compatible with Tree::Binary
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tree-DAG_Node - An N-ary tree.
      • See also Data::RenderAsTree
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::MultiNode - A multi-node tree object. Most useful for modeling hierarchical data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Parser - Module to parse formatted files into tree structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::R - Perl extension for the R-tree data structure and algorithms.
      • See also Tree::STR
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Simple - A simple tree object.
      • Has an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Simple::View - A set of classes for viewing Tree::Simple hierarchies.
      • Has an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Simple::VisitorFactory - A factory object for dispensing Visitor objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::STR - A Sort-Tile-Recursive tree index.
      • See also Geo::Geos::Index::STRtree
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Ternary - Perl implementation of ternary search trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tree::Trie - A data structure optimized for prefix lookup.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tree-Visualize - A module for visualizing Tree structures.
      • http://metacpan.org/dist/Tree-XPathEngine - A re-usable XPath engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Util::Medley::List - Utility methods for working with lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WordListRole::Bloom - Provide word_exists() that uses bloom filter.
      • http://metacpan.org/dist/XML-XPathEngine - A re-usable XPath engine for DOM-like trees.
    • DataTypes
      • DataTraversal - ConstantStuff.
      • DatesAndTimes
      • DataValidation
      • JsonStuff
      • LinguaStuff
      • TheRFCGuide
      • RegexpStuff
      • [[UUID]]
      • Types::Standard - for Type::Tiny
      • https://tc39.es/ecma426/#sec-base64-vlq
      • https://www.inkandswitch.com/tangents/bijou64/
      • https://wiki.haskell.org/Newtype
      • https://www.sqlite.org/datatype3.html - For data types in SQLite
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Bitfield - Bitmask for Tracking Boolean Sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-AsciiEmoji - Emoji.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-CrockfordBase32Utils - Utilities related to Crockford's Base 32 encoding.
      • https://www.crockford.com/base32.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Attribute::Lexical - Sane scoping of function/variable attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Basic::Types::XS - Fast but limited type constraints.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CEFACT-Unit - CEFACT unit handling.
      • Uses Data::CEFACT::Unit below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Contract::Declare - Simple contract system for Perl interfaces.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Convert-Binary-C - Binary Data Conversion using C Types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Convert::VLQ - Convert to and from VLQ base64 representation used in source maps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::CEFACT::Unit - Data object for CEFACT unit.
      • See CEFACT::Unit above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Float - Details of the floating point data type.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Frame - This implements a data frame container that uses PDL for individual columns.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HashType - Data object for hash type.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HexConverter - High performance hex string to binary converter.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-IconText - Work with icon text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Cloudlet - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Generate - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Interface::Known - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Interface::Simple - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Interface::Subobjects - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Identifier::Interface::Userdata - Format independent identifier object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-InfoBox - Data object for info box.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Integer - Details of the native integer data type.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Login - Data object for login.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Message-Board - Data object for Message board.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-OFN-Common - OFN common data objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::OFN::Thing - Data object for OFN thing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::OptList - Parse and validate simple name/value option pairs.
      • Used by the pragma 'syntax'
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::OptList::Object - Data::OptList, but object-oriented.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Perl - Base classes wrapping fundamental Perl data types..
      • Data::Perl is a collection of classes that wrap fundamental data types that exist in Perl
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Pond - Perl-based open notation for data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TagMap - Module for working with tag maps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Text::Simple - Data object for text in language.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Types - Validate and convert data types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DefHash - Define things according to a specification, using hashes.
      • See DefHash in various places in this para
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Encode::Bijou64 - Encode and decode Bijou64 integers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Enum-Declare - Declarative enums for Perl with compile-time constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Enum-Declare-Common - A curated collection of commonly-needed enums.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Format::Human::Bytes - Format a bytecount and make it human readable.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::DefHash - Manipulate defhash.
      • See DefHash in various places in this para
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hydrogen - Utilities for the simplest elements of Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Keyword-Anonymous-Object - Anonymous objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooseX-Types - Organize your Moose types in libraries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooseX-Types-DateTime - DateTime related constraints and coercions for Moose .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::Types::DateTime::MoreCoercions - Extensions to MooseX::Types::DateTime.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::HandlesVia - NativeTrait-like behavior for Moo..
      • But prefer Sub::HandlesVia below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Newtype - Perl implementation of an approximation for Haskell's newtype.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Number::Bytes::Human - Convert byte count to human readable format.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Format - Perl extension for formatting numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Format-Metric - Format number with metric prefix, with some options.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Types - Enable Perl data types.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Range-Merge - Merges ranges of data including subset/superset ranges.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Regexp::Pattern::DefHash - Regexp patterns related to DefHash.
      • See DefHash in various places in this para
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rope - Tied objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scalar::Number - Numeric aspects of scalars.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scalar::Readonly - Functions for controlling whether any scalar variable is read-only.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Specio - Type constraints and coercions for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::BooleanSimple - Gets the boolean representative of a string.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Struct-Dumb - Make simple lightweight record-like structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::HandlesVia - Alternative handles_via implementation.
      • Replaces MooX::HandlesVia above for trait handling
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-HandlesVia-XS - XS parts for Sub::HandlesVia; no user-serviceable parts inside.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-BoundedInteger - Limit the magnitude of a number in a scalar.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-Cycle - Cycle through a list of values via a scalar.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-Toggle - False and true, alternately, ad infinitum.
      • An endless series of Booleans
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Guess - Infer data types from an array of scalars.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Library - Tiny, yet Moo(se)-compatible type libraries.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Params - Sub signature validation using Type::Tiny type constraints and coercions.
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/toby_inkster/2023/02/using-typeparams-effectively.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Registry - A glorified hashref for looking up type constraints.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Type-Tiny - Tiny, yet Moo(se)-compatible, type constraint.
      • Contains Types::Standard and many other modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Tiny::Class - Type constraints based on the "isa" method.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Types-Capabilities - Don't care what type of data you are given, just what you can do with it.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Common - The one stop shop.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Common::Numeric - Commonly used numeric types.
      • Drop-in replacement for MooseX::Types::Common::Numeric
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Common::String - Commonly used string types.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Types-Equal - Type constraints for single value equality.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Types::Music - Type Library for Music Programming.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Win32::API::Struct - C struct support package for Win32::API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XDR/source/XDR.pm - External Data Representation Standard.
      • A file of constants
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XDR-Gen - Generator for XDR (de)serializers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XDR::Parse - Creation of an AST of an XDR specification.
      • See RfcGuide for rfc4506
    • DataValidation
      • DataTypes - DataTraversal.
      • DebuggingStuff
      • EmailStuff
      • [[HTMLHandling]]
      • StringStuff
      • https://openrefine.org/ - OpenRefine is a powerful free, open source tool for working with messy data
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::SahUtils - Collection of CLI utilities for Sah and Data::Sah.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Attribute::Validate - Validate your subs with attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alt-Params-Util-PLICEASE - Simple, compact and correct param-checking functions.
      • Contains Params::Util
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Brick - Complex business rule data validation.
      • See also Brick::Tutorial, Brick::UserGuide
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::ArgChecker - An extensible CGI parameter validation module (allowing commonly used checks on parameters to be called more concisely and consistently).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::FormMagick::Validator - Validate data from FormMagick forms.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Validate - Advanced CGI form parser and type validation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::ParmList - Methods for processing named parameter lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Utils - Class utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Censor - Censor sensitive stuff in a data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Checks - Value constraint checking.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Constraint - Prototypical value checking.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Domain - Data description and validation.
      • See also CGI::Expand and Test::InDomain
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::FormValidator - Validates user input (usually from an HTML form) based on input profile.
      • http://mark.stosberg.com/Tech/perl/form-validation/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::FormValidator::Tutorial - A tutorial!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::FormValidator::Util::HTML - HTML utility functions for DFV.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Message::Simple - Data object for simple message.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Processor - A tool for transforming, verifying, and producing Perl data structures from / against a schema, defined as a Perl data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Sah - Fast and featureful data structure validation.
      • It's See Also contains a long list of validators
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Sah::Coerce - Coercion rules for Data::Sah.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Sah::CoerceCommon - Common stuffs for Data::Sah::Coerce and Data::Sah::CoerceJS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Sah::CoerceJS - Generate coercer code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Sah::Tiny - Validate Sah schemas with as little code as possibl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Transmute - Transmute (transform) data structure using rules data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Validate - Common data validation methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Validate-Domain - Domain and host name validation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Validate::IP - IPv4 and IPv6 validation methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Validate-Sanctions - Validate a name against sanctions lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Verifier - Profile based data verification with Moose type constraints.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::LocalizeNewlines - Localize the newlines for one or more files.
      • See also PerlIO::eol
      • 1 of 2: dos2unix.pl
      • 2 of 2: unix2dos.pl
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Flat::Profile - Streaming-first profiling for CSV/TSV flat files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Flat-Schema - Deterministic schema contracts for flat files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Func-Util - Functional programming utilities with XS/OP acceleration.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getargs::Long - Named subroutine arguments, with optional type checking.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getargs::Mixed - Perl extension allowing subs to handle mixed parameter lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Getopt::GetArgs - Perl module to allow enhanced argument passing, including passing of case-insensitive named arguments as well as positioned arguments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Template::Associate::FormValidator - HTML::Template::Associate Data::FormValidator plugin.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/kura - Store constraints for Data::Checks, Type::Tiny, Moose and more.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Kwalify - Kwalify schema for data structures.
      • Contains 19 methods for checking data properties - Mo utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mo::utils
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mo::utils::Array - Mo array utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mo-utils-CEFACT - Mo UN/CEFACT Common Code utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mo-utils-CSS - Mo CSS utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mo::utils::Hash - Mo hash utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mo-utils-Language - Mo language utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mo::utils::Number - Mo number utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mo-utils-URI - Mo utilities for URIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mouse::Util::TypeConstraints - Type constraint system for Mouse.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Number::ZipCode::JP - Validate Japanese zip-codes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OMOP-CSV-Validator - Validates OMOP CDM CSV files against their expected data types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Check - A generic input parsing/checking mechanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Filter - Secure field filtering for parameter construction.
      • Checks for presence or absence, but does not validate
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Get - Get the parameters to a subroutine in any way you want.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Sah - Validate method/function parameters using Sah schemas.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Smart - Use both positional and named arguments in a subroutine - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Params-SomeUtil - Simple, compact and correct param-checking functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Validate - Validate method/function parameters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::Validate::Strict - Validates a set of parameters against a schema.
      • Note: Silently skips type checks for undef values
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Params::ValidationCompiler - Build an optimized subroutine parameter validator once, use it forever.
      • Recommended by Dave Rolsky
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlIO-eol - PerlIO layer for normalizing line endings.
      • See also File::LocalizeNewlines
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Poz - A simple, composable, and extensible data validation library for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Return-Set - Return a value optionally validated against a strict schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sah - Schema for data structures (specification).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah/view/lib/Sah/FAQ.pod - Frequently asked questions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sah::Schemas::DefHash - Sah schemas for DefHash.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah-SchemaBundle-Identifier - A string that starts with [A-Za-z_] followed by zero of more [A-Za-z0-9_].
      • Contains many sub-modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah-SchemaBundle-CrockfordBase32 - Sah schemas related to Crockford's Base 32 encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah-SchemaBundle-KDEActivity - Sah schemas related to KDE activities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah/view/lib/Sah/Type.pod - Standard types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scalar::Util - A selection of general-utility scalar subroutines.
      • This module contains a dozen subroutines
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Signature::Attribute::Checked - Apply value constraint checks to subroutine parameters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Smart::Args - Argument validation for you.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Ident - Clean up strings for use as identifiers and in URLs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::NamedParams - Perl extension for using named arguments with any sub.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Valiant - Object validation inspired by Ruby on Rails and more.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Validate-Simple - (Relatively) Simple way to validate input parameters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Validate-Tiny - Minimalistic data validation.
    • DatesAndTimes
      • DataTypes - For MooseX::Types::DateTime - [[cron]].
      • [[HTMLHandling]]
      • LinguaStuff
      • LocaleStuff
      • TheRFCGuide
      • http://datetime.perl.org/
      • https://dev.to/davorg/processing-dates-and-times-with-perl-31gh - (2021-11-11) deprecates Date::Manip
      • https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/common-microsyntaxes.html#global-dates-and-times - For Global dates and times
      • https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime - For Date and Time Format
      • https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#dateTime
      • https://www.kcaran.com/posts/utc-local-date-conversion-in-perl-with-time-piece.html
      • https://www.tondering.dk/claus/calendar.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-SpreadRevolutionaryDate - Spread date and time from Revolutionary (Republican) Calendar on Bluesky, Twitter, Mastodon, Freenode and Liberachat..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-week - Colorful calendar command for ANSI terminal.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Calendar::Simple - Perl extension to create simple calendars.
      • Uses DateTime if installed
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date - Extremely fast Date framework with timezones, microseconds, relative dates and intervals support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Age - Return an age or age range from date(s).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Calc - Gregorian calendar date calculations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Calendar - Calendar objects for different holiday schemes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Cmp - Compare two dates with approximate parsing support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Extract - Extract probable dates from strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Find - Find year, month, day from (filename) strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays - Date::Holidays::* adapter and aggregator for all your holiday needs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::Abstract - Abstract class for Date::Holidays::* packages.
      • See also Date::Holidays::Super
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::Adapter - An adapter class for Date::Holidays::* modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Holidays-AU - Determine Australian Public Holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Holidays-BY - Determine public holidays and business days in Belarus.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::DE - Determine German holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::DK - Determine Danish public holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::FR - Determine French holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::PT - Determine Portuguese public holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::Super - A SUPER class for the Date::Holidays::* modules.
      • See also Date::Holidays::Abstract
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Holidays-TW - Determine whether it is Taiwan Holidays or not.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::UK - Determine UK Public Holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Holidays-US - Date::Holidays Adapter for US Federal holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Holidays::USExtended - Provides an extended set of United States holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Interval - Handling of temporal intervals based on Date::Manip.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Japanese::Era - Conversion between Japanese Era / Gregorian calendar.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Japanese::Holiday - Calculate Japanese Holiday.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Manip - Date manipulation routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Parse - Parse date strings into time values.
      • Provide string to unixtime conversions - https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Parse::Modern.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::RangeParser::EN - Parser for plain English date/time range strings.
      • Extremely flexible
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Utility - A class that represents a datetime in various formats.
      • Has a huge number of methods
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime - A date and time object for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Calendar::Japanese::Era - DateTime Extension for Japanese Eras.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Event::Jewish::Sunrise - Calculate halachically interesting times.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Event::Sunrise - Perl DateTime extension for computing the sunrise/sunset on a given day.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Builder - Create DateTime parser classes and objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::DateManip - Perl DateTime extension to convert Date::Manip dates and durations to DateTimes and vice versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::DB2 - Parse and format DB2 dates and times.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Duration - Format and parse DateTime::Durations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Flexible - Flexibly parse strings and turn them into DateTime objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::ICal - Parse and format iCal datetime and duration strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Intl - A Web Intl.DateTimeFormat Class Implementation.
      • This is a big module with a huge range of features
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-ISO8601 - Parses ISO8601 formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-JP - Japanese DateTime Parser and Formatter.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Lite - Parse and format datetimes with strptime patterns, returning DateTime::Lite objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Mail - Convert between DateTime and RFC2822/822 formats.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2822
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::MySQL - Parse and format MySQL dates and times.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Natural - Parse informal natural language date/time strings.
      • Note: This package contains various modules, including DateTime::Format::Natural::Lang::EN, which provides the English specific grammar and variables
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-DateParse - Parses Date::Parse compatible formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-RelativeTime - A Web Intl.RelativeTimeFormat Class Implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::Strptime - Parse and format strp and strf time patterns.
      • But see DateTime::Format::Lite
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Text - Find a Date in Text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Unicode - Unicode CLDR Formatter for DateTime.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Format::W3CDTF - Parse and format W3CDTF datetime strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-HiRes - Create DateTime objects with sub-second current time resolution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Lite - Lightweight, low-dependency drop-in replacement for DateTime.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Locale - Localization support for DateTime.pm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Locale-FromCLDR - DateTime Localised Data from Unicode CLDR.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Ordinal - The great new DateTime::Ordinal!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Schedule - Determine scheduled days in range based on exclusions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Set - Datetime sets and set math.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::SpanSet - Set of DateTime spans.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-TimeZone - Time zone object base class and factory.
      • Normally use via DateTime above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Tiny - A date object, with as little code as possible.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTimeX-Easy - Parse a date/time string using the best method available.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTimeX-Format-Ago - I should have written this module "3 years ago".
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Date - Find and Format Date Headers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Date::Format - Produce RFC 2822 date strings.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2282
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::CalendarMonth - Generate and manipulate HTML calendar months.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-CalendarMonthSimple - Perl Module for Generating HTML Calendars.
      • But use the previous module
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Date - Date conversion routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Month-Simple - Simple month-based date arithmetic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-CalDAVTalk - Module to talk CalDAV and give a JSON interface to the data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-CardDAVTalk - A library for talking to CardDAV servers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Oxford-Calendar - University of Oxford calendar conversion routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/POSIX-RT-Signal - POSIX Real-time signal handling functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/POSIX-RT-Timer - POSIX real-time timers.
      • Contains POSIX::RT::Clock
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SIRTX-Datecode - Module for interacting with SIRTX Datecodes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::JSCalendar - Convert between iCalendar * JSCalendar.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-JSContact - Bidirectional vCard <=> JSContact conversion.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-AutoRes - Use Time::HiRes or fall back to core code.
      • Date formating subroutines - https://metacpan.org/dist/TimeDate.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Duration - Rounded or exact English expression of durations. Built-in.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Duration::LocaleObject - Time duration string in language chosen by an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Duration::Object - Time::Duration, but an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Duration::Parse - Parse string that represents time duration.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Duration::Parse::More - Parse natural language time duration expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Elapsed - This module transforms the elapsed seconds into a human readable string.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-HiRes - High resolution alarm, sleep, gettimeofday, interval timers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-HiRes-Sleep-Until - Provides common ways to sleep until....
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-HiRes-Value - Represent a time value or interval in exact microseconds.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::JulianDay - Julian calendar manipulations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Left - Object model for time limits.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Local - Efficiently compute time from local and GMT time.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Moment - Represents a date and time of day with an offset from UTC.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-OlsonTZ-Data - Olson timezone data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::ParseDate - Date parsing both relative and absolute.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Piece - Object Oriented time objects. Built-in.
      • Includes Time::Seconds
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Precise - Extending standard time related functions to always include nanoseconds and always use full year.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::Spec - A wrapper around struct timespec.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Str - Parse and format date/time strings in multiple standard formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::TAI64 - Perl extension for converting TAI64 strings into standard unix timestamps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TimeZone-TimeZoneDB - Interface to https://timezonedb.com for looking up Timezone data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Vigil-Calendar - Provides a way to describe a calendar _month so that you can easily build HTML calendars or populate your flavour of web calendar.
    • DebianOS
      • My home page http://savage.net.au/index.html for the Debian.Wiki - TheOSGuide.
      • - Lists the TCP port # and name of dozens of services.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Container-Builder - Build Container archives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dpkg - Module with core variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DPKG-Packages-Parser - Parses the Debian Packages file.
    • DebuggingStuff
      • MeasureMetrics - For Devel::Cover - DataTraversal.
      • MemoryUsage
      • ModuleBuilding
      • TestingHelp
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Carp-Always - Warns and dies noisily with stack backtraces.
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/dami/2024/05/carpobject-an-object-oriented-replacement-for-carp-and-carpclan.html - https://metacpan.org/pod/Carp::Object.
      • Can be used by Struct::Dumb. See DataTypes - https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Dump.
      • See also Data::Pretty
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Dump-Color - Like Data::Dump, but with color.
      • Data::Printer - https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Dump-HTML-Collapsible.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Dump::Partial - Dump data structure compactly and potentially partially.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Dumper - Stringified perl data structures, suitable for both printing and eval.
      • See Data::TreeDumper, Tree::Simple::View & the latter's See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Dumper::Compact - Vertically compact width-limited data formatter.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Dumper-Concise - Less indentation and newlines plus sub deparsing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Dumper-Interp - Interpolate Data::Dumper output into strings for human consumption.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Dumper::Simple - Easily dump variables with names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Debug-CodeBlock - Add DEBUG codeblocks to your code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Debug-Comments - Source filter which turns comments into log messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Debug::Easy - A Handy Debugging Module With Colorized Output and Formatting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Debug::Filter::PrintExpr - Convert comment lines to debug print statements.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::Assert - Assertions for Perl >= 5.14.
      • See also assertions
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Bug - Transparent inline debugging probe (pure Perl).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-EndStats - Display run time and dependencies after running code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-MAT-Dumper - Write a heap dump file for later analysis.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-OverloadInfo - Introspect overloaded operators.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::ptkdb - Perl debugger using a Tk GUI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-REPL - A modern perl interactive shell.
      • Unfortunately, I (author of Runtime::Debugger) did not find a way to get the lexical variables in a scope
      • See also Shell::Perl
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::StackTrace - An object representing a stack trace.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::StrictMode - Determine whether strict (but slow) tests should be enabled.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Trace-Subs - Generate, track, store and print code flow and stack traces.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dump-Krumo - Fancy, colorful, human readable dumps of your data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Loo - Pure XS data introspector and code deparser with customisable colour output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/perl - perl5db.pl - The standard perl debugger.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Reply - Read, eval, print, loop, yay!.
      • Same problem as Devel::REPL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Runtime-Debugger - Easy to use REPL with existing lexical support and DWIM tab completion.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Smart::Comments - Comments that do more than just sit there.
    • DFA
      • MathsLibraries - [[Graphs]].
      • ParallelProcessing - For Sub::Genius
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deterministic_finite_automaton
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-GUI-Cellgraph - Draw pattern by cellular automata.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-GUI-GoLP - A GUI for viewing Life-like cellular automata, in Perl/Prima.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-FSM - Data object for Finite State Machine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DFA-Kleene - Kleene's Algorithm for Deterministic Finite Automata.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DFA::Statemap - This is the SMC (State Machine Compiler) runtime for target language Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Dict::FSA - This module is a perl wrapper around fsa.
      • See http://www.eti.pg.gda.pl/~jandac/fsa.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FLAT - Formal Language & Automata Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FSA::Engine - A Moose Role to convert an object into a Finite State Machine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FSM::Basic - Finite state machine using HASH as state definitions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FSA::Rules - Build simple rules-based state machines in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::FSM - Deterministic top-down parser based on a Finite State Machine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::FA - A Set of Discrete Finite Automata.
      • Contains Set::FA::Element, a single Discrete Finite Automaton
    • DistZilla
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Dist::Zilla - Distribution builder; installer not included!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-Zilla-Plugin-GitHub-CreateRelease - Create a GitHub Release.
    • DnsStuff
      • [[HTTPHandling]] - EventStuff.
      • WebServices
      • https://c-ares.org/
      • https://dnsrobot.net - For DNS Robot: 53 free online DNS and network tools
      • https://publicsuffix.org/list/public_suffix_list.dat
      • https://metacpan.org/release/EGOR/Alien-cares-0.01 - Find or build the c-ares async DNS resolver library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::DNS::EtcHosts - Use /etc/hosts before DNS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sdseasydyn - EasyDNS DDNS updater (CLI-first App distribution).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sslmaker - Be your own SSL certificate authority.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-DNS - An interface to the DNS root zone database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DNS-Robot - Perl client for the DNS Robot API (dnsrobot.net).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/EV::cares - High-performance async DNS resolver using c-ares and EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DNS - Perl Interface to the Domain Name System.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::DNS::Native - Non-blocking system DNS resolver.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DNS-Paranoid - Paranoid dns resolver.
      • Can use with Furl::HTTP
      • If using LWP, should wrap Net::DNS::Paranoid module with LWPx::ParanoidHandler
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DNS-Resolver-Unbound - Net::DNS resolver based on libunbound.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DNS-SEC - DNSSEC extensions to Net::DNS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Domain - Attempt to evaluate the current host's internet name and domain.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Domain-Parts - Extract sub-domain, domain and TLD parts of a domain name..
      • Meaning 'www.perlmonks.org' => Domain: perlmonks.org, Subdomain: www, TLD: org
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Domain::TLD - Work with TLD names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::IDN::Encode - Internationalizing Domain Names in Applications (IDNA).
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3492, rfc5890
      • See also URI in [[HTTPHandling]]
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-IDN-PP - A pure-Perl implementation of the Punycode algorithm for encoding internationalized domain names (IDNs).
      • The Mozilla Public Suffix List - https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::PublicSuffixList.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SSLeay - Perl bindings for OpenSSL and LibreSSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenDNS-MyIP - Get your public IP address.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-DNSMadeEasy - Accessing DNSMadeEasy API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Zonemaster-Backend - A system for running Zonemaster tests asynchronously through an RPC-API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Zonemaster-CLI - Run Zonemaster tests from the command line.
      • One of a set of Zonemaster::* modules - A tool to check the quality of a DNS zone.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Zonemaster-Engine
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Zonemaster-LDNS - Perl wrapper for the ldns DNS library.
    • EBookHandling
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-EbookUtils - Command-line utilities related to ebooks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chandra-EPUB - Epub reader built with Perl and Chandra.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EBook-Gutenberg - Fetch ebooks from Project Gutenberg.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EBook-Ishmael - Convert ebook documents to plain text.
      • Formats supported
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/FB3-Convert - Scripts and API for converting FB3 from and to different formats.
    • EditorConfigFiles
      • DataTraversal - ConfigFiles.
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://editorconfig.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Config::Neat - Parse/render human-readable configuration files with inheritance and schema validation.
    • EmailStuff
      • LoggingStuff - For Log::Dispatch::Email::Mailer - [[Acronyms]].
      • NetworkProgramming
      • TheRFCGuide
      • WebServices
      • JMAP rather than IMAP: https://jmap.io/
      • EAV::XS (Address validation)
      • https://metacpan.org/author/RJBS - For Email::* & Mail::*
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-mailmake - App harness for the mailmake CLI.
      • See Mail::Make below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Milter-Limit - Sendmail Milter that limits message rate by sender.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Milter::Limit::Plugin::BerkeleyDB - BerkeleyDB driver for App::Milter::Limit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Milter::Limit::Plugin::SQLite - SQLite driver for App::Milter::Limit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-wsgetmail - Fetch mail from the cloud using webservices.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-JSEmail - Convert between Email::MIME and the JSON format from RFC 8621 (JMAP Mail).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Abstract - Unified interface to mail representations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Abuse-Investigator - Analyse spam email to identify originating hosts, hosted URLs, and suspicious domains.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Address - RFC 2822 Address Parsing and Creation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Address-List - RFC close address list parsing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::MIME - Easy MIME message handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Mailer - Multi-purpose emailer for HTML, auto-text, attachments, and templates.
      • Modelled on Email::Simple
      • Looks good
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Send-YYClouds - Send simple mail using smtp relay server.
      • This module has a huge list of contributors - https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Sender.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Email-Sender-Transport-Mailgun - Mailgun transport for Email::Sender.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP - Send email over SMTP.
      • Replaces Email::Sender::Transport::SMTPS
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP::Persistent - An SMTP client that stays online.
      • The stock Email::Sender::Transport::SMTP reconnects each time it sends a message. This transport only reconnects when the existing connection fails
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Simple - Simple parsing of RFC2822 message format and headers.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2822
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Stuffer - A more casual approach to creating and sending Email:: emails.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Email::Valid - Check validity of Internet email addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Encode-IMAPUTF7 - Modification of UTF-7 encoding for IMAP.
      • https://www.exim.org/ - Supercedes sendmail, under Linux at least.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MailTools - Bundle of ancient email modules.
      • Includes Mail::Internet
      • If you start writing a new application, you should use the Mail::Box distribution, which has more features and handles messages much better according to the RFCs
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-AuthenticationResults - Object Oriented Authentication-Results Headers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mail::BIMI - Brand Indicators for Message Identification (BIMI) retrieval, validation, and processing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Box - Manage a mailbox, a folder with messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Box-IMAP4 - Handle IMAP4 folders as client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Box-Parser-C - Parsing folders for MailBox with C routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mail::Box::Parser::Perl - Reading messages from file using Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Box-POP3 - Handle POP3 folders as client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-DKIM-Iterator - Iterative DKIM validation or signing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-DMARC - Perl implementation of DMARC.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mail::DMARC::PurePerl - Pure Perl implementation of DMARC.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-DWIM - Do-What-I-Mean Mailer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Exim-ACL-Geolocation - Map IP addresses to location information.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mail::IMAPClient - An IMAP Client API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-IMAPTalk - IMAP client interface with lots of features.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Make - Strict, Fluent MIME Email Builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Message - General message object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Milter-Authentication - A Perl Mail Authentication Milter.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-POP3Client - Perl 5 module to talk to a POP3 (RFC1939) server.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc1939
      • Part of Mail::Message - Base-class and error reporter for Mail::Box.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Transport - Use Mail Transfer Agents (MTAs).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-Sendmail - Simple platform independent mailer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mail-SPF - An object-oriented implementation of Sender Policy Framework.
      • - Spamassassin scores from database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MailboxValidator::SingleValidation - Email verification module for Perl using MailboxValidator API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Base32 - Base32 encoder and decoder.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Base32::XS - Encoding and decoding Base32.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Base64 - Encoding and decoding of base64 strings.
      • http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base64#URL_applications
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Charset - Charset Information for MIME.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::EncWords - Deal with RFC 2047 encoded words (improved).
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2047
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Entity - Class for parsed-and-decoded MIME message.
      • Before reading further, you should see MIME::Tools to make sure that you understand where this module fits into the grand scheme of things
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIME-Lite - Low-calorie MIME generator.
      • There are a number of alternatives, like Email::MIME or MIME::Entity and Email::Sender, which you should probably use instead
      • Actually, just start with Mail::Box
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIME-Mini - Minimal code to parse/create mbox files and mail messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::QuotedPrint - Encoding and decoding of quoted-printable strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIME::Tools - Modules for parsing (and creating!) MIME entities.
      • POD contains 2 important diagrams
      • "MIME Type" is the old name for "Media Type" - Definition of MIME types.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIME-Types
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::IMAP::Client - Not so simple IMAP client library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-SimpleMail - A simple module to send emails via simplemail.co.in.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-MitDK - Perl API for http://mit.dk/.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/KARASIK/Net-MitDK-0.07/source/README.md - For English docs
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::validMX - Use DNS and/or regular expressions to verify if an email address could be valid.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Qmail-Deliverable - Determine deliverability of local addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sieve-Generator - Generate Sieve email filter scripts.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sisimai - Analyze bounce mails.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-MailboxOrg - Perl client for Mailbox.org API.
    • EnumStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Enum - Typed enum.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-Enumeration - Abstract parent class of enum classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Type::Enum - Build Enum-like classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Enum - Immutable enumeration classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooseX::Enumeration - A native attribute trait for enums.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Enumeration - Shortcuts for working with enum attributes in Moo.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Enum - replacement for if ($foo eq 'bar').
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-PadX-Enum - Syntactic sugar for enum-like singleton-bearing Object::Pad classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Type::Tiny::Enum - String enum type constraints.
    • EventStuff
      • AlgorithmicStuff - For retry mechanisms - AiEngines.
      • ArchiveFiles
      • ChildProcesses
      • DatabaseAndSQL - For Async::Redis, DBD::PgAsync etc
      • DataTraversal - For CSV handling
      • DatesAndTimes
      • DnsStuff

      • [[HTTPServers]]

      • PoeStuff

      • RotateLogFiles
      • SpreadsheetStuff

      • XWindowsStuff - For i3
      • YamlStuff
      • [[ZMQ]]
      • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Using_promises - For a good intro to promises
      • https://docs.nats.io/reference/reference-protocols/nats-protocol
      • https://gearman.org/protocol/ - For the upstream protocol spec
      • https://libuv.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlop#I%2FO-Operators
      • https://perl.plover.com/TieFile/why-not-DB_File
      • https://promisesaplus.com/ - For Promises/A+
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-liburing - Alien wrapper for liburing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Cron - Abstract implementation of the cron(8) scheduling algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::RateLimiter::TokenBucket - Loop-agnostic Token Bucket Rate Limiter.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent - The DBI of event loop programming.
      • Contains AE, a simpler/faster/newer/cooler AnyEvent API
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AnyEvent-FTP - Simple asynchronous FTP client and server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::Gearman - Asynchronous Gearman client/worker module for AnyEvent applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AnyEvent-Impl-Prima - Prima event loop adapter for AnyEvent.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AnyEvent-ITM - Debug ITM/SWD stream deserializer for AnyEvent.
      • Aka YAYA "Yet Another Download Accelerator" - Moo wrapper for queued downloads via Net::Curl & AnyEvent.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::Net::Curl::Queued
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::Promises - Simple implementation of Promises/A+ spec.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::XSPromises - Another Promises library, this time implemented in XS for performance.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AnyEvent-YACurl - Yet Another curl binding for AnyEvent.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Async-Event-Interval - Scheduled and one-off restartable asynchronous events.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Conduit - Serve HTTP with Future::IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Events - Extensible instrumentation framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/EV - Perl interface to libev, a high performance full-featured event loop.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-ClickHouse - Async ClickHouse client using EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Etcd - Async etcd v3 client using native gRPC and EV/libev.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Future - Minimalist and high-performance async control flow for EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Gearman - Asynchronous Gearman client and worker on libev.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/EV::Hiredis - Asynchronous redis client using hiredis and EV.
      • See also EV::Redis below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Kafka - High-performance asynchronous Kafka/Redpanda client using EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-MariaDB - Async MariaDB/MySQL client using libmariadb and EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Memcached - High-performance asynchronous memcached client using EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Nats - High-performance asynchronous NATS client using EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Pg - Asynchronous PostgreSQL client using libpq and EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/EV::Redis - Asynchronous redis client using hiredis and EV.
      • Fork of EV::Hiredis
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/EV-Websockets - WebSocket client/server using libwebsockets and EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Event-RPC - Event based transparent Client/Server RPC framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Feersum - A PSGI engine for Perl based on EV/libev.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future - Represent an operation awaiting completion.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Future::AsyncAwait - Deferred subroutine syntax for futures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-Batch - Process multiple Future-returning operations with concurrency control.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-Batch-XS - XS implementation of batch processing for Future operations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO - Future-returning IO methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Impl-Glib - Implement Future::IO using Glib.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Impl-KQueue - Implement Future::IO using kqueue(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Future::IO::Impl::Ppoll - Implement Future::IO using ppoll(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Impl-Tickit - Implement Future::IO with Tickit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Impl-Uring - A Future::IO implementation for IO::Uring.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Impl-UV - Implement Future::IO using UV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-Resolver - Name resolver methods for Future::IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-IO-TLS - A TLS interface for Future::IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Future::Queue - A FIFO queue of values that uses Futures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-Selector - Manage a collection of pending futures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-Uring - Future-returning io_uring functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Future::Utils - Utility functions for working with Future objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Future-XS - Experimental XS implementation of Future.
      • Object-style access to files, pipes, etc - https://metacpan.org/dist/IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::All - IO::All to Larry Wall!.
      • The Swiss Army knife of file ops
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::AIO - Asynchronous/Advanced Input/Output.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::AIO::LoadLimited - A tiny IO::AIO extension that allows to load multiple files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::AIO::Util - Useful functions missing from IO::AIO.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Any - Open anything.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async - Asynchronous event-driven programming.
      • This module has an interesting list of modules in See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Handle - Event callbacks for a non-blocking file descriptor.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Loop - Core loop of the IO::Async framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Loop::Epoll - Use IO::Async with epoll on Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Loop::Epoll::FD - Use IO::Async with Epoll and special filehandles.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async-Loop-EV - Use IO::Async with EV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async-Loop-FutureIO - Use IO::Async with Future::IO.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Loop::Poll - Use IO::Async with poll(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async-Loop-Ppoll - Use IO::Async with ppoll(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Async-Loop-Uring - Use IO::Async with IO::Uring.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Notifier - Base class for IO::Async event objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Process - Start and manage a child process.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::SSL - Use SSL/TLS with IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Stream - Event callbacks and write buffering for a stream filehandle.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Timer - Base class for Notifiers that use timed delays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Async::Timer::Periodic - Event callback at regular intervals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-AsyncX-Sendfile - Adds support for Sys::Sendfile to IO::Async::Stream.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Barf - Barfing content to output file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Dir - Supply object methods for directory handles.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-FD - Faster accept, socket, listen with file descriptors, not handles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::File - Supply object methods for filehandles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Handle - Supply object methods for I/O handles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Interactive - Utilities for interactive I/O.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::KQueue - Perl interface to the BSD kqueue system call.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Pipe - Supply object methods for pipes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Poll - Object interface to system poll call.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Ppoll - Object interface to Linux's ppoll() call.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Seekable - Supply seek based methods for I/O objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Select - OO interface to the select system call.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Select-SSL - IO::Socket::SSL compatible IO::Select.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket - Object interface to socket communications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::INET - Object interface for AF_INET domain sockets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::INET6 - Object interface for AF_INET/AF_INET6 domain sockets - Deprecated.
      • Use IO::Socket::IP instead
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::IP - Family-neutral IP socket supporting both IPv4 and IPv6.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::Multicast - Send and receive multicast messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::SSL - SSL sockets with IO::Socket interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::Socks - Provides a way to create socks client or server both 4 and 5 version.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapped - Object wrapped by IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapper.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::Socks::Wrapper - Add SOCKS support for any perl object / package / program.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Socket::SSL - SSL sockets with IO::Socket interface.
      • Has lots of docs and sample code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Trace - Log I/O of an arbitrary process.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Uring - io_uring for Perl.
      • http://pod.tst.eu/http://cvs.schmorp.de/libev/ev.pod - http://software.schmorp.de/pkg/libev.html.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Ryu - Perl interface to Ryu (sane NV-to-string conversion).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-LibAsyncNS - A Perl wrapper around libasyncns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Promise::AsyncAwait - Async/await with promises.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Promise::ES6 - ES6-style promises in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Promise-Me - Fork Based Promise with Asynchronous Execution, Async, Await and Shared Data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Promise::XS - Fast promises in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Promises - An implementation of Promises in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Riemann-Client - A Perl client for the Riemann event system.
      • http://riemann.io/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Ryu - Asynchronous stream building blocks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Ryu-Async - Use Ryu with IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Signal::Mask - Signal masks made easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Socket - Networking constants and support functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Socket6 - IPv6 related part of the C socket.h defines and structure manipulators.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Socket::More - List network interfaces and scope passive sockets to them.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::SigAction - Perl extension for Consistent Signal Handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Defer - Execute code when leaving a block.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Timer-Wheel - Lightweight timer/event scheduler.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UV - Perl interface to libuv.
    • ExceptionHandling
      • FeaturesAndPragmas - DebuggingStuff - For Carp::Always.
      • ModuleBuilding - For Aion
      • ParallelProcessing
      • PerlInternals
      • SwitchStatements
      • TestingHelp
      • WantArray
      • https://phoenixtrap.com/2021/12/07/sweeter-perl-exception-classes/
      • https://gist.github.com/Ovid/5205534f7dcc52e4d931aaff301b39aa
      • Aspect::Guard
      • autodie::Scope::Guard
      • B::Hooks::EndOfScope - PerlInternals
      • B::Hooks::EndOfScope::XS - PerlInternals
      • End
      • ExclusiveLock::Guard
      • Guard
      • Guard::Stats
      • Guard::Timer
      • Hook::LexWrap
      • Hook::Scope
      • Object::Destroyer
      • odg
      • Perl::AtEndOfScope
      • Proc::Guard
      • ReleaseAction
      • Scope::Guard
      • Scope::local_OnExit
      • Scope::OnExit (deprecated)
      • Sub::Prepend
      • Sub::ScopeFinalizer
      • Test::Mock::Guard
      • Test2::Util::Guard
      • Value::Canary
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion-Carp - Adds stack trace to exceptions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aspect - General purpose guard object for destroy-time actions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/autodie - Wrapper class for calling subs at end of scope.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Capture::Tiny - Capture STDOUT and STDERR from Perl, XS or external programs.
      • In core, so 1st choice
      • This module has a huge See Also list
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Throwable - A minimal lightweight exception class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Clean-Eval - Run code under eval without leaking $@ and get a rich error object back on failure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Error - Error/exception handling in an OO-ish way.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Error::Pure - Perl module for structured errors.
      • See Task::Error::Pure below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Error-Pure-Output-Bio - Output bioperl subroutines for Error::Pure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Error-Show - Locate and Diagnose Errors/Exceptions in Perl programs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Base - Lightweight exceptions.
      • Has a great See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Class - A module that allows you to declare real exception classes in Perl.
      • But see Throwable & HTTP::Throwable
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Died - Convert simple die into real exception object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Reporter - A generic exception-reporting object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Exception-Reporter-Summarizer-PlackRequest - A summarizer for Plack::Request objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Exception-Sink - General purpose compact exception handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::System - The exception class for system or library calls.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Exception-ThrowUnless - It provides functions much like the standard perl functions, except that if they fail, they will die instead of returning undef.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Warning - Convert simple warn into real exception object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExclusiveLock-Guard - Lexically-scoped lock management.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Glitch - Exception Handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Guard - Safe cleanup blocks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Guard-Stats - Create guard objects and gather averall usage statistics from them.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Timer - A scope guard that keeps time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hook::LexWrap - Lexically scoped subroutine wrappers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hook::Output::File - Redirect STDOUT/STDERR to a file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hook-Output-Tiny - Easily enable/disable trapping of STDOUT/STDERR.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hook::Scope - Perl extension for adding hooks for exiting a scope.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Exception - Throw HTTP-Errors as (Exception::Class-) Exceptions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Throwable - A set of strongly-typed, PSGI-friendly HTTP 1.1 exception libraries.
      • Build on top of Throwable
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Ouch - Exceptions that don't hurt.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Syntax - Syntax Check Perl files and strings.
      • However it doesn't show any errors by design (only interested in process return code)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Proc-Guard - Process runner with RAII (Resource acquisition is initialization) pattern.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scope::Guard - Lexically-scoped resource management.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Prepend - Prepend code to named subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::ScopeFinalizer - Execute code on exiting scope.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Check - Wraps 'perl -c' so it works even if modules are unavailable.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Task::Error::Pure - Install the Error::Pure modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Throwable - A role for classes that can be thrown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Throwable::SugarFactory - Build a library of syntax-sugared Throwable-based exceptions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unexpected - Localised exception classes composed from roles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Value::Canary - Callbacks for value destruction.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/X::Tiny - Base class for a bare-bones exception factory.
    • ExtensibleProvisioningProtocol
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-pepper - A command-line EPP client.
      • Uses Net::EPP
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc5731, rfc9154
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-EPP - A Perl library for the Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP)..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::EPP::Registry::Nominet - A simple client interface to the Nominet EPP service.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::EPP::Server - Provides a high-level framework for developing Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP) servers.
    • FAQ
      • CodingStyle - CPAN.
      • SecurityPolicy - For Dist::AutomationPolicy
      • https://www.defectivebydesign.org
      • TheISOGuide - TheEUNormGuide.
      • TheLanguageGuide
      • TheOSGuide
      • TheRFCGuide
      • A copy of this file is at http://savage.net.au/misc/AI_POLICY.md - The module Safe::Hole includes a file called AI_POLICY.md. Examine that for ideas.
      • See also https://www.xda-developers.com/linux-kernel-now-allows-ai-written-code/
      • See also https://wiki.gentoo.org/wiki/Project:Council/AI_policy
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - The fundamental problem is that AI must compress reality into model-legible forms. In this setting, adversaries can exploit the compression. They donât have to attack the territory; they can attack the map. Models lack local contextual knowledge. They process symbols, not meaning. A human sees a suspicious URL; an AI sees valid syntax. And that semantic gap becomes a security gap.
      • Some are oriented to specific problems, whereas others are based on a different way of thinking. Typically, the latter are event-based, not data-based, so they react to things like start tag, end tag, etc. It all depends on how you think about your problem, and your specific requirements - There isn't one! But there are different ones for different problems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Tree::AboutObjects - For the article: "User's View of Object-Oriented Modules" - https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin/view/lib/Marlin/Manual/Beginning.pod.
      • And I have my preferences aka biases: Moo instead of Moose. Mojolicious instead of all the alternatives - My time is limited..
      • - It is semantic mimicry: adversarial instructions that resemble legitimate prompts, which trigger self-compromise.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - LLMs process token sequences, but no mechanism exists to mark token privileges. The LLM can't tell the difference between legitimate input and corrupt input, so can be tricked into giving the latter priority. This might make it unsolvable in todayâs LLMs.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - In training, we face poisoned datasets and backdoored models. In inference, we face adversarial inputs and prompt injection. During operation, we face a contaminated context and persistent compromise. We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?.
    • FeaturesAndPragmas
      • ExceptionHandling - DataTypes - For Data::OptList.
      • MathsLibraries - For e.g. bigint, bignum
      • ModuleBuilding - For e.g. Exporter::Lexical, Lexical::Import
      • NewOperators - For Syntax::Feature::QwComments
      • PerlDocumentation
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/base - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/bigfloat - Transparent big floating point number support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/bigint - Transparent big integer support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/bignum - Transparent big number support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/bigrat - Transparent big rational number support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/builtins - Install all the new builtins from the builtin namespace.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/criticism - Perl pragma to enforce coding standards and best-practices.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/diagnostics - Perl pragma to produce verbose warning diagnostics.
      • Likewise, the splain program
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/exact - Perl pseudo pragma to enable strict, warnings, features, mro, filehandle methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/exact-conf - Cascading merged application configuration extension for exact.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/experimental - Experimental features made easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/feature - Perl pragma to enable new features.
      • See https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Base for sample code
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Full::Pragmata - Common pragmata for Perl scripts and modules.
      • A must-read document
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/lazy - Lazily install missing Perl modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/lexically - Lexically import functions from non-lexical exporters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/parent - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlhist - The Perl history records.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlpolicy - Various and sundry policies and commitments related to the Perl core.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/strict - Perl pragma to restrict unsafe constructs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/strictures - Turn on strict and make most warnings fatal.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Base - For sample code - Perl pragma to activate if/given/when (via use feature 'switch';).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/syntax - Activate syntax extensions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Feature::Function - Provides a function keyword.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/warnings - Perl pragma to control optional warnings.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlop#I%2FO-Operators
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AnyEvent::Filesys::Notify - An AnyEvent compatible module to monitor files/directories for changes.
