      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-perlmv-scriptlet-prefix_with_number - Prefix filenames with number (usually to make them easily sortable).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-renwd - REName current (Working) Directory.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sdif - Contains utility programs cdif, sdif and watchdiff to post-process output of diff.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Sky - Wrapper to rsync/etc. to upload files to a remote server and give download links.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::UniqFiles - Report unique/duplicate file {names,contents}.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AWS-Lambda - Perl support for AWS Lambda Custom Runtime.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AWS-S3 - Lightweight interface to Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AWS::XRay - AWS X-Ray tracing library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Snapback2 - Routines for support of rsync-based snapshot backup.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cwd-Guard - Temporary changing working directory (chdir).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cwd-utf8 - Fully UTF-8 aware Cwd.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Env::Path - Advanced operations on path variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Fcntl - Various flag constants and helper functions from C's fcntl.h.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Access-Driver - Convenient File Access with "Batteries included".
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-BackupCopy - Create a backup copy of the file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::BaseDir - Use the Freedesktop.org base directory specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Basename - Parse file paths into directory, filename and suffix.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Basename-Extra - Extension to File::Basename, adds named access to file parts and handling of filename suffixes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-BOM-Utils - Check, Add and Remove BOMs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::ChangeNotify - Watch for changes to files, cross-platform style.
      • File::chdir gives you $CWD and @CWD, which are not global - A more sensible way to change directories.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::chdir
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Copy - Copy files or filehandles.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Copy-clonefile - Call clonefile system call.
      • MacOS only
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Copy::NoClobber - Rename copied files safely if destination exists.
      • See also File::Util::Rename below
      • Refers to contents not file names - App::UniqFiles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::DirList - Provide a sorted list of directory content.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Fetch - A generic file fetching mechanism.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Find - Traverse a directory tree. Built-in.
      • But see File::Find::Rule
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-FindRoot - Find the directory that's the root for a project.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-FindUniq - Find unique or duplicate file {contents,names}.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Find-Closures - Functions you can use with File::Find.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Find-Mason - Find files that contain Mason components.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Find-Rule - Alternative interface to File::Find.
      • But see Path::Iterator::Rule below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Find-utf8 - Fully UTF-8 aware File::Find.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Finder - Nice wrapper for File::Find ala find(1).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::FStore - Module for interacting with file stores.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::FStore::Base - Module for interacting with file stores.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::FStore::File - Module for interacting with file stores.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Glob - Perl extension for BSD glob routine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::GlobMapper - Extend File Glob to Allow Input and Output Files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::HomeDir - Find your home and other directories on any platform.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Information - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Information::Base - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Information::Filesystem - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Information::Inode - Generic module for extracting information from filesystems.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Listing - Parse a directory listing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Lockfile-Emacs - Create/check/delete Emacs-style lockfiles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::MimeInfo - Determine file type from the file name.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::MimeInfo::Applications - Find programs to open a file by mimetype.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-MimeInfo-Simple - Simple implementation to determine file type.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::MMagic - Guess file type.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-MoreUtil - File-related utilities, about 27 of them actually.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Open-NoCache-ReadOnly - Open a file and flush from memory on closing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Path - Create or remove directory trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Path::Redirect - Poor man's symbolic link path redirection.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Print::Many - Print to more than one file descriptor at once.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Process - Process text files with customer handlers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::pushd - Change directory temporarily for a limited scope.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw - Fast IO operations using direct system calls.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw-Base64 - Base64 plugin for File::Raw.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw-Gzip - Gzip / zlib / raw deflate plugin for File::Raw.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw-Hash - Cryptographic and integrity digests as a File::Raw plugin.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw-JSON - JSON / JSONL plugin for File::Raw.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Raw-Separated - CSV/TSV plugin for File::Raw.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::ReadBackwards - Read a file backwards by lines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Rotate::Backup - Make backups of multiple directories and rotate them on unix.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::RsyBak - Backup files/directories with histories, using rsync.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Save-Home - Place file safely under user home directory.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::ShareDir - Locate per-dist and per-module shared files.
      • But see the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-ShareDir-Tarball - Deal transparently with shared files distributed as tarballs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::ShareDir::Tiny - Locate per-dist and per-module shared files.
      • For the installation half of the solution, there are several options available that depend on your installation tool. For ExtUtils::MakeMaker there is File::ShareDir::Install. Other tools like Module::Build, Module::Build::Tiny and Dist::Build have built in support for sharedirs
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Slurper - A simple, sane and efficient module to slurp a file.
      • And File::Slurp is obsolete
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Slurper-Temp - File::Slurper + File::Temp.
      • And be warned, File::Temp uses global variables. See TestingHelp
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Spec - Portably perform operations on file names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Spec::Functions - Portably perform operations on file names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::stat - By-name interface to Perl's built-in stat() functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-stat-Extra - An extension of the File::stat module, provides additional methods..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-StatX - StatX for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Sticker - Read, Write file meta-data.
      • Includes sticker which sticks tags onto files, populates a database with file meta-data
      • See also Data::TagDB in DatabaseAndSQL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-FStore - Module for interacting with file stores.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Stubb - Stub file creator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Temp - Return name and handle of a temporary file safely.
      • And be warned, File::Temp uses global variables. See TestingHelp
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::URIList - Module for reading and writing RFC 2483 URI lists.
      • See RfcGuide for rfc2483
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Util::Copy - Utilities related to copying files.

      • See also File::Copy::NoClobber above

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::Which - Perl implementation of the which utility as an API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Filename::Compressed - Check whether filename indicates being compressed.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Filename-Timestamp - Extract date/timestamp from filename, if any.

      • https://cowboyprogramming.com/2007/01/05/evolve-your-heirachy/ - ArchiveFiles - for more SCS stuff.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chess-Plisco - A comprehensive chess library for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chess4p - A library for chess related functionality.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-SCS-DefParser - A Perl module to parse units contained in SII definition files (plain text variant).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Game-DijkstraMap - A numeric grid of weights plus some related functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Game::Entities - A simple entity registry for ECS designs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Game-FaceGenerator - A web app to combine random images into faces.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Game-HexDescribe - A web app to add random table driven data to map data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Game::Life - Plays Conway's Game of Life.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Game::Life::Faster - Plays John Horton Conway's Game of Life.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Game::PlatformsOfPeril - The platforms of peril.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Game-TextMapper - A web app to generate maps based on text files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Game::TextPatterns - Generate patterns of text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Games-Mahjongg-Solver-deBondt-FFI - Perl/FFI bindings to Mahjongg Solitaire Solver (after peeking) by Michiel de Bondt.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Games-ScottAdams - Perl extension for representing Scott Adams games.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Games-Solitaire-BlackHole-Solver - A solver for Black Hole Solitaire.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/KOBOLDWIZ/LibOrigin-0.1.4 - Perl extension for making Origin games.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-RCON-Minecraft - Minecraft RCON remote console.
      • https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MC/issues/MC-72390
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Poker-Eval - Deal, score, and calculate expected win rates of poker hands.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Run-WeeklyChallenge - Facilitates running a solution to https://theweeklychallenge.org using one or more sets of inputs provided as JSON command line arguments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SDL3 - Simple DirectMedia Layer 3.0.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MetaSyntactic - All Acme::MetaSyntactic themes at once.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::Maze - Mazes in the terminal.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-ARDB - Perl client for the ARC Raiders Database API (ardb.app).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-MetaForge - Perl client for MetaForge gaming APIs.
    • GenealogyStuff
      • Given a date, runs it through Genealogy::Gedcom::Date to create a DateTime object - Create a DateTime object from a Genealogy Date.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Format-Genealogy
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gedcom::Individual - A module to manipulate GEDCOM individuals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-ChroniclingAmerica - Find URLs for a given person on the Library of Congress Newspaper Records.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-FindaGrave - Find URLs on FindaGrave for a person.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Genealogy::Gedcom::Date - Parse GEDCOM dates in French r/German/Gregorian/Hebrew/Julian.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-Military-Branch - Extract military branch from free-text genealogy notes.
      • https://github.com/nigelhorne/Genealogy-Obituary-Lookup - Lookup an obituary.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-Obituary-Parser - Extract structured family relationships from obituary text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-Occupation - Normalise and translate genealogical occupation strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-Relationship - Calculate the relationship between two people.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Genealogy-Relationship-Name - Return a genealogical relationship name from step counts.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Genealogy::Wills - Lookup in a database of wills.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Genealogy-Map - Extract and map genealogical events from a GEDCOM file.
    • GeographicStuff
      • PdlLanguage - MathsLibraries.
      • SystemMonitoring
      • https://opencagedata.com/api
      • https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/geocode/json - For API v3
      • https://what3words.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-gdal - Compile GDAL, the Geographic Data Abstraction Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::libmaxminddb - Find or install libmaxminddb.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-geoip - A tool to show geographical data based on hostname or IP address(es).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-geos-af - Compile GEOS, the Geometry Engine, Open Source.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-proj - Compile the Proj library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-OFN-Address - Immutable data object for OFN (OtevÅenÃ© formÃ¡lnÃ­ normy) representation of address in the Czech Republic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-OFN-Common - OFN common data objects.
      • IP::Info - IO::IPFinder - ipfinder.io.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Address-Formatter - Take structured address data and format it according to the various global/country rules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Address-Parser - Lightweight country-aware address parser from flat text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Address-Parser-Country - Resolve a place string component to a canonical country name.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-AnomalyDetector - Detect anomalies in geospatial coordinate datasets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::BUFR - Perl extension for handling of WMO BUFR files.
      • https://wiki.met.no/bufr.pm/start
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::CheapRuler - A collection of very fast approximations to common geodesic measurements.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-Abbreviations - Quick and Dirty Interface to https://github.com/mapbox/geocoder-abbreviations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::All - This is wrapper for other geocoder cpan modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::Bing - Geocode addresses with the Bing Maps API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-CA - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://geocoder.ca for both Canada and the US.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-Free - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using free databases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-GeoApify - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://www.geoapify.com/maps-api/.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-GeocodeFarm - Geocode addresses with the GeocodeFarm API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::Google::V3 - Google Maps Geocoding API V3.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3 - Google Places Geocoding API V3.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::List - Call many Geo-Coders.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::Many - Module to tie together multiple Geo::Coder::* modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-OpenCage - Geocode coordinates and addresses with the OpenCage Geocoding API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::OSM - Geocode addresses with the OpenStreetMap Nominatim API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::Ovi - Geocode addresses with the Ovi Maps API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-Mapbox - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://mapbox.com.
      • https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/
      • Based on Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3 - Google Places Geocoding API V3
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces::V3
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Coder::TomTom - Geocode addresses with the TomTom Map Toolkit API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-US-Census - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality for the US using https://geocoding.geo.census.gov.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coder-XYZ - Provides a Geo-Coding functionality using https://geocode.xyz.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Converter::dms2dd - Parse and convert values in Degrees/Minutes/Seconds formats to decimal degrees.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Coordinates-DecimalDegrees - Convert between degrees/minutes/seconds and decimal degrees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::GDAL::FFI - A foreign function interface to GDAL.
      • See also https://gdal.org/en/stable/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-GeoNames - Perform geographical queries using GeoNames Web Services .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Geos - Perl interface for GEOS C++ library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Geos::Algorithm - Fundamental computational geometry algorithms.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Geos::Coordinate - The lightweight class used to store coordinates.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Geos::Envelope - Defines a rectangular region of the 2D coordinate plane.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Geos::Index::STRtree - A spatial index structure which uses the Sort-Tile-Recursive (STR) algorithm.
      • See also Tree::R, Tree::STR
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-H3 - H3 Geospatial Hexagon Indexing System.
      • https://github.com/uber/h3
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-H3-FFI - Perl FFI binding to H3 v3.x library functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Inverse - Calculate geographic distance from a latitude and longitude pair.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::IPinfo - The official Perl library for IPinfo.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-IP2Location - Look up of country etc (24 fields) using IP address. It supports both IPv4 and IPv6 addressing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-IP2Proxy - Reverse search of IP addresses to detect VPN servers, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-IPfree - Geo::IPfree - Look up the country of an IPv4 address.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Leaflet - Generates a Leaflet JavaScript map web page.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::LibProj::cs2cs - IPC interface to PROJ cs2cs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Local-Server - Returns the configured coordinates of the local server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Location-IP - Map IP addresses to location information.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Location-Point - Location information.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Location-TimeZoneFinder - Map geographic coordinates to time zone names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-IPinfo - The official Perl library for IPinfo.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::Point - A point on the globe.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Proj4 - PROJ cartographic projections library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Point - Base class for 2-dimensional points on the earth surface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-Spline - Calculate geographic locations between GPS fixes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::StreetAddress::US - Perl extension for parsing US street addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Geo::WeatherNWS - A simple way to get current weather data from the NWS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-WebService-OpenCellID - Perl API for the opencellid.org database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geo-What3Words - Turn WGS84 coordinates into three word addresses and vice-versa using what3words.com HTTPS API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Geography-States-Borders - Return the borders of states and provinces.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GIS::Distance - Calculate geographic distances.
      • Contains GIS::Distance::Haversine etc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/GIS::Distance::Fast - C implementation of GIS::Distance formulas.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Google-GeoCoder-Smart - Simple Google Geocoding API client.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::GoogleMaps::V3 - A simple wrapper around the Google Maps API.
      • See also Geo::Coder::GooglePlaces - Google Maps Geocoding API
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-OSM - A module to generate an interactive OpenStreetMap with customizable coordinates and zoom level.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IBGE-Municipios - Interface para cÃ³digos de municÃ­pios do IBGE.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IP::Geolocation::MMDB - Read MaxMind DB files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-API-Nominatim - Perl client for local or public OpenStreetMap Nominatim Geocoding service. Open source, unrestricted and free.
      • The Starlink AST library provides facilities for transforming coordinates from one system to another in an object oriented manner - Interface to the Starlink AST library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Starlink::AST
      • See also http://starlink.eao.hawaii.edu/starlink/AST
    • GitStuff
      • https://libgit2.org/ - UsernamePassword - For GitHub::Config::SSH::UserData.
      • https://www.addictivetips.com/ubuntu-linux-tips/best-github-clients-for-linux/
      • https://github.com/bayashi/Duadua/compare/0.35...0.36 - Compare 2 versions of a module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Build - Build external dependencies for use in CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::Build::Plugin::Download::GitHub - Alien::Build plugin to download from GitHub.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Libgit2 - Find or build libgit2, the linkable Git library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-GHGen - Comprehensive GitHub Actions workflow generator, analyzer, and optimizer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ghmulti - Helps when using multiple Github accounts with SSH keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-git_log_ch_usrdata - Change username and email address in git log.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::GitGot - A tool to make it easier to manage multiple git repositories.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Git-Autofixup - Create fixup commits for topic branches.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Git-Perl - Adds custom git command "git perl" to make it easier to monitor latest changes in perl modules, and make you collaborate faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::GitHubUtils - Utilities related to GitHub.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-GitKtti - Git flow helper scripts for safe branch management.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-GitUtils - Day-to-day command-line utilities for git.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Workflow-Lint - Linter for GitHub Actions workflow files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Bunch - Manage gitbunch directory (directory which contain git repos).
      • But see http://joeyh.name/code/mr/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Grouper - Categorize git repositories into one/more groups and perform actions on them.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Hooks - Framework for implementing Git (and Gerrit) hooks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Libgit2 - Low-level FFI bindings to libgit2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Native - Native Git for Perl via libgit2 (FFI, no fork/exec).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-RemoteURL-Parse - Parse and classify Git remote URLs (GitHub, GitLab).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Repository - Perl interface to Git repositories.
      • But see Git::Native
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Git-Server - Secure Git Server with more granular hooks capabilities than default git.
      • Similar purpose and functionality to Git::Hooks but more powerful with less dependencies
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Git::Wrapper - Wrap git(7) command-line interface.
      • But see Git::Native
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GitHub-Actions - Work in GitHub Actions using native Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GitHub-Crud - Create, Read, Update, Delete files, commits, issues, and web hooks on GitHub.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GitHub-RSS - Collect data from Github.com for feeding into RSS.
      • https://github.com/Ovid/git-workflow - Explains our company's git-workflow code.
      • https://ovid.github.io/articles/easy-git-workflow.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Group::Git - Base module for group of git repository operations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parse-Gitignore - Parse .gitignore files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pithub - A Perl interface to github.
      • Sample usage: https://perladvent.org/2022/2022-12-03.html

    • Graphs
      • [[DFA]] - ChartingAndPlotting.
      • ParallelProcessing - For Sub::Genius
      • https://gephi.org
      • http://graphml.graphdrawing.org/ - For GraphML
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petri_net
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Graphs::Reachable::Tiny - Compute reachable nodes in a graph.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Hopen - A dataflow library with first-class edges.
      • It is the underlying engine of the App::hopen cross-platform software build generator, but can be used for any dataflow task that can be represented as a directed acyclic graph (DAG)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph - Graph data structures and algorithms.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Directed - Directed graphs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Easy - Convert or render graphs (as ASCII, HTML, SVG or via Graphviz).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Easy::As_svg - Output a Graph::Easy as Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph-Easy-Introspect - Introspection and AST for Graph::Easy layouts.
      • Every rule is evaluated for each vertex in a graph and, if a match is found, an action associated with the rule is executed - A Perl implementation of a graph rewriting method (a.k.a. graph grammar).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph-Kruskal - Kruskal's Algorithm for Minimal Spanning Trees in Graphs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Layout::Aesthetic - A module for laying out graphs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Layout::Aesthetic::Topology - Graph topology for use by Graph::Layout::Aesthetic.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::MaxFlow - Compute maximum flow between 2 vertices in a graph.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::MoreUtils - Utilities for graphs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph-Nauty - Perl bindings for Nauty.
      • https://pallini.di.uniroma1.it/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph-PetriNet - Perl extension for Petri Nets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graph::Undirected - Undirected graphs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Graph-VF2 - VF2 subgraph isomorphism detection method for Perl Graph.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GraphViz2 - A wrapper for AT&T's Graphviz.
      • https://github.com/graphviz-perl
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Graphics::Libplot - Part of GNU plotutils.
      • Docs: https://www.gnu.org/software/plotutils/
      • sudo apt-get install plotutils
      • Perl interface Graphics::Libplot - Perl extension for libplot plotting library
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SVG - Perl extension for generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Svg-Simple - Write Scalar Vector Graphics using Perl syntax.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SVG-TT-Graph - Base module for generating SVG graphics.

      • [[IoT]] - [[Acronyms]] - For S.M.A.R.T..

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Asm-X86 - List of instructions and registers of x86-compatible processors, validating and converting instructions and memory references.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/BACnet - High-level interface for BACnet device communication and COV subscriptions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Power-Inspection - Power Equipment Fault Inspection and Troubleshooting Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Device-AVR-UPDI - Interact with an AVR microcontroller over UPDI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Device::Chip::MAX7219Panel - Chip driver for a panel of MAX7219 modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Device-Chip-SSD1306 - Chip driver for monochrome OLED modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Device-Firmata - Module for controlling Firmata devices.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Device-GPIB - Access a variety of generic and specific GPIB devices.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Device-Serial-SLuRM - Communicate the SLÂµRM protocol over a serial port .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Disk::SmartTools - Provide tools to work with disks via S.M.A.R.T..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MikroTik-Client - Non-blocking interface to MikroTik API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Async::ArtNet - Use Art-Net with IO::Async.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art-Net
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ - Perl module for MQTT Protocol (http://mqtt.org/).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::MQTT::Simple - Minimal MQTT version 3 interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OPCUA-Open62541 - Perl XS wrapper for open62541 OPC UA library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Physics-Ellipsometry-VASE - Variable Angle Spectroscopic Ellipsometry data fitting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Power::Outlet - Control and query network attached power outlets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Speak - Convert text to speech using Google's engine and play it on speakers.
    • HashHandling
      • ConstantStuff - ArrayHandling.
      • DataTypes
      • DataTraversal
      • Hash::Util - Built-in to Perl
      • [[HTTPHandling]]
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Expand - Convert flat hash to nested data using TT2's dot convention.
      • Module's See Also is useful
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Hash-Diff-Smart - Smart structural diff for Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Hash-Patch-Smart - Apply structural, wildcard, and array-aware patches to Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HashMap - Fast type-specialized hash maps implemented in C.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HashMap-Shared - Type-specialized shared-memory hash maps for multiprocess access.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::MultiValuedHash - Hash whose keys have multiple ordered values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Case - Base class for hashes with key-casing requirements.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Flatten - Flatten/unflatten complex data hashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Match - Match contents of a hash against rules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Merge - Merges arbitrarily deep hashes into a single hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Merge::Simple - Recursively merge two or more hashes, simply.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::MultiValue - Store multiple values per key.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Ordered - A fast, pure-Perl ordered hash class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Util - A selection of general-utility hash subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Util-Exists-Tiny - Some hash helper functions related to perl's "exists" function..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Util::FieldHash - Support for Inside-Out Classes. Built-in.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Util::FieldHash::Compat - Use Hash::Util::FieldHash or ties, depending on availability.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Util-Merge - Utility functions for merging hashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Util-Pick - The non-destructive utilities for picking hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Util-Set - Set operations on hash keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hash-Util-Set-XS - XS implementation of Hash::Util::Set.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Wrap - Create on-the-fly objects from hashes .
      • The pod's SEE-ALSO has a great list of alternatives
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Rollup - Translate an HTTP query string to a hierarchical structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pivot::ArrayOfHashes - Pivot arrays of hashes, such as those returned by DBI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Array::AsHash - Tie arrays as hashes by splitting lines on separator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-Hash-DBD - Tie a plain array to a database table.
      • Contains Tie::Hash::DBD
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-DBI - Tie hashes to DBI relational databases.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash - Base class definitions for tied hashes. Built-in.
      • Contains Tie::StdHash, Tie::ExtraHash
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::Abbrev - A hash which can be accessed using abbreviated keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::Abbrev::Smart - A hash which can be accessed using abbreviated keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::Array - A hash which is internally implemented as a sorted array.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::Create - Extend Tie::ExtraHash, enables the tie-object to reflect the tied array.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::Indexed - Ordered hashes for Perl.
      • It generally fails tests on Perl 5.18 and above due to the hash randomization change
      • See https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Ordered#SEE-ALSO
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-Hash-KeysMask - Control key aliasing by mask function, e.g. omit case of character distinction.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::MultiValue - Store multiple values per key.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::Hash::MultiValueOpts - Store multiple values per option.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::InsertOrderHash - Insert-order-preserving tied hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::IxHash - Ordered associative arrays for Perl.
      • Slow. See https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Ordered#SEE-ALSO
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::LLHash - Ordered hashes (quite slow).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tie-OrderedHash - Ordered Hashes with a public C ABI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::RefHash - Use references as hash keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::RefHash::Weak - A Tie::RefHash subclass with weakened references in the keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tie::StoredOrderHash - Ordered associative arrays for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Util::H2O - Convert a hash into an object with methods.
      • https://perladvent.org/2023/2023-12-22.html (reads config files, generates accessors)
      • https://perladvent.org/2022/2022-12-06.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Util-H2O-More - Convenience utilities built on Util::H2O (baptise, d2o, INI/YAML/HTTP helpers, Getopt helpers).
    • HealthMatters
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ACME-2026 - Checklists for glorious 2026 goals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-MineralUtils - Utilities related to mineral supplements.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sah-SchemaBundle-Nutrient - Sah schemas related to nutrients.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TableData-Health-Nutrient - Nutrients database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Search-PharmGKB - Search and retrieve information from the PharmGKB database.
    • HTMLHandling
      • DataTraversal - CodeTidyingCode.
      • DBIx::HTML
      • Form::Sensible
      • Form::Factory
      • Form::Toolkit
      • Form::Diva
      • HTML::Form
      • HTML::Form::Fu
      • HTML::Parser
      • HTML::Selector::XPath
      • HTML::Strip
      • HTML::TableContentParser
      • [[HTTPHandling]]
      • Mojo::DOM58
      • Mojo::Template
      • OpenGraphProtocol - For MooX::Role::SEOTags
      • Web::Query
      • Spreadsheet::HTML
      • StringStuff - For String::Tagged::*
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • XML::LibXML
      • https://datatables.net
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Litavis
      • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • https://www.w3.org/TR/REC-html40/sgml/entities.html
      • https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/ - The HTML Living Standard.
      • https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/common-microsyntaxes.html
      • See also http://savage.net.au/misc/unicode.html, on my site, for some common cases, special cases, and PÄ«nyÄ«n (Pinyin) vowels for Chinese - https://tools.w3cub.com/html-entities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-htidx - Generate static HTML directory listings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CGI-FormMagick - Easily create CGI form-based applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Crayon - Dedupe, minify and extend CSS.
      • But see Litavis below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::TableReader::Decoder::HTML - Access the tables of an HTML document.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Form-Tiny - Input validator centered around Type::Tiny.
      • https://brtastic.xyz/blog/article/perl-form-tiny
      • Generate nested HTML (HTML4, XHTML and HTML5) tags with custom indentation, custom encoding and automatic attribute value rotation - Just another HTML tag generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::AutoTag
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Defang - Cleans HTML as well as CSS of scripting and other executable contents, and neutralises XSS attacks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Detoxifier - Practical module to strip harmful HTML.
      • Can be used by Spreadsheet::HTML. See SpreadsheetStuff - Display HTML locally in a browser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::DOMbo - Convert between XML::DOM and {XML/HTML}::Element trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::ElementTable - Perl extension for manipulating a table composed of HTML::Element style components.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Entities - Encode or decode strings with HTML entities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Entities-Numbered - Conversion of numbered HTML entities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Escape - Faster than HTML::Entities because it does less.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-FromMail - Base-class for the HTML producers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-GenerateUtil - Routines useful when generating HTML output.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::HTMLDoc - Perl interface to the htmldoc program for producing PDF Files from HTML content.
      • For htmldoc see https://www.msweet.org/htmldoc/
      • HTMLDOC converts HTML and Markdown source files or web pages to EPUB, PostScript, or PDF files with an optional table of contents
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Object - HTML Parser, Modifier and Query Interface.
      • This module is yet another HTML parser, manipulation and query interface, but probably the most comprehensive one
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Parser - HTML parser class.
      • Contains HTML::Entities
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::ParseBrowser - Simple interface for User-Agent string parsing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::PopupTreeSelect - HTML popup tree widget.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-RewriteAttributes - Concise attribute rewriting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Scrubber - Perl extension for scrubbing/sanitizing HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Similarity - Calculate the structural similarity between two HTML documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-StripScripts - Strip scripting constructs out of HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Tabulate - HTML table rendering class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Tagset - Data tables useful in parsing HTML.
      • These hashes hold info per HTML tag about tags
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Tidy - (X)HTML validation in a Perl object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Tiny - Lightweight, dependency free HTML/XML generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Tree - Build and scan parse-trees of HTML.
      • Contains various articles
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::TreeBuilder - HTML::TreeBuilder - Parser that builds a HTML syntax tree.
      • Can be slow for large docs, hence the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::TreeBuilder::LibXML - HTML::TreeBuilder and XPath compatible interface with libxml.
      • HTML::TreeBuilder::LibXML is drop-in-replacement for HTML::TreeBuilder::XPath
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::TreeStructured - Perl extension for generating tree structured HTML- Has minimal documentation.
      • But see HTML::PopupTreeSelect
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::T5 - HTML validation in a Perl object.
      • This is a replacement for HTML::Lint
      • Requires Alien::TidyHTML5, which provides the C html-tidy library or ascertains its presence
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Valid - Tidy/validate HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Zoom - Selector based streaming template engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML5-DOM - Super fast html5 DOM library with css selectors (based on Modest/MyHTML).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Litavis - CSS preprocessor and compiler implemented in C via XS.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MathML-Entities - Convert XHTML+MathML Named Entities to Numeric Character References.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Rose-HTML-Objects - Object-oriented interfaces for HTML..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-HTML-Container - There are many modules in this series. See e.g. UsernamePassword.
      • And Tags::HTML::Page, Tags::HTML::Element etc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-HTML-Footer - Tags helper for HTML footer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-HTML-Messages - Tags helper for HTML messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::CleanFragment - Clean up text to use as URL fragment or filename.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Table-HTML - Generate HTML table.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Table::HTML::DataTables - Generate HTML table with jQuery and DataTables plugin.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::ClickSource - Determine the source of a visit on your website : organic, adwords, facebook, referer site.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XAO-Base - Set of base classes for the XAO family of products.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XAO::FS - XAO framework database layer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XAO-Web - XAO Web Developer, dynamic content building suite.
      • https://ejelta.com/xao/

      • ChildProcesses - BrowserStuff.

      • SystemMonitoring
      • WebServices
      • http://blogs.perl.org/users/neilb/2011/10/cpan-modules-for-parsing-user-agent-strings.html
      • https://daniel.haxx.se/http2/
      • https://data-star.dev/
      • https://dev.to/fgasper/perling-and-curling-2i10
      • https://http2.github.io/
      • https://http3-explained.haxx.se/
      • https://nghttp2.org/
      • https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml
      • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-nghttp2 - Find or build the nghttp2 HTTP/2 C library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Authen-WebAuthn - A library to add Web Authentication support to server applications.
      • This module lets you validate Web Authentication registration and authentication responses
      • https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-Stripe-Webhook - A Perl module for handling webhooks sent by Stripe.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Ajax - A perl-specific system for writing Asynchronous web applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Expand - Convert flat hash to nested data using TT2's dot convention.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Info - Information about the CGI environment.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Session - Persistent session data in CGI applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Session::Driver::mariadb - CGI::Session driver for MariaDB database.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Simple - A Simple totally OO CGI interface that is CGI.pm compliant.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cookie - Cookie Object with Encryption or Signature.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Cookie::Jar - Cookie Jar Class for Server & Client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cookies-Roundtrip - Convert between different HTTP Cookie formats, well, at least we tried!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::URIID - Extractor for identifiers from URIs.
      • See also File::FStore in FileHandling
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::URIID::Base - Extractor for identifiers from URIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Datastar::SSE - Module for creating Datastar Server Events.
      • Parses the User-Agent string - Detect User-Agent, do up again!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Duadua
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FU - Framework Ultimatum: A Lean and Efficient Zero-Dependency Web Framework..
      • The Firefox extension "Tamper Data" is an effective tool for examining the headers of the requests to the server
      • https://github.com/WICG/ua-client-hints/blob/main/README.md - HTTP::UserAgentClientHints.
      • This repository hosts the User Agent Client Hints (UA-CH) specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Body - HTTP Body Parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-BrowserDetect - Determine Web browser, version, and platform from an HTTP user agent string.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Cookies - HTTP cookie jars.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Cookies-Chrome - Cookie storage and management for Google Chrome.
      • Uses HTTP::Cookies
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::CookieJar::LWP - LWP adapter for HTTP::CookieJar.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Cookies::Microsoft - Access to Microsoft cookies files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Cookies::Mozilla - Cookie storage and management for Mozilla.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Cookies::Netscape - Access to Netscape cookies files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::DetectUserAgent - Yet another HTTP useragent string parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::DAV - A WebDAV client library for Perl5.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Headers - Class encapsulating HTTP Message headers.
      • But see HTTP::XSHeaders
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Message - HTTP style message (base class).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Parser-XS - A fast, primitive HTTP request parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Parser2-XS - Yet another http parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Promise - Asynchronous HTTP Request and Promise.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Promise::Stream::Brotli - Stream Encoder for Brotli Encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Request::Common - Construct common HTTP::Request objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Request-FromCurl - Create a HTTP::Request from a curl command line.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Request-Webpush - HTTP Request for web push notifications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Response - HTTP style response message.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-SecureHeaders - Manage security headers with many safe defaults.
      • https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Headers_Cheat_Sheet.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Session2 - HTTP session management - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::State - Fast RFC6265bis-draft + CHIPS Cookie Jar.
      • See RfcGuide for rfc6265
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Status - HTTP Status code processing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::StructuredFieldValues - Encode and decode HTTP Structured Field Values (RFC 9651) in Perl.
      • See RfcGuide for rfc9651
      • Uses IO::Socket::IP (or IO::Socket::INET) - A small, simple, correct HTTP/1.1 client.
      • Uses HTTP::CookieJar
      • Eschew HTTP::Lite
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Tiny
      • Contains interesting See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Tinyish - HTTP::Tinyish is a wrapper module for HTTP client modules LWP, HTTP::Tiny and HTTP client software curl and wget.
      • Backends are searched in the order of: LWP, HTTP::Tiny, curl and wget
      • HTTP::Tinyish will auto-detect if the backend also supports HTTPS, and use the appropriate backend based on the given URL to the request methods
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Tiny::Mech - Wrap a WWW::Mechanize instance in an HTTP::Tiny compatible interface..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Tiny::UA - Higher-level UA features for HTTP::Tiny.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-XSHeaders - Fast XS Header library, replacing HTTP::Headers and HTTP::Headers::Fast.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LightTCP-Server - A configurable TCP server with HTTP, CGI, and threading support (Pure Perl OOP).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LightTCP-SSLclient - SSL/TLS HTTP client with proxy support and certificate pinning.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LWP-Protocol-https - Provide https support for LWP::UserAgent.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LWP::Protocol::Net::Curl - The power of libcurl in the palm of your hands!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/libwww-perl - REST::Client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LWP-UserAgent-Throttled - Throttle requests to a site.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Amazon-Glacier - An implementation of the full Amazon Glacier RESTful 2012-06-01 API..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Amazon::Signature::V4 - Implements the Amazon Web Services signature version 4, AWS4-HMAC-SHA256.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Curl - Perl interface for libcurl.
      • Contains Net::Curl::Easy, but see Net::Curl::Easier
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Curl-Easier - Convenience wrapper around Net::Curl::Easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Curl::Easier - Perl interface for curl_easy_* functions.
      • But see Net::Curl::Easier
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Firewall-BlockerHelper - Helps with managing firewalls for banning IPs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PApp - Multi-page-state-preserving web applications.
      • Expects you to use modperl or similar
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::HTTP::UserAgent - Parse User-Agent strings - Deprecated.
      • Use HTTP::BrowserDetect instead
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Protocol-Tus - Tus protocol handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Query-Param - Lightweight object interface for parsing and creating query strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Router::Boom - Fast routing engine for web applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Router::R3 - URL router library with high performance.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Router::Ragel - A high-performance URL router using the Ragel finite state machine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Router-Simple - Simple HTTP router.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Router::XS - Fast URI path to value lookup.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Slug - XS URL slug generator with custom ops.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Squid-Guard - Redirector for the Squid web proxy.
      • Requires Plack - Lightweight Web Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Terse
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI - Uniform Resource Identifiers (absolute and relative).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI - Encodes Unicode string in Punycode.
      • See also Net::IDN::Encode in DnsStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::AnyService - Simple /etc/services-driven URI objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Based - Define a base URI and then generate variations on it.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Escape - Percent-encode and percent-decode unsafe characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Escape::XS - Drop-In replacement for URI::Escape.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Router - Highest performance powerful URI router (URI path to value lookup) for HTTP frameworks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Shortener - Shorten URIs so that you don't have to rely on external services.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::Split - Parse and compose URI strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/URI-Template - Object for handling URI templates (RFC 6570).
      • See RfcGuide for rfc6570
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URL::Encode - Encoding and decoding of application/x-www-form-urlencoded encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URL::Encode::XS - XS implementation of URL::Encode.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/VAPID - Voluntary Application Server Identification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Web-Components - MVC pattern for Web::Simple.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Web::ComposableRequest - Composable request class for web frameworks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Web-Simple - A quick and easy way to build simple web applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Chain - A web request chain.
      • You must, after every action that GETs or POSTs a page, check that Mech succeeded, or all bets are off - https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Mechanize/view/lib/WWW/Mechanize/FAQ.pod.
    • HTTPServers
      • [[HTTPHandling]] - EventStuff.
      • LinguaStuff - For CGI::Lingua
      • ParallelProcessing - For IO::Async
      • PlackServer -For what came before PAGI
      • http://nginx.org/en/docs/
      • https://github.com/h2o/picohttpparser
      • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amon2 - Lightweight web application framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amon2-Plugin-Web-CSRFDefender - Anti CSRF filter - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Apertur-SDK - Official Perl SDK for the Apertur API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::HTTPThis - Export the current directory over HTTP.
      • Actually, this is just a very thin wrapper around Plack::App::DirectoryIndex
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Async-Microservice - Async HTTP Microservice Moose Role.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CGI-ACL - Decide whether to allow a client to run this script.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CGI-Alternatives - Documentation for alternative solutions to CGI.pm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ClearPress - Simple, vaguely-MVC web application framework.
      • http://clearpress.net/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Furl - Lightning-fast URL fetcher.
      • Furl is a replacement for LWP
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Gears - A framework to build web frameworks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::CryptoCookie - Perl extension for encrypted HTTP cookies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTTP-Handy - A tiny HTTP/1.0 server for Perl 5.5.3 and later.
      • See also HP::Handy
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::ServerEvent - Create strings for HTTP Server Sent Events.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Server::Simple - Lightweight HTTP server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTTP::Server::Simple::CGI - CGI.pm-style version of HTTP::Server::Simple.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hypersonic - Blazing fast HTTP server with JIT-compiled C event loop.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Minima - Efficient web framework built with modern core classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mojolicious - Real-time web framework.
      • Mojo::Base::Tiny
      • Data::Object
      • FlightRecorder
      • Mojo::UserAgent::Cached
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Monoceros - PSGI/Plack server with event driven connection manager, preforking workers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-HTTP - Use HTTP with IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-HTTP-Server - Serve HTTP with IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-MCP-Server - Async MCP server base class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-HTTP - Low-level HTTP connection (client).
      • Contains Net::HTTPS
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::HTTP::API - Easily create client for net API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::HTTP::NB - Non-blocking HTTP client.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::HTTP2 - HTTP/2 in Perl, simplified.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-HTTP2-nghttp2 - Perl XS bindings for nghttp2 HTTP/2 library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Respite - Representational State (Perl Iscool) Transfer Engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Server - Extensible, general Perl server engine.
      • Has a massive amount of flexibility
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetServer-Generic - Generic TCP/IP server class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Server::HTTP - Very basic Net::Server based HTTP server class.
      • See also Net::Server::Fork, Net::Server::INET, Net::Server::PreFork, Net::Server::PreForkSimple, Net::Server::MultiType, Net::Server::Single Net::Server::SIG Net::Server::Daemonize Net::Server::Proto
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Server::PreFork - Net::Server personality.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Async::MCP - A hot-deployable variant of Net::Server::PreFork.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAGI - Perl Asynchronous Gateway Interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAGI::Middleware - Base class for PAGI middleware.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAGI::Middleware::AccessLog - Request logging middleware.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAGI::Middleware::Builder - DSL for composing PAGI middleware.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PAGI::Middleware::Session::Store::Cookie - Encrypted client-side session store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAGI-Server - PAGI Reference Server Implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAGI/view/lib/PAGI/Tutorial.pod - A comprehensive guide to building async web applications with PAGI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Protocol-HTTP - Very fast HTTP protocol incremental parser and serializer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Protocol::HTTP::Compression::Brotli - Brotli compression plugin for Protocol::HTTP.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Protocol-HTTP - HTTP response class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Protocol::HTTP2 - HTTP/2 protocol implementation (RFC 7540).
      • See RfcGide to rfc7540
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Shuvgey - AnyEvent HTTP/2 (RFC 7540) Server for PSGI.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc7540
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PSGI - Perl Web Server Gateway Interface Specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Server::Starter - A superdaemon for hot-deploying server programs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Session::Storage::Secure - Encrypted, expiring, compressed, serialized session data with integrity.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Starlet - A simple, high-performance PSGI/Plack HTTP server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Starman - High-performance preforking PSGI/Plack web server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-WebDyne-Apache - Prerequisite modules for running WebDyne under Apache.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-WebDyne-PAGI - Install PAGI dependencies for WebDyne.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-WebDyne-Plack - Optional Plack integration for WebDyne.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-WebDyne-Starman - Optional Starman integration for WebDyne.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thunderhorse - A no-compromises brutally-good web framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Trickster - A modern, battle-tested micro-framework for Perl web applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Twiggy - AnyEvent HTTP server for PSGI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/UniEvent-HTTP-Manager - Extremely fast asynchronous preforking / threading event based web server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebDyne - Dynamic Perl web application framework with template compilation and modular design.
    • HumanRights
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-LegalRepData - Access to HUDOC Database.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ECHR-OpenData - Access to HUDOC Database.
    • IoLayers
      • CryptoStuff - ArchiveFiles.
      • binmode $fh, ':raw'; - open my $fh, '<', $filename;.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Divert - Divert STDOUT to capture and process output.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::Pty - Pseudo TTY object class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Stty - Change and print terminal line settings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IO-Tty - Low-level allocate a pseudo-Tty, import constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IOLayer-QuotedPrint - PerlIO layer for quoted-printable strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::gzip - Perl extension to provide a PerlIO layer to gzip/gunzip.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::Via::Base64 - PerlIO layer for base64 (MIME) encoded strings.
      • Beware the capital 'V' in the name, unlike the following, which use 'v'
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlIO-via-Include - PerlIO layer for including other files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::LineNumber - PerlIO layer for prefixing line numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::MD5 - PerlIO layer for creating an MD5 digest of a file.
      • Uses Digest::MD5
      • Handles b64digest, digest and hexdigest
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlIO-via-Pod - PerlIO layer for extracting plain old documentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::QuotedPrint - PerlIO layer for quoted-printable strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::Rotate - PerlIO layer for encoding using rotational deviation (rot13 etc).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::StripHTML - PerlIO layer to strip HTML tags from an input file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::UnComment - PerlIO layer for removing comments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::UnPod - PerlIO layer for removing plain old documentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlIO::via::xz - PerlIO layer for XZ (de)compression.
      • Uses IO::Compress::Xz and IO::Uncompress::UnXz
    • JavaScript
      • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Regular_expressions - JsonStuff.
      • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Using_promises - For a good intro to promises
      • https://libuv.org/
      • https://promisesaplus.com/ - For Promises/A+
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JE - Pure-Perl ECMAScript (JavaScript) Engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JavaScript::Duktape - Perl interface to Duktape embeddable javascript engine.
      • See also https://duktape.org/api.html and https://duktape.org/guide
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JavaScript::Minifier - Perl extension for minifying JavaScript code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JavaScript::Minifier::XS - XS based JavaScript minifier.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JavaScript-Packer - Perl version of Dean Edwards' Packer.js.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JavaScript-QuickJS - Run JavaScript via QuickJS in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JavaScript::SpiderMonkey - Perl interface to the JavaScript Engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JavaScript::V8::XS - Perl XS binding for the V8 Javascript embeddable engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JQ-Lite - A lightweight jq-like JSON query engine in Perl.
      • Sample code
    • JsonStuff
      • DataTraversal - CryptoStuff.
      • FileHandling - For File::Raw::JSON
      • JavaScript
      • OrgDocHandling
      • TheRFCGuide
      • YamlStuff
      • CSS.and.Javascript.html - for jq and JSON::JQ
      • https://github.com/json-schema/JSON-Schema-Test-Suite
      • https://jsonlines.org/
      • https://json-model.org/#/
      • https://json-schema.org
      • https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/reference
      • https://json-structure.org/
      • http://jwt.io/
      • http://self-issued.info/docs/draft-jones-json-web-token-06.html
      • http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-zyp-json-schema
      • https://www.json.org/json-en.html
      • https://www.relaxedjson.org/specification
      • http://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html - For Mojo::JSON
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::JWT - JWT utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-jsonvalidate - App harness for the jsonvalidate CLI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CBOR::Free - Fast CBOR for everyone.
      • See RfcGuide for rfc7049
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CBOR-XS - Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR, RFC7049).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-JSON_Object - Role for Class::JSON_Object.
      • Marc Lehmann has (long ago?) said bad things about this module. See the POD for JSON::SIMD, under Interoperability as above - Recommended despite the old comments below.
      • It can be necessary. See ~/bin/test.cpanel.json.xs.type.pl
      • Code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Ref-JSON - Provide Perl reference code to access values in an arbitrary JSON data structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-API - Module to interact with a JSON API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Create - Encodes Perl variables into JSON.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::GRDDL - Transform JSON to RDF.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Hyper - Extract links from JSON via a schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-JsonModel - Perl Runtime for JSON Model.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Lines - Parse JSONLines with perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-LINQ - LINQ-style query interface for JSON and JSONL files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::MaybeXS - Use Cpanel::JSON::XS with a fallback to JSON::XS and JSON::PP.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Path - Implements JSONPath, an XPath-like language for searching JSON-like structures.
      • JSONPath is described at http://goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Pointer - A Perl implementation of JSON Pointer (RFC6901).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-PP - JSON::XS compatible pure-Perl module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Relaxed - An extension of JSON that allows for better human-readability.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::RPC - JSON RPC 2.0 Server Implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::RPC::Common - Transport agnostic JSON RPC helper objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::RPC::Dispatcher - A JSON-RPC 2.0 server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::RPC::Simple - Simple JSON-RPC client and dispatcher (WD 1.1 subset only currently).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::RPC::Simple::Lite - A simple and lite JSON-RPC client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-RPC-Spec - Yet another JSON-RPC 2.0 Implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-RPC2 - Transport-independent implementation of JSON-RPC 2.0.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Schema - Validate JSON against a schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-AsType - Generates Type::Tiny types out of JSON schemas.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-Generate - Generate JSON Schemas from data!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-Modern - Validate data against a schema using a JSON Schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-Tiny - Validate data against a schema, minimally.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-Types - Create Type::Tiny types defined by JSON Schemas.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Schema-Validate - Lean, recursion-safe JSON Schema validator (Draft 2020-12).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-SIMD - JSON serialising/deserialising, done correctly and faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Structure - JSON Structure validation library for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Syck - JSON is YAML (but consider using JSON::XS instead!).
      • See also YAML::Syck
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::T - Transform JSON using JsonT.
      • http://goessner.net/articles/jsont/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Tiny - Minimalistic JSON. No dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-ToHTML - Render JSON-based Perl datastructures as HTML tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Typist - Replace mushy strings and numbers with rigidly typed replacements.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::Validator - Validate data against a JSON schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSON::WebToken - JSON Web Token (JWT) implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-XS - JSON serialising/deserialising, done correctly and fast.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-YY - Fast JSON encoder/decoder with document manipulation API, backed by yyjson.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSONL-Subset - Extract a percentage of lines from a JSONL file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSV - A perl implementation of JSON Schema (draft-04) validator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JSV::Validator - A perl implementation of JSON Schema (draft-04) validator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/mb::JSON - A simple JSON parser for multibyte string.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/RPC::JSON - JSON-RPC Client Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Schema-Validator - Tools for validating and loading Schema.org vocabulary definitions.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Koha-Contrib-ValueBuilder-RepeatableAutocomplete - Repeatable autcomplete value-builder for Koha.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Koha-Contrib-Sudoc - Chargeur Koha par Tamil.
