      • EventStuff - For EV::Etcd - CryptoStuff.
      • WebServices
      • https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/ - Secret handling - https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets - Uses Kubernetes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IO::K8s - Objects representing things found in the Kubernetes API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Kubernetes::REST - A Perl REST Client for the Kubernetes API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-Kubernetes - Async Kubernetes client for IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-Zitadel - Async Perl client for Zitadel identity management (IO::Async + Future).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Zitadel - Perl client for Zitadel identity management (OIDC + Management API).
    • LaTeX
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting - BibTeX.
      • TemplateStuff
      • Manage the media files generated for the Reveal HTML presentation - https://metacpan.org/dist/BeamerReveal.
      • Converts LaTeX Beamer (PDF-centric) presentations into Reveal.js (Web-centric) HTML presentations
      • In this ecosystem, the .rvl file is a Reveal Configuration/Manifest file. It acts as the "instruction manual" for the conversion process
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LaTeX::Driver - Latex driver.
      • Easily format content into PDF/PS/DVI with LaTeX templates - https://metacpan.org/dist/LaTeX-Easy-Templates.
      • Format text content, living in a Perl data structure, into printer-ready documents (PDF/Postscript/DVI)
      • Documents how to install LaTeX
      • Uses Text::Xslate
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LaTeX::Encode - Encode characters for LaTeX formatting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LaTeX-Replicase - Perl extension implementing a minimalistic engine for filling real TeX-LaTeX files that act as templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LaTeX::Table - Perl extension for the automatic generation of LaTeX tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LaTeX-ToUnicode - Convert LaTeX commands to Unicode.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Latex - LaTeX support for the Template Toolkit.
    • LicenceStuff
      • TestingHelp - RegexpStuff.
      • Yes, licence is spelled that way in Australia
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Licensecheck - Functions for a simple license checker for source files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Software::License - software-license --holder 'Paul Marquess' --year 1995 --license Perl_5 --type license > LICENSE.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Enterprise-Licence - Licence or License.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/License::SPDX - Object for SPDX licenses handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LicenseScanner - This file is used to gather all CPAN licenses from third party dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Regexp-Pattern-License - Regular expressions for legal licenses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-Copyright - Copyright class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-License - Packages that provide templated software licenses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-License-MIT_0 - MIT No Attribution License (MIT-0).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-Policies - Create policy files: CODE_OF_CONDUCT, CONTRIBUTING, FUNDING, GOVERNANCE, SECURITY, SUPPORT, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-Policy-CodeOfConduct - Generate a Code of Conduct policy.
      • "Package" refers to the collection of files distributed by the Copyright
      • "Standard Version" refers to such a Package if it has not been modified,
      • "Copyright Holder" is whoever is named in the copyright or copyrights for
      • "You" is you, if you're thinking about copying or distributing this Package.
      • "Reasonable copying fee" is whatever you can justify on the basis of
      • "Freely Available" means that no fee is charged for the item itself, though
    • LinguaStuff
      • DataTypes - DataTraversal.
      • DatesAndTimes - For DatesAndTimes
      • [[HTTPServers]] - For CGI::ACL
      • TheRFCGuide
      • TabularData
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Gimei - A CLI app for Data::Gimei, a module generating fake Japanese names and addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Calendar-Indonesia-Holiday - List Indonesian public holidays.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Lingua - Create a multilingual web page.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Convert::CEGH - Coptic/Ethiopic/Greek/Hebrew Gematria & Transliteration.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Convert-Number-Coptic - Convert Between Western and Coptic Numeral Systems.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Convert-Number-Digits - Convert Digits Between the Scripts of Unicode.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Convert-Number-Ethiopic - Convert Between Western and Ethiopic Numeral Systems.
      • See also https://ethiopic.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Convert-Number-Roman - Convert Between Western and Roman Numeral Systems.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Date-Ethiopic - ICalendar for the Ethiopic Calendar System.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Gimei - A Perl module ported from Ruby's gimei that randomly generates Japanese names and addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-HanConvert - The data for converting between traditional and simplified Chinese languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Validate::Japanese - Validate Japanese Input.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Calendar-Coptic - DateTime Module for the Coptic Calendar System..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/I18N::Langinfo - Query locale information.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/I18N::LangTags - Functions for dealing with RFC3066-style language tags.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3066
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/I18N::LangTags::Detect - Detect the user's language preferences.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/I18N::LangTags::List - Tags and names for human languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Jacode - Perl program for Japanese character code conversion.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Jacode4e - Converts Character Encodings for Enterprise in Japan.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Jacode4e::RoundTrip - Converts JIS X 0213 Encodings with Round-Trip.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Koha-Contrib-Tamil - Various scripts to manage Koha instances.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Boolean::Tiny - A smaller Lingua::Boolean, with support for more languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-AFR-Numbers - Converts numeric values into their Afrikaans equivalents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-AFR-Word2Num - Converts Afrikaans words into numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-DEU-Word2Num - Word 2 Number conversion in DEU.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-ABC - American, British, and Canadian English.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::AddressParse - Extract components of a street address from free format text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-GivenNames - An SQLite database of derivations of English given names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-Infinitive - Determine the infinitive form of a conjugated word.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-Inflexion - Inflect English nouns, verbs, adjectives, and articles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::NameCase - Correctly case a person's name from UPPERCASE or lowcase.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::NameParse - Extract the components of a person or couples full name, from free form text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-Nickname - Genealogical nickname matching (Liz=Beth).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-Segmenter - Subdivide texts into passages that represent subtopics.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::StopWords - Typical stop words for an English corpus.
      • See Lingua::StopWords below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-StopWordList - A sorted list of English stop words.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::Titlecase - Titlecase English words by traditional editorial rules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::EN::TitleParse - Parse titles in people's names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-EN-Titlecase-Simple - John Gruber's headline capitalization script.
      • Lingua::EN::Titlecase provides a much more heavyweight, modular solution for the same problem
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-famibeib - Module to interact with the famibeib artificial language.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::FR::Numbers::Ordinate - Go from cardinal number (4) to ordinal ("4Ã¨me").
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Interset - DZ Interset is a universal morphosyntactic feature set to which all tagsets of all corpora/languages can be mapped.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-JA-Moji - Handle many kinds of Japanese characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::JA::Name::Splitter - Split a Japanese name into given and family.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-JPN-Word2Num - Word to number conversion in Japanese.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-NameUtils - Identify given/family names and capitalize correctly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-RO-Numbers - Convert numeric values into their Romanian string equivalents and viceversa .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-RU-Numeral - Perl extension for generate Russian wording of numerals from the natural numbers and 0 (zero).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-RU-Num2Word - Numbers to words in Russian (without currency, but with specified gender).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-RUS-Num2Word - Converts numbers to money sum in words (in Russian roubles).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Stem - Stemming of words.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Stem::Snowball - Perl interface to Snowball stemmers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::StopWords - Stop words for several languages.
      • See Lingua::EN::StopWords above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-String - Class to contain a string in many different languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-Text - Class to contain text in many different languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Maketext' - Framework for localization.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Maketext/view/lib/Locale/Maketext/TPJ13.pod - Article about software localization.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Equivalence-Amharic - Normalization Utilities for Amharic.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::ProperCase::Surname - Converts Surnames to Proper Case.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-Lingua-Num2Word - Install all PetaMem number-to-word conversion modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-Lingua-PetaMem - Install all PetaMem Lingua number conversion modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-Lingua-Word2Num - Install all PetaMem word-to-number conversion modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::DoubleMetaphone - Phonetic encoding of words..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Metaphone - A modern soundex. Phonetic encoding of words.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Metaphone::Amharic - The Metaphone Algorithm for Amharic.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Phonetic::Metaphone - Metaphone algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::TransMetaphone - Translingual Phonetic Encoding of Words.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::WagnerFischer::Amharic - The Wagner-Fischer Algorithm for Amharic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Unicode-Japanese - Convert encoding of Japanese text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Win32::Locale - Get the current MSWin locale or language.

      • DatesAndTimes - ChildProcesses.

      • FileHandling - For Linux::FD

      • TheOSGuide
      • https://www.devuan.org/
      • https://xfce.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::RPM::Spec::License - Base class for rpm-spec-license tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Clone - An interface to the linux clone, unshare, setns, pivot_root and kcmp syscalls.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Event - Front door for the Linux::Event ecosystem.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Event-Clock - Cached monotonic time and deadline math for Linux::Event schedulers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Event-Connect - Non-blocking outbound socket connect for Linux::Event.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Event::Fork - Minimal async child spawning on top of Linux::Event.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Event-Listen - Listening sockets for Linux::Event.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Event::Loop - Linux-native event loop (epoll + timerfd + signalfd + eventfd + pidfd).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Event-Stream - Buffered, backpressure-aware I/O for nonblocking file descriptors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Event::Timer - Thin wrapper around Linux::FD::Timer (timerfd).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Event::Watcher - Mutable watcher handle for Linux::Event::Loop.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-FD-Mem - Memory file descriptors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Inotify - Classes for supporting inotify in Linux Kernel >= 2.6.13.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Inotify2 - Scalable directory/file change notification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Perl - Linux system calls with pure Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Prctl - Perl extension for controlling process characteristics.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Socket::Accept4 - Accept4(2) bindings for Perl5.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::RPM::Spec - Perl extension to parse RPM spec files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RPM-Query - Perl object overlay of the RPM query command.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-OsRelease - Read operating system details from standard /etc/os-release file.
      • Under Debian that file is /usr/lib/os-release
    • LocaleStuff
      • DatesAndTimes - BusinessApps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/libintl-perl - For Locale::TextDomain::FAQ etc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Convert::Number::Digits - Convert Digits Between the Scripts of Unicode..
      • Methods included
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Localize::Format::Maketext - Maketext Formatter.
      • Warning: POD is void
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Localize::Format::Sprintf - Format strings using regular sprintf.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DateTime-Locale - Localization support for DateTime.pm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::AU - Abbreviations for territory and state identification in Australia and vice versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-CA - Two letter codes for province identification in Canada and vice versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Codes - A distribution of many modules to handle locale codes.
      • For example, there are codes associated with different countries, different currencies, different languages, etc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Country - Module for dealing with country code sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Country::Multilingual - To translate country names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Intl - A Web Intl.Locale Class Implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Maketext-Lexicon-DB - Dynamically load lexicon from a database table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Maketext-Utils - Adds some utility functionality and failure handling to Local::Maketext handles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::Messages - Gettext Like Message Retrieval.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Object - An object-oriented representation of locale information..
      • Contains Locale::Object::Continent, Locale::Object::Country, Locale::Object::Currency, Locale::Object::CurrencyConverter, Locale::Object::DB, Locale::Object::Language
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Places - Translate places between different languages using http://download.geonames.org/.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Simple - Functions for translate text based on gettext data, also in JavaScript.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::SubCountry - Convert state, province, county etc. names to/from ISO 3166-2 codes, get all states in a country.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::TextDomain - Perl Interface to Uniforum Message Translation.
      • Included in https://metacpan.org/dist/libintl-perl
      • The latter also contains Locale::Util - Portable l10n and i10n functions, and many others
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-TextDomain-OO-Extract - Extracts internationalization data.
      • Includes a long list of examples
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Unicode - Unicode Locale Identifier compliant with BCP47 and CLDR.
      • Locale::Unicode::Boolean Boolean: Representation Class
      • Locale::Unicode::Exception Locale: Unicode Exception C
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Locale-Unicode-Data - Unicode CLDR SQL Data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Locale::US - Two letter codes for state identification in the United States and vice versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Telugu-TGC - Tailored Grapheme Clusters for Telugu language.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Unicode-Japanese - Convert encoding of japanese text.
    • LoggingStuff
      • EmailStuff - ApacheStuff.
      • JsonStuff
      • ModuleBuilding
      • PoeStuff
      • TheRFCGuide
      • RotateLogFiles
      • TemplateStuff
      • https://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dancer2-Plugin-LogReport - Logging, exceptions and translations in Dancer2 via Log::Report.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBI-Log - Log all DBI queries.
      • POD refers to DBIx::Class::UnicornLogger, DBIx::Class::Storage::Debug::PrettyTrace
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/eris - eris exists to transform unstructured, chaotic log data into structured messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Abstraction - Logging Abstraction Layer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Any - Bringing loggers and listeners together.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Any::Adapter::Base - No description.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/MJGARDNER/Log-Any-Adapter-MacOS-OSLog-v0.0.1 - Log to macOS' unified logging system.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Any-Adapter-MojoLog - Log::Any integration for Mojo::Log.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Any::Adapter::Util - Common utility functions for Log::Any.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Dispatch - Dispatches messages to one or more outputs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Dispatch::Email - Base class for objects that send log messages via email.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Dispatch-Email-Mailer - Log::Dispatch::Email subclass that sends mail using Email::Mailer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Dispatch-TAP - Log to TAP output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Dispatch-UnixSyslog - Log events to syslog with Unix::Syslog.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Dispatch-WarnDie - Log standard Perl warnings and errors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Dispatch::XML - Collect one or more messages in XML format.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Dispatchouli - A simple wrapper around Log::Dispatch.
      • Contains Log::Fmt, etc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Fmt-XS - XS components to speed up Log::Fmt.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Handler - Log messages to several outputs.
      • This is my favourite
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Handler::Plugin::DBI - A plugin for Log::Handler using Log::Hander::Output::DBI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-JSON-Lines - Log in JSONLines format.
      • https://jsonlines.org
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Journald - Send messages to a systemd journal.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Log4perl - Log4j implementation for Perl.
      • Warning: Use this if you want maximum complexity in your code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Log4perl-Appender-AmazonSES - Send via Amazon SES (SMTP over TLS).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Log4perl-Appender-TAP - Append to TAP output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Log4perl-Config-YamlConfigurator - Reads Log4perl YAML configurations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::LogMethods - Writes your logging code for you!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Mini - It's a very simple logger which can log your messages to a file or STDERR.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Report - Report a problem, with exceptions and translation support.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Report-Lexicon - Translation component of Log::Report.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Report-Optional - Pick Log::Report or ::Minimal.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Report-Template - Template Toolkit with translations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::Syslog::Constants - Contains syslog priority constants as defined in RFC3164.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc3164
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Syslog-Fast - Send syslog messages over TCP, UDP, or UNIX sockets with minimal CPU overhead.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Log-Timer - Track nested timing information.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Log::WarnDie - Log standard Perl warnings and errors on a log handler.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LWP-ConsoleLogger - LWP tracing and debugging.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LWP-CurlLog - Log LWP::UserAgent / HTTP::Tiny requests as curl commands.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/No::Worries::Log - Logging without worries.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/No::Worries::Syslog - Syslog handling without worries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parse-Syslog-Line - Simple syslog line parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Readonly-Values-Syslog - Syslog Constants.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Syslog - Perl interface to the UNIX syslog(3) calls.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SyslogScan::Daemon - Watch log files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SyslogScan::Daemon::BlacklistDetector - Notice when a mail server has been blacklisted.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SyslogScan::Daemon::SpamDetector - Notice spammers in the log files.

      • https://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/syntax - http://neilb.org/reviews/markdown.html.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::MultiMarkdown
      • https://fletcherpenney.net/multimarkdown/
      • https://www.markdowntutorial.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-md - Greple module for Markdown syntax highlighting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-md2html - Blah blah blah.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-mdee - Markdown, Easy on the Eyes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CommonMark - Interface to the CommonMark C library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Liveman - Compiler from Markdown to tests and documentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Markdent - An event-based Markdown parser toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Markdent - Markdown extensions implemented on GitHub.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Markdown-ODF - Create ODF documents from Markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Markdown-Parser - Markdown Parser Only.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Markdown::Perl - Very configurable Markdown processor written in pure Perl, supporting the CommonMark spec and many extensions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Markdown-Render - Render markdown as HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Markdown-Simple - Markdown to HTML converter.
      • Code generated by co-pilot
      • Uses XS
      • https://github.com/swsnr/mdcat - Fancy cat for Markdown (that is, CommonMark).
      • Release binaries: https://github.com/swsnr/mdcat/releases/
      • An add-on which generates docs from code comments: https://github.com/EvanCarroll/p5-doc-comments
      • https://github.blog/2022-02-14-include-diagrams-markdown-files-mermaid/ - Mermaid is a JavaScript based diagramming and charting tool that takes Markdown-inspired text definitions and creates diagrams dynamically in the browser.
      • Warning: It outputs to an iFrame within your browser
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Regexp::Common::Markdown - Markdown Common Regular Expressions.
      • Used by Markdown::Parser
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Term-ANSIEncode - A markup language to generate basic ANSI text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-HTML-Turndown - Convert HTML to Markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Markdown - Convert Markdown syntax to (X)HTML. Buggy.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Markdown-Discount - Fast function for converting markdown to HTML (requires C compiler).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Markdown-Hoedown - Hoedown for Perl5.
      • https://github.com/hoedown/hoedown
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Markup - Parse text markup into HTML.
      • This distribution includes support for 15 markup formats
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Markup::Asciidoctor - Asciidoc parser for Text::Markup.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::MultiMarkdown - Convert MultiMarkdown syntax to (X)HTML.
    • MarlinStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin - ð Pretty fast class builder with most Moo/Moose features ð.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-Antlers - A more Moose-like syntax for Marlin.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-X-Clone - Marlin extension to add a clone method to your class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-X-ToHash - Marlin extension to add a to_hash method to your class.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-X-UndefTolerant - Marlin extension to make your constructor forgive undefs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-XAttribute-LocalWriter - Marlin attribute extension for localizing attribute values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Marlin-XAttribute-Lvalue - Marlin attribute extension for lvalue accessors.
    • Marpa
      • http://jeffreykegler.github.io/Marpa-web-site/libmarpa_api/latest/index.html - Libmarpa-specific terms - TheRFCGuide.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Pointer-Marpa
      • http://savage.net.au/Perl-modules/html/marpa.faq/faq.html - Edit this FAQ and re-upload it
      • - https://colabti.org/ircloggy/marpa/.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GraphViz2 - A wrapper for AT&T's Graphviz.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GraphViz2-Marpa - A Marpa-based parser for Graphviz dot files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GraphViz2-Marpa-Extractor - Extracts a simplified AST from GraphViz2::Marpa tree.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GraphViz2-Marpa-PathUtils - Provide various analyses of Graphviz dot files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JSON-Pointer-Marpa - Marpa::R2 based JSON Pointer RFC6901 implementation.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc6901
      • JSON Pointer identifies a specified value within a JSON document, and is similar to XPath
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Marpa::R2 - Release 2 of Marpa.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MarpaX-Grammar-GraphViz2 - Convert a Marpa grammar into an image.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MarpaX-Grammar-Parser - Converts a Marpa grammar into a tree using Tree::DAG_Node.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MarpaX-Languages-Dash - A Marpa-based parser for the DASH language.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MarpaX-Languages-SVG-Parser - A nested SVG parser, using XML::SAX and Marpa::R2.
      • https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr9/tr9-44.html#Paired_Brackets - https://www.unicode.org/notes/tn39/.
    • MathsLibraries
      • [[DFA]] - ChartingAndPlotting.
      • FeaturesAndPragmas
      • GeographicStuff
      • [[HTMLHandling]] - For MathML::Entities
      • PdlLanguage
      • SetStuff
      • StatisticsStuff
      • https://docs.octave.org/latest/Linear-Programming.html - For the Octave Manual
      • https://github.com/chrisarg/Bit - for Bit
      • https://www.gnu.org/software/glpk/ - For the GNU Linear Programming Kit
      • https://xlattice.sourceforge.net/components/protocol/kademlia/specs.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Bit - Find or install the Bit library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Qhull - Build and Install the Qhull library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Kademlia - Pure Perl implementation of the Kademlia DHT algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Loops - Looping constructs: NestedLoops, MapCar*, Filter, and NextPermute*.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::FastPermute - Rapid generation of permutations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Permute - Handy and fast permutation with object oriented interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::TimelinePacking - Arrange time intervals into non-overlapping lines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ArithmeticsUtils - Utilities related to arithmetics.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::GUI::Cellgraph - Draw pattern by cellular automata.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::GUI::Harmonograph - Sculpting beautiful circular drawings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::GUI::Juliagraph - Drawing Mandelbrot and Julia fractals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/bignum - Transparent big integer support for Perl.
      • Contains bigfloat, bigint, bignum, bigrat
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bijection - Bijection of an integer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bijection-XS - Bijection of an integer faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bit-Set - Perl interface for bitset functions from the 'bit' C library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Bit::Vector - Efficient bit vector, set of integers and "big int" math library.
      • If true & false are a problem, see https://blogs.perl.org/users/dean/2026/05/installing-bitvector-on-debian-13-trixie.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-AnyNum - Arbitrary size precision for integers, rationals, floating-points and complex numbers.
      • metacpan.org/pod/Math::BaseCalc - Convert numbers between various bases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Base-Convert - Very fast base to base conversion.
      • See also Math::BaseConvert below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::BaseConvert - Fast functions to CoNVert between number Bases.
      • See also Math::Base::Convert above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Basic - Basic math.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Basic-XS - The great new Math::Basic::XS!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt - Arbitrary size integer math package.
      • Contains Math::BigFloat and Math::BigRat etc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-BitVect - A math backend library based on Bit::Vector.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-FastCalc - Math::BigInt::Calc with some XS for more speed.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-GMP - Backend library for Math::BigInt etc. based on GMP.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-GMPz - A math backend library based on Math::GMPz.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-Lite - What Math::BigInts are before they become big.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-BigInt-Pari - A math backend library based on Math::Pari.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::BigRat - Arbitrary size rational number math package.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Cephes - Perl interface to the cephes math library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Complex - Complex numbers and associated mathematical functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::FakeBfloat16 - Emulate bf16 floating point arithmetic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-FakeDD - DoubleDouble precision arithmetic for all architectures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-FakeFloat16 - Emulate _Float16 floating point arithmetic..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::BigFloat - Arbitrary size floating point math package.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Cartesian::Product - Generate the Cartesian product of zero or more lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Disarrange::List - Generate all the disarrangements of a list.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Float32 - Perl interface to float (aka _Float32) floating point type.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Formula - Expressions on steroids.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::GMatrix - Extension of Math::Matrix for (2D graphics-)vector manipulation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::GMP - High speed arbitrary size integer math.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-GMPf - Perl interface to the GMP library's floating point (mpf) functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-GMPq - Perl interface to the GMP library's rational (mpq) functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::GMPn - Fixed length integer arithmetic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-GMPz - Perl interface to the GMP library's integer (mpz) functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-GMP_OLOAD - Assist in the overloading of Math::GMP objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::GSL::Alien - Easy installation of the GSL library.
      • GSL means Gnu Scientific Library
      • It has a See Also section
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Int128 - Manipulate 128 bits integers in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Int64 - Manipulate 64 bits integers in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Int64::C_API::Sample - C API Usage sample for Math::Int64.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Interpolator - Interpolate between lazily-evaluated points.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-MPC - Perl interface to the MPC (multi precision complex) library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-MPFI - Perl interface to the MPFI (interval arithmetic) library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-MPFR - Perl interface to the MPFR (floating point) library..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::NLopt - Math::NLopt - Perl interface to the NLopt optimization library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Pari - Perl interface to PARI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Permute::List - Generate all permutations of a list.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Permute::Lists - Generate all the permutations of zero or more nested lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Permute::Partitions - Generate all the permutations of a partitioned list..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Permutation - Pure Perl implementation of functions related to the permutations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::PlanePath - Points on a path through the 2-D plane.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Polygon - Class for maintaining polygon data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Polynomial - Perl class for polynomials in one variable.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Polynomial::Solve - Find the roots of polynomial equations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Prime::FastSieve - Generate a list of all primes less than or equal to $n.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Prime::Util - Utilities related to prime numbers, including fast sieves and factoring.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Prime-Util-GMP - Utilities related to prime numbers and factoring, using GMP.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-ReedSolomon-Encoder - Calculate Reed-Solomon Error Correction Codes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Round - Perl extension for rounding numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::RPN - Perl extension for Reverse Polish Math Expression Evaluation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Subsets::List - Generate all the subsets of a list.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Symbolic - Symbolic calculations.
      • This module comes with a huge list of modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-Factor - Re-arrange a Math::Symbolic expression into a product of factors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Symbolic::Custom::Collect - Collect up Math::Symbolic expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-CollectSimplify - Simplify Math::Symbolic expressions using Math::Symbolic::Custom::Collect.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-Equation - Work with equations of Math::Symbolic expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-Matrix - Matrix routines for Math::Symbolic.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Symbolic::Custom::Polynomial - Polynomial routines for Math::Symbolic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-ToShorterString - Shorter string representations of Math::Symbolic trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Symbolic-Custom-ToTallString - Pretty-print Math::Symbolic expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Transform::List - Generate specified transformations of a list.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::Trig - Trigonometric functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-Vector-Similarity - Cosine similarity, euclidean distance and other vector comparison functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Math::VectorReal - Module to handle 3D Vector Mathematics.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Number::AnyBase - Converts decimals to and from any alphabet of any size (for shortening IDs, URLs etc.).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Number::Equation - Track how a number is calculated progammatically.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Equation-XS - Track how a number is calculated progammatically.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Fraction - Perl extension to model fractions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Numeric-Matrix - SIMD-accelerated 2D matrices with BLAS GEMM.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Numeric-Vector - SIMD-accelerated numeric vectors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parse-RPN - A minimalist RPN parser/processor (a little like FORTH).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PERLANCAR::Permute::Named - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Permute::Named - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Permute::Named::Iter - Permute multiple-valued key-value pairs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Permute-Unnamed - Permute multiple-valued lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Permute-Unnamed-Iter - Permute multiple-valued lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Qhull - Interface to the Qhull convex hull, Delauny triangulation, Voronoi diagram software suite.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Shannon-Entropy - Calculate the Shannon entropy H of a given input string.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Shannon-Entropy-XS - Calculate the Shannon entropy H of a given input string faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/smallnum - Transparent "SmallNumber" support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/smallnum-XS - Faster transparent "SmallNumber" support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/uinteger - Like "use integer", but unsigned.
    • MeasureMetrics
      • https://www.influxdata.com/blog/infrastructure-monitoring-basics-telegraf-influxdb-grafana/ - InfluxDB - https://grafana.com/ - Query, visualize, alert on, and explore your metrics, logs, and traces wherever they are stored.
      • https://www.purestorage.com/au/knowledge/what-is-a-time-series-database.html - Time Series
      • https://www.slapbirdapm.com/ - Perl application observability platform
      • https://github.com/b/statsd_spec - StatsD Metrics Export Specification
      • https://docs.influxdata.com/telegraf/v1/ - Telegraf
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Cover - Code coverage metrics for Perl.
      • https://grafana.com/ - Is an open-source analytics and visualisation platform. It lets you create dashboards that visualise data from various sources.
      • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/grafana-prometheus/ - For sample code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lab-Measurement - API documentation manual for Lab::Measurement.
      • Lab::Measurement is a huge package with support for many types of hardware
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Statm::Tiny - Simple access to Linux /proc/../statm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Metrics-Any - Abstract collection of monitoring metrics.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Metrics-Any-Adapter-Prometheus - A metrics reporting adapter for Prometheus.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Measure-Everything - Log::Any for Stats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-Statsd - Asynchronous API for Etsy's statsd protocol.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Prometheus - Export monitoring metrics for prometheus.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Statsd - Perl client for Etsy's statsd daemon.
      • This module was forked from Net::Statsd::Tiny
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Statsd-Lite - A lightweight StatsD client that supports multimetric packets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Statsd-Tiny - A tiny StatsD client that supports multimetric packets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-Metrics-Halstead - Compute Halstead complexity metrics.
      • https://ology.github.io/2019/03/19/halstead-software-complexity-of-perl-code/
      • https://prometheus.io/ - Is an open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit.
      • https://theweeklychallenge.org/blog/grafana-prometheus/ - For sample code
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SlapbirdAPM-Agent-Dancer2 - The SlapbirdAPM user-agent for Dancer2/Mojo/Plack applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Time-Progress - Elapsed and estimated finish time reporting.
    • MemoryUsage
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Size - Perl extension for finding the memory usage of Perl variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Map - Memory mapping made simple and safe.
      • Contains map_anonymous (for when your processes have a parent-child relationship)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Smaps - A Perl interface to /proc/PID/smaps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Linux-Smaps-Tiny - A minimal and fast alternative to Linux::Smaps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Memory-Usage - Tools to determine actual memory usage.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Memchmark - Check memory consumption.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SysV::SharedMem - SysV Shared memory made easy.
      • But see the caveats discussed in https://metacpan.org/pod/POSIX::RT::SharedMem
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MemManager - A memory allocated and manager for low level code in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MemManager-CMalloc - Allocates buffers using C's malloc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MemManager-Device - Device-specific memory management extensions for Task::MemManager.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MemManager-View - Provides convenient views for Task::MemManager buffers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-MemManager-View-PDL - Create PDL views of Task::MemManager buffers.
    • MobilePhones
      • https://librephone.fsf.org/ - https://developer.android.com/tools/adb - Android Debug Bridge is a versatile command-line tool that lets you communicate with a device.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Android-ElectricSheep-Automator - Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep? Smartphone control from your desktop.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Make-Page - Automate making HTML tags 'head' and 'body'.
    • ModernPerl
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Modern::Perl - Enable all of the features of Modern Perl with one import.
      • Usage: use Modern::Perl '2024';
      • But V 1.20250607 says: Enhance documentation to discourage this module for v5.38 and newer
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Modern-Perl-Prelude - Project prelude for modern Perl style on Perl 5.30+.
    • ModuleBuilding
      • CommandLineStuff - CodingStyle.
      • [[CPAN]]
      • CpanFile
      • cpanm
      • DataTraversal
      • DataTypes - For Data::OptList::Object
      • DebuggingStuff
      • DistZilla
      • EventStuff
      • ExceptionHandling - For many scope-related modules
      • [[FAQ]] - For AI & policy
      • FeaturesAndPragmas - e.g. for lexically, strictures
      • FileHandling - For more stat & lstat stuff
      • [[Graphs]]
      • Jargon alert - Class aka OO aka Object Orientated
      • MarlinStuff
      • ModulePlugins
      • NewOperators - For Phasers, etc
      • ParallelProcessing
      • PerlInternals
      • SecurityPolicy
      • SwitchStatements - For Multi::Dispatch, Sub::MultiMethod
      • TabularData - For code which uses Role::TinyCommons
      • TestingHelp
      • UtilityStuff - For perlport
      • VersionWare
      • WeirdAndWonderful = For Ancient, a collection of utilities
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/dean/2026/03/shipping-a-perl-cli-as-a-single-file-with-appfatpacker.html
      • http://fabrice.bellard.free.fr/tcc/ - For tcc
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/robert_acock/2026/01/ready-set-compile-you-slow-camel.html - Read this first
      • https://github.com/sanko/infix - For infix
      • https://github.com/Perl/PPCs/blob/main/ppcs/ppc0022-metaprogramming.md
      • https://github.com/tatsuhiro-t/wslay - For Wslay
      • https://libwebsockets.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/recent - MetaCPAN's list of recent Perl module releases
      • http://rperl.org/ - For RPerl
      • https://savage.net.au/misc/Mojo.Wiki.html - For Beam::Wire
      • http://www.dsmit.com/cons/
      • https://www.sqlite.org/cli.html
      • https://ovid.github.io/articles/corinna-in-the-perl-core.html
      • https://andrewshitov.com/2023/07/06/built-in-classes-in-perl-5-38/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-AutoLoad - Automatically load uninstalled CPAN modules on the fly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Damn - 'Unbless' Perl objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-ExtUtils-XSOne-Test-Calculator - A scientific calculator demonstrating ExtUtils::XSOne.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::Intraweb - Allows you to use modules not yet installed on your system.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Affix - A Foreign Function Interface eXtension.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion - A postmodern object system for Perl 5, such as âMouseâ, âMooseâ, âMooâ, âMoâ and âMâ, but with improvements.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion-Fs - Utilities for the file system: reading, writing, searching, replacing files, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/aliased - Use shorter versions of class names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien - External libraries wrapped up for your viewing pleasure!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::Base - Base classes for Alien:: modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Build - Build external dependencies for use in CPAN.
      • This module contains a huge list of plugins, including Alien::Base
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::Build::Plugin::Build::CMake - CMake plugin for Alien::Build.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::CMake - Build and make available CMake library - http://cmake.org/.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::cmake3 - Find or download or build cmake 3.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-cmake4 - Find or download or build cmake 4.
      • Contains an important See also
      • https://duckdb.org/ - Find or build DuckDB.
      • See also FFI::Platypus::Record below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::fpm - Convert directories, rpms, python eggs, rubygems, and more to rpms, debs, solaris packages and more.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libwebsockets - Find or build libwebsockets C library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libui - Build and Install libui: A portable GUI library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::TinyCC - Retrieve useful information about the Alien installation of tcc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alien::TinyCCx - Retrieve useful information about the Alien installation of tcc with extended symbol tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Wslay - Discover or download and install Wslay.
      • Wslay is a WebSocket library written in C. It implements the protocol version 13 described in RFC 6455
      • See also Net::WebSocket::EVx below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-Xmake - Locate, Download, or Build and Install Xmake.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Alt - Alternate Module Implementations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-codefork - Worlds dumbest code forker.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::cpanminus - Get, unpack, build and install modules from CPAN.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-cpanminus-reporter - Send cpanm output to CPAN Testers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::cpanoutdated - Detect outdated CPAN modules in your environmen.
      • Installs cpan-outdated
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::depak - Pack dependencies onto your script file.
      • See also App::FatPacker::Simple
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::DualLivedList - Perl extension to determine if a module is Dual-Lived.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::FatPacker - Pack your dependencies onto your script file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-FatPacker-Simple - Only fatpack a script.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::hopen - hopen build system command-line interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-LintPrereqs - Check extraneous/missing/incorrect prerequisites in dist.ini.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::MechaCPAN - Mechanize the installation of CPAN things.
      • Can update version #s and push to CPAN - A standalone authoring tool for Module::Build::Tiny and Dist::Build.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::ModuleBuildTiny
      • See also Module::Build::Tiny
      • Leon's articles
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/leon_timmermans/2025/03/an-introduction-to-appmodulebuildtiny-part-1-setting-things-up.html
      • https://blogs.perl.org/users/leon_timmermans/2025/03/an-introduction-to-appmodulebuildtiny-part-2-authoring.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Module-Lister - List the Perl modules in @INC.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-newver - Scan upstream for new software versions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-perlimports - Make implicit imports explicit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PerlPackage2PlantUMLClassDiagram - Generate Plant UML's class diagraom from Perl packages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PP-Autolink - Run the pp (PAR Packager) utility while automatically finding dynamic libs to link.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-prepare4release - Prepare a Perl distribution for release (skeleton).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::PrereqGrapher - Generate dependency graph using Perl::PrereqScanner.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ThisDist - Print Perl {distribution,module,author,...} associated with current directory.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aspect - Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP) for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Assert::Conditional - Conditionally-compiled code assertions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/authority - Loads a module only if it has a particular authority.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/autovivification - Lexically disable autovivification.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::CallChecker - Custom B-based op checking for subroutines.
      • See also Devel::CallParser
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::LintSubs - Perl compiler backend to check sub linkage.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Badger - Perl Application Programming Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/bareword::filehandles - Disables bareword filehandles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/base - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Blessed::Merge - Merge Blessed Refs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/BsDiPa - Create or apply binary difference patch.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/C::Blocks - Embedding a fast C compiler directly into your Perl parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/C::DynaLib - Dynamic Perl interface to C compiled code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/C::TCC - An interface to the TCC(Tiny C Compiler).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/C::TinyCompiler - Full C JIT compiling using the Tiny C Compiler.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/C-Tokenize - Reduce a C file to a series of tokens.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CallBackery - Appliance Frontend Builder.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Caller-Reverse - Reverse the caller stack.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Accessor - Automated accessor generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Accessor::Fast - Faster, but less expandable, accessors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Accessor::Lite - A minimalistic variant of Class::Accessor.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Accessor::Validated - Drop-in constructor validation for Class::Accessor::Fast-based classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-Autouse - Run-time load a class the first time you call a method in it..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::EHierarchy - This is intended for use as a base class for objects that need support for class or object hierarchies.
      • See also https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::PlainConfig
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Inspector - Get information about a class and its structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-Mite - A minimal, integrated Class and Role system for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::MOP::Class - Class Meta Object.
      • See also Class::MOP::Module, Class::MOP::Package
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::MOP::Module - Module Meta Object.
      • See also Class::MOP::Class, Class::MOP::Package
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::MOP::Package - Package Meta Object.
      • See also Class::MOP::Class, Class::MOP::Module
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::SingletonMethod - Extend individual objects with additional methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::Tiny - Minimalist class construction.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-Unload - Unload a class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::XSAccessor - Generate fast XS accessors without runtime compilation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Class::XSAccessor::Array - Generate fast XS accessors without runtime compilation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Class-XSConstructor - A super-fast (but limited) constructor in XS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/common::sense - Save a tree AND a kitten, use common::sense!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Compiler::Lexer - Lexical Analyzer for Perl5.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/EORBKV/cons-2.3.0 - A Software Construction System.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Contextual::Return - Create context-sensitive return values.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/BBB/cpan2rpm-2.036 - A Perl module packager.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::API::BuildPL - Documentation of the API for using Build.PL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Meta - The distribution metadata for a CPAN dist.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Meta::Check - Verify requirements in a CPAN::Meta object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Meta::Requirements - A set of version requirements for a CPAN dist.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Meta::YAML - Read and write a subset of YAML for CPAN Meta files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Requirements::Dynamic - Dynamic prerequisites in meta files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Static::Install - Static CPAN installation reference implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CPAN::Static::Spec - Static install specification for CPAN distributions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-DeploymentHandler - Extensible DBIx::Class deployment.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-DynamicSubclass - Convenient way to use dynamic subclassing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::VersionedSubs - All your code are belong into the DB.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dev-Util - Base modules for Perl Development.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::AssertOS - Require that we are running on a particular OS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::CallChecker - Custom op checking attached to subroutines.
      • But see Devel::CallParser
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-CallParser - Custom parsing attached to subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::CheckLib - Check that a library is available.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::CheckOS - Check what OS we're running on.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::FindPerl - Find the path to your perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-Hide - Forces the unavailability of specified Perl modules (for testing).
      • See also Test::Without::Module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-MaintBlead - Handle maint / blead code paths for distributions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::OverrideGlobalRequire - Override CORE::GLOBAL::require safely.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-PPPort - Perl/Pollution/Portability.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Devel::Required - Automatic update of required modules documentation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-ThreadsForks - Check for availability of threads or forks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Developer-Dashboard - A local home for development work.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dios - Declarative Inside-Out Syntax.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-Build - A modern module builder, author tools not included!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-Build-DynamicPrereqs - Support dynamic prerequisites in Dist::Build.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/English - Use nice English (or awk) names for ugly punctuation variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Eval::Closure - Safely and cleanly create closures via string eval.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Export::These - Terse Module Configuration and Symbol (Re)Exporting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Export-XS - Replacement for Exporter.pm + const.pm in XS, with C++ API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exporter - Implements default import method for modules.
      • https://dev.to/tobyink/introducing-exporteralmighty-mc5 - Combining Exporter::Tiny with some other stuff for added power.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exporter::Lexical - Exporter for lexical subs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exporter::Tiny - An exporter with the features of Sub::Exporter but only core dependencies.
      • Includes Exporter::Shiny
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Builder - An overview of the foundations of the ExtUtils::Builder Plan framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Builder-Compiler - An interface around different compilers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::CBuilder - Compile and link C code for Perl modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::CChecker - Configure-time utilities for using C headers, libraries, or OS features.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-CFeatureTest - Test a host for available C language features and libraries.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::Config - A wrapper for perl's configuration.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Depends - Easily build XS extensions that depend on XS extensions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::HasCompiler - Check for the presence of a compiler.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::Helpers - Various portability utilities for module builders.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::InstallPaths - Build.PL install path logic made easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::MakeMaker - Create a module Makefile.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-MakeMaker - Beware directives INSTALL_BASE and PREFIX.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-ParseXS - Converts Perl XS code into C code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ExtUtils::PkgConfig - Simplistic interface to pkg-config.
      • But see Alien::libtickit
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Typemaps-Misc - A collection of miscelaneous typemap templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-XSOne - Combine multiple XS files into a single shared library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Factory-Sub - Generate a factory of subs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Faster - Do some things faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Feature::Compat::Class - Make class syntax available.
      • Probably prefer this to Object::Pad, but is not as featureful as the latter
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Feature::Compat::Defer - Make defer syntax available.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/FFI-C-Stat - Object-oriented FFI interface to native stat and lstat.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FFI::CheckLib - Check that a library is available for FFI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FFI::Platypus - Write Perl bindings to non-Perl libraries with FFI. No XS required.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/FFI-Platypus-Lang-V - Documentation and tools for using Platypus with the V programming language.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/FFI-Platypus-Lang-Zig - Documentation and tools for using Platypus with the Zig programming language.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FFI::Platypus::Record - FFI support for structured records data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FFI::Raw - Perl bindings to the portable FFI library (libffi).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FFI::TinyCC - Tiny C Compiler for FFI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Function::Parameters - Define functions and methods with parameter lists ("subroutine signatures").
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Glib-Object-Introspection - Dynamically create Perl language bindings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hades - Less is more, more is less!.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hades-Realm-Exporter - Hades realm for Exporter.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hades-Realm-Import-Export - Hades realm for Import::Export.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Hades-Realm-OO - Hades realm for object orientation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::AsObject - Treat hashes as objects, with arbitrary accessors/mutators.
      • But see Object::Adhoc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Objectify - Create objects from hashes on the fly.
      • But see Object::Adhoc
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Import-Box - Box up imports into an object or unified function..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Import::Into - Import packages into other packages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/indirect - Lexically warn about using the indirect method call syntax.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Inline - Write Perl Subroutines in Other Programming Languages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Inline-Support
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Inline-C - C Language Support for Inline.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Inline::CPP - Write Perl subroutines and classes in C++.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Inline::Java - Write Perl classes in Java..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Inline-Lua - Embed Lua and Fennel code in your Perl scripts.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Inline::Module - Support for Inline-based CPAN Extension Modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/InlineX::CPP2XS - Convert from Inline C++ code to XS.
      • https://medium.com/@mayurkoshti12/install-perl-module-without-cpan-2fe935f3a45a
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JavonetPerlSdk - Javonet allows you to reference and use modules or packages written in many languages.

      • Groovy
      • Clojure
      • C#

      • Perl
      • Python
      • JavaScript
      • TypeScript
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JTM-Boilerplate - Default Boilerplate for Joelle Maslak's Code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/LaunchDarkly::Server - Perl server side SDK for LaunchDarkly, a fancy release manager.
      • See https://launchdarkly.com/docs/sdk/server-side/c-c--
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lexical-Accessor - True private attributes for Moose/Moo/Mouse.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lexical::Import - Clean imports from package-exporting modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/lib-archive - Load pure-Perl modules directly from TAR archives.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Long::Jump - Mechanism for returning to a specific point from a deeply nested stack.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Medusa - Subroutine auditing via attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Medusa-XS - High-performance XS audit logging with the :Audit attribute.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Meow - Object à¸^â¢ï»â¢^à¸ Orientation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/meta - Meta-programming API.
      • This package provides an API for metaprogramming; that is, allowing code to inspect or manipulate parts of its own program structure
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Minilla - CPAN module authoring tool.
      • Contains Minilla::Tutorial
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mite - Moose-like OO, fast to load, with zero dependencies.
      • Demo: https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Mitey-Cards
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Build - Build and install Perl modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Build::CleanInstall - Subclass of Module::Build which removes the old module before installing the new one.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Build::Convert - Makefile.PL to Build.PL converter.
      • But for authoring, use App::ModuleBuildTiny - Looks good.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-CheckVersion - Check module (e.g. check latest version) with CPAN (or equivalent repo).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-CheckLatestVersion - Warn/die when a module is not the latest version.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::ExtractUse - Find out what modules are used.
      • This is not Module::Extract::Use, below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Extract::Use - Discover the modules a module explicitly uses.
      • This is not Module::ExtractUse, above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Extract::VERSION - Extract a module version safely.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Load - Runtime require of both modules and files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Features - Define features for modules.
      • Features of modules that generate text tables - https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Features::PluginSystem.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Generate - Assist with module generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Generic - Generic Module to inherit from.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Install - Standalone, extensible Perl module installer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Install::AssertOS - A Module::Install extension to require that we are running on a particular OS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::List - Module 'directory' listing.
      • The POD helpfully contains a long list of similar modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Manifest-Skip - MANIFEST.SKIP Manangement for Modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Patch - Patch package with a set of patches.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Release - Automate software releases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Runtime - Runtime module handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Signature - Module signature file manipulation.
      • See also Test::Signature in TestingHelp
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Starter - A simple starter kit for any module.
      • See also Module::Starter::Plugin
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Starter::AddModule - Add a new module to a distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Starter/view/lib/Module/Starter/Plugin.pod - How Module::Starter plugins work.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Starter::Simple - A simple, comprehensive Module::Starter plugin.
      • Part of Module::Starter
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Starter-Smart - A Module::Starter plugin for adding new modules into an existing distribution.
      • See Module::Starter::AddModule
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Starter::PBP - Create a module as recommended in "Perl Best Practices".
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Moo - Minimalist Object Orientation (with Moose compatibility).
      • MooX::Press, which contains Zydeco::Lite
      • MooX::Pression
      • MooX::Singleton
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mooish-Base - Importer for Mooish classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mooish-AttributeBuilder - Build Mooish attribute definitions with less boilerplate.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Moo::Role - Minimal Object Orientation support for Roles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Moonshine::Magic - d[ o_0 ]b - has, extends, lazy_components.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooseX-XSConstructor - Glue between Moose and Class::XSConstructo.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Keyword::Field - Field attributes that cannot be set via the constructor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooX-LazierAttributes - Lazier Attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Private::Attribute - Private attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::StrictConstructor - Make your Moo-based object constructors blow up on unknown attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::Traits - Automatically apply roles at object creation time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::TypeTiny - Optimized type checks for Moo + Type::Tiny.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MooX::XSConstructor - Glue between Moo and Class::XSConstructor.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MOP4Import::Declare - Boilerplate base class for Runnable Module .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mouse - Moose minus the antlers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MouseX-OO_Modulino - Turn your Mouse class into JSON-aware Object-Oriented Modulino.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MouseX::SingletonMethod - Mouse with Singleton Method facility.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MouseX::Traits - Automatically apply roles at object creation time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/multidimensional - Disables multidimensional array emulation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Carmel - perlbrew --install-multi.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/namespace-allclean - Avoid imports all subroutines into your namespace.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/namespace::clean - Keep imports and functions out of your namespace.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Nefarious - Wicked or criminal objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-WebSocket-EVx - Perl wrapper around Wslay websocket library.
      • See also Alien::Wslay above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/noop - A no-operation function, optimized to a single custom op.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/No::Worries - Coding without worries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Adhoc - Make objects without the hassle of defining a class first.
      • POD contains an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Anon - Create objects on the fly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Configure - Runtime Configuration for an Object.
      • Was called Class::Debug
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-HashBase - Build hash based classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::ID - A unique identifier for any object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::InsideOut - Comprehensive inside-out object support module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::InsideOut::Metadata - Introspection for Object::InsideOut classes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Pad - This module is essentially the basis of Perl's new keyword 'class'!.
      • A simple syntax for lexical field-based objects
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Pad/view/lib/Object/Pad/Guide/MigratingFromClassicalPerl.pod
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Proto - Objects with prototype chains.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Proto-Sugar - Moo-se-like syntax for Object::Proto.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ODS - Object Data Store.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Extend - Add and override per-object methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Object-Realize-Later - Delayed creation of objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Result - Allow subs to build and return objects on-the-fly.
      • But see Object::Adhoc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Simple - Simplest class builder, Mojo::Base porting, fast and less memory.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Tap - Tap into a series of method calls to alter an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Object::Util - A selection of utility methods that can be called on blessed objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Package::Stash - Routines for manipulating stashes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Package::Subroutine - Minimalistic import/export and other util methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Package::Variant - Parameterizable packages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PAR-Packer - PAR Packager.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/parent - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/parent::versioned - Establish an ISA relationship with base classes at compile time, with version checking.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parse-H - A parser for C header files that calls the given subroutines when a symbol of a specified type is encountered.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-APIReference - Programmatically query the perlapi.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::OSType - Map Perl operating system names to generic types.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-PrereqScanner - A tool to scan your Perl code for its prerequisites.
      • Command-line tool: scan-perl-prereqs
      • See also App::PrereqGrapher
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::PrereqScanner::Lite - Lightweight Prereqs Scanner for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-PrereqScanner-NotQuiteLite - A tool to scan your Perl code for its prerequisites.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Squish - Reduce Perl code to a few characters as possible.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Strip - Reduce file size by stripping whitespace, comments, pod etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::perlbrew - See http://savage.net.au/misc/Debian.Wiki.html for curl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlBuildSystem - Make replacement with rules written in perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlX-Maybe - Return a pair only if they are both defined.
      • Ship with Syntax::Feature::Maybe
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlX::Perform - Syntactic sugar for if (defined ...) { ... }.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl7-Handy - Handy Perl7 scripting environment on Perl5.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PPI::Dumper - Dumping of PDOM trees.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Probe::Perl - Information about the currently running perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Quiq - Class library for rapid development.
      • POD in German
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Basic - Just roles. Nothing else.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Commons - Roles that can be commonly used, for the mutual benefit of all.
      • Uses Moo::Role. See Role::TinyCommons below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Commons::Authority - A class method indicating who published the package.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Commons::Tap - An object method which helps with chaining, inspired by Ruby.
      • Part of Role::Commons
      • DO NOT USE THIS MODULE! Use Object::Tap or Object::Util instead
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Tiny - Roles: a nouvelle cuisine portion size slice of Moose.
      • Included in Role::Tiny::With
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::TinyCommons - Roles that can be commonly used, for the mutual benefit of all.
      • Uses Role::Tiny. See Role::Commons
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::TinyCommons::Iterator::Basic - A basic iterator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::TinyCommons::Iterator::Resettable - A resettable iterator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Role::Tiny::With - Neat interface for consumers of Role::Tiny roles.
      • Included in Role::Tiny
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Safe - Compile and execute code in restricted compartments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Safe::Hole - Make a hole to the original main compartment in the Safe compartment.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Safe::Isa - Call isa, can, does and DOES safely on things that may not be objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scalar::Andand - Guarded method invocation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Simple-Accessor - Very simple, light and powerful accessor.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Simple::Tuple - Because tuples should be simple and simple to use!.
      • Utility to define singleton methods - https://github.com/tom-lpsd/p5-singleton-method.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SourceCode-LineCounter-Perl - Count lines in Perl source code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Stefo - Compile Perl subs to optimized bytecode via optree walking.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-Delete - Perl module enabling one to delete subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Identify - Retrieve names of code references.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Mutate - Examination and modification of subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Name - (Re)name a sub.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-Quote - Efficient generation of subroutines via string eval.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::StrictDecl - Detect undeclared subroutines in compilation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-Uplevel - Apparently run a function in a higher stack frame .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Util - A selection of utility subroutines for subs and CODE references.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Subclass::Of - Import a magic subclass.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/syntax - Activate syntax extensions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Construct - Explicitly state which non-feature constructs are used in the code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Keyword-Wielding - Adds a "wielding" keyword to make it easier to call the same function or method multiple times.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UNIVERSAL::AUTHORITY::Lexical - An AUTHORITY method for every class, within a lexical scope.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/URI::PackageURL - Perl extension for Package URL (aka "purl").
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Venus - Standard Library.
      • List all methods, scalars, arrays, hashes and globs - Introspection of the package.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Xerarch
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XS::Framework - Framework for building C++ XS modules with shared code, typemaps and C++ perl API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XS::Install - ExtUtils::MakeMaker based module installer for XS modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XS-JIT - Lightweight JIT compiler for XS code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XS::Manifesto - Shared XS modules manifesto.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XS::TCC - Embed, wrap & compile C code in Perl without going to disk.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/YAOO - Yet Another Object Orientation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Zydeco - Jazz up your Perl.
      • http://zydeco.toby.ink/
      • http://blogs.perl.org/users/toby_inkster/2020/02/mooxpression-now-much-faster.html
      • Was Moox::Pression
    • ModulePlugins
      • ModuleBuilding - ChildProcesses.
      • Note that "commandline" does not necessarily mean "plain-text running in a terminal" - Utilities for commandline-based programs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Commandable
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Keyword-Pluggable - Define new keywords in pure Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Pluggable - Automatically give your module the ability to have plugins.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::Pluggable::Object - Automatically give your module the ability to have plugins.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/next-XS - Replacement for next::method, performing very fast, and super::method (next-by-mro), with Perl and C++ interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plugins - Generic plugins framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plugins::API - Inter-plugin callback API.
