      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lang-Monkey - Monkey programming language.
    • MusicStuff
      • WebServices - HardwareStuff.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forte_number
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numeral_analysis
      • https://lilypond.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-RtMidi - Install RtMidi.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-DrivePlayer - GTK3 music player for Google Drive.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/JV/App-Music-ChordPro-6.070.7 - A lyrics and chords formatting program.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::MusicTools - Command line utilities for music composition and analysis.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-ISMN - Work with International Standard Music Numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/idi - Easy, command-line MIDI.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LyricFinder - Fetch song lyrics from several internet lyric sites.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI - Read, compose, modify, and write MIDI files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-Drummer-Tiny - Glorified metronome.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI::Event - MIDI events.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-RtController - Control your MIDI controller.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-RtController-Filter-CC - Control-change based RtController filters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-RtController-Filter-Tonal - Tonal RtController filters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-RtMidi-FFI - Bindings for librtmidi - Realtime MIDI library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI::RtMidi::FFI::Device - OO interface for MIDI::RtMidi::FFI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI::RtMidi::ScorePlayer - Play a MIDI score in real-time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI::Simple - Procedural/OOP interface for MIDI composition.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MIDI-Stream - MIDI bytestream decoding and encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MIDI::Util - MIDI Utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::AtonalUtil - Atonal music analysis and composition.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Canon - Routines for musical canon construction.
      • - Get Chord Tone List from Chord Name.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Chord::Positions - Generate various chord inversions and voicings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Duration - Add 32nd, 64th, 128th and tuplet durations to MIDI-Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Duration-Partition - Partition a musical duration into rhythmic phrases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Drummer - Use MIDI::Drummer::Tiny.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Harmonica-TabsCreator - Convert tunes into harmonica tabs .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Interval-Barycentric - Compute barycentric musical interval space.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Intervals - Breakdown of musical intervals.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::LilyPondUtil - Utility methods for lilypond data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-MelodicDevice-Arpeggiation - Apply arpeggiation patterns to groups of notes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Note - Representation of a single musical note, and various manipulation routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::PitchNum - Note name and pitch number utility roles.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-Scala - Scala scale support for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Scales - Supply necessary notes / offsets for musical scales.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-ScaleNote - Manipulate the position of a note in a scale.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Music-SimpleDrumMachine - Simple 16th-note-phrase Drummer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Tempo - Various conversions to and from BPM.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Tension - Music tension analysis.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::Tension::Cope - Tension analysis for equal temperament music.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::ToRoman - Convert notes and chords to Roman numeral notation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Music::VoiceGen - Musical voice generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Types-MIDI - Type library for MIDI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WebService::MusicBrainz - API to search the musicbrainz.org database.
    • NetworkProgramming
      • EmailStuff - Acronyms.
      • IpAddresses
      • PacketCapture
      • RegexpStuff
      • TheRFCGuide - For rfc1939
      • [[SSH]]
      • SystemMonitoring
      • WebServices
      • https://blog.urth.org/2021/03/29/security-issues-in-perl-ip-address-distros/
      • https://github.com/netdisco/snmp-info/
      • http://netdisco.org/
      • https://suricata.io/ - For open source network analysis and threat detection
      • http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MAC_Address
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Acme::UPnP - Cheap UPnP Port Mapping (IGD).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Netdisco - An open source web-based network management tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::NetdiscoX::Web::Plugin::Observium - Port Traffic Links and Thumbnail Graphs from Observium.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ReslirpTunnel - A wrapper for the reslirp-tunnel application over SSH for remote connections.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Check-NetworkSpans - See if bidirectional traffic is being seen on spans.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Check-SuricataFlows - Make sure Suricata is seeing data via reading the Suricata flows json.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Control-CLI - Command Line Interface I/O over either Telnet or SSH (IPv4 & IPv6) or Serial port.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Control-CLI-Extreme - Interact with CLI of Extreme Networking products over any of Telnet, SSH or Serial port.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Throttler - Limit data throughput.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Device::MAC - Handle hardware MAC Addresses (EUI-48 and EUI-64).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Device::OUI - Resolve an Organizationally Unique Identifier.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Device::WWN - Encode/Decode Fiber Channel World Wide Names.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/FTN::Addr - Working with FTN addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gearman::Client - Client for gearman distributed job system.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gearman::Objects - A parent class for Gearman::Client and Gearman::Worker.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gearman::Server - Function call "router" and load balancer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gearman::Worker - Worker for gearman distributed job system.
      • https://www.pc-tools.net/unix/grepcidr/ - Filter IPv4 and IPv6 addresses matching CIDR patterns.
      • See Acronyms
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/libnet - This is a collection of perl5 modules which all related to network programming.
      • The majority of the modules available provide the client side of popular server-client protocols
      • libnetFAQ - https://metacpan.org/dist/libnet/view/lib/Net/libnetFAQ.pod
      • Net::Cmd - Network Command class (as used by FTP, SMTP etc)
      • Net::Config - Local configuration data for libnet
      • Net::Domain - Attempt to evaluate the current host's internet name and domain
      • Net::FTP - FTP Client class
      • Net::FTP::dataconn - FTP Client data connection class
      • Net::NNTP - NNTP Client class
      • Net::Netrc - OO interface to users netrc file
      • Net::POP3 - Post Office Protocol 3 Client class (RFC1939)
      • Net::SMTP - Simple Mail Transfer Protocol Client
      • Net::Time - time and daytime network client interface
      • See also Net::LMTP2 below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mnet - Testable network automation and reporting .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/NetAddr::IP - Manages IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and subnets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-BART - Balanced Routing Tables for IPv4 and IPv6.
      • https://perladvent.org/2022/2022-12-17.html - Perl extension for merging IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-CIDR-Set - Manipulate sets of IP addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DHCP - Object methods to create a DHCP packet.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-DHCPv6 - DHCPv6 packet decoder/encoder.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::EmptyPort - Find a free TCP/UDP port.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::FTP - FTP Client class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::IP - Perl extension for manipulating IPv4/IPv6 addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-IP-XS - IPv4/IPv6 address library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-LMTP2 - A client class for the LMTP protocol which claims to fix Net::SMTP.
      • See also libnet above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::MAC - Perl extension for representing and manipulating MAC addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::MacMap - A module used to determine the vendor of an interface based on the MAC addres.
      • But see the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-MAC-Vendor - Look up the vendor for a MAC.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Netmask - Parse, manipulate and lookup IP network blocks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Nostr - Perl client and relay library for the Nostr protocol.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Packet - A framework to easily send and receive frames from layer 2 to layer 7.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Patricia - Patricia Trie perl module for fast IP address lookups.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-SIP - Framework SIP (Voice Over IP, RFC3261).
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc321
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-UPnP - Perl extension for UPnP.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::UPnP::AV::MediaServer - Perl extension for UPnP.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::UPnP::ControlPoint - Perl extension for UPnP control point.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Versa::Director - Versa Director REST API client library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetAddr-MAC - MAC hardware address functions and object (EUI48 and EUI64).
      • https://metacpan.org/release/KORNIX/NetBox-API-0.1.0 - Perl interface to NetBox API.
      • https://netboxlabs.com/docs/netbox/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetBox-Client - Perl5 interface to NetBox API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetPacket-LACP - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.1D Spanning Tree protocol packets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetPacket-LLC - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.3 LLC protocol packets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NetPacket-SpanningTree - Assemble and disassemble IEEE 802.1D Spanning Tree protocol packets.
      • Has good SEE ALSO - Asynchronous Stream Building Blocks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SNMP-Info - OO Interface to Network devices and MIBs through SNMP.
      • https://github.polettix.it/ETOOBUSY/2022/03/22/socat-local-to-remote-port/ - Socat is a command line based utility that establishes two bidirectional byte streams and transfers data between them.
      • - https://metacpan.org/dist/Socket - Networking constants and support functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Webservice::InternetDB::API - Fast IP Lookups for Open Ports and Vulnerabilities using InternetDB API.
      • Uses https://internetdb.shodan.io/
      • Refers to https://www.freepublicapis.com/
    • NewOperators
      • FeaturesAndPragmas - ArrayHandling.
      • SwitchStatements
      • [[XS]]
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Keyword::API - This module provides a pure perl interface for the keywords API added to the perl core in 5.12.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Keyword-Declare - Declare new Perl keywords...via a keyword...named keyword.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Keyword-Simple - Define new keywords in pure Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlX::SafeNav - Safe-navigation for Perl aka Optional Chaining.
      • See also https://github.com/Perl/PPCs/blob/main/ppcs/ppc0021-optional-chaining-operator.md
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/List::Keywords - A selection of list utility keywords.
      • first, all, any, none, notall, reduce, reductions, ngrep, nmap
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Feature-Loop - Provides the loop BLOCK syntax for unconditional loops.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Feature::Maybe - Use syntax qw/maybe/.
      • Included in PerlX::Maybe
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Feature-QwComments - Pragma to allow comments in qw().
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Feature-Void - Provides the void operator to force void context.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Feature-With - Simulate Pascal's "with" statement in Per.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Dynamically - Dynamically change the value of a variable.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::PhaserExpression - Phasers as arbitrary expressions rather than blocks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::Elem - Element-of-list operators.
      • Samples
      • $s elem @strs # like any { $s eq $_ } @strs
      • $n â @nums # like any { $n == $_ } @nums
      • Yes, this is a UTF-8 named operator. No I will not apologise
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::Equ - Equality operators that distinguish undef.
      • $str equ $str # like eq but cares about undef
      • $num === $num # like == but cares about undef
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::Elem - elem and similar operators, and the `in` meta-operator allow you to easily ask the question "does this string appear in that set of strings?" or similar for numbers. Useful combined with match/case.
      • Samples
      • $x elem @strings # is a stringy comparison
      • $x â @numbers # is a numbery comparison
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::In
      • $x in:isa @classes
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Operator-Is - Match operator using Data::Checks constraints.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Operator::Zip - Infix operator to compose two lists together.
      • @list Z @list # like List::Util::zip but faster
      • @list M @list # like List::Util::mesh but faster
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XS-Parse-Keyword - XS functions to assist in parsing keyword syntax.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XS-Parse-Sublike - XS functions to assist in parsing sub-like syntax.
    • OfficeFiles
      • SpreadsheetStuff - OrgDocHandling.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::OfficeUtils - Utilities related to Office suite files (.doc, .docx, .odt, .xls, .xlsx, .ods, etc).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AsposeCellsCloud-CellsApi - Allow developers to process and manipulate spreadsheet documents (like Microsoft Excel, OpenDocument Spreadsheet, CSV, etc.) without Microsoft Office.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AsposeSlidesCloud-SlidesApi - No description.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Document::OOXML - Manipulation of Open Office XML files.
      • Similar to MsOffice::Word::Surgeon
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ODF-lpOD - An OpenDocument management interface.
      • For .doc: Text::Extract::Word - Tamper with the guts of Microsoft docx documents, with regexes.
      • For .docx: https://blogs.perl.org/users/c_y_fung/2023/03/textextractword-msofficewordsurgeon---weekly-travelling-in-cpan.html
      • POD contains an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ODF::lpOD - An OpenDocument management interface.
      • Possibly unmaintained. See comments at end of ODF::lpOD_Helper
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ODF::lpOD_Helper - Fix and enhance ODF::lpOD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ODF-MailMerge - "Mail Merge" or just substitute tokens in ODF documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/MsOffice::Word::Template - Generate Microsoft Word documents from Word templates.
    • OpenGL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenGL - v0.70.
      • Contains OpenGL::Array and OpenGL::Tessellation
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenGL-GLFW - Perl bindings for the GLFW library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenGL-GLUT - Perl bindings to GLUT/FreeGLUT GUI toolkit.
      • Perl OpenGL (POGL) and Perl OpenGL::Modern (POGL2) development share common resources. At some point OpenGL::Modern will replace the legacy OpenGL implementation. Together they are referred to as POGL - Perl extension to Modern OpenGL API up to 4.5.
    • OpenGraphProtocol
      • https://json-ld.org/ - https://ogp.me/.
      • https://schema.org/
      • https://perlhacks.com/2025/01/adding-structured-data-with-perl/ - For JSON_LD
      • https://perlhacks.com/2025/09/easy-seo-for-lazy-programmers/ - For MooX::Role::SEOTags
      • The Open Graph protocol enables any web page to become a rich object in a social graph
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Web-PageMeta - Get page open-graph / meta data.
    • OpenTelemetry
      • [[HTTPServers]] - [[HTTPHandling]].
      • https://opentelemetry.io/ - Active
      • https://opentracing.io/ - Archived
      • http://savage.net.au/index.html - The Mojolicious Wiki
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenTelemetry - A Perl implementation of the OpenTelemetry standard.
      • See also https://opentelemetry.io/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenTelemetry-Exporter-OTLP - An OpenTelemetry Protocol exporter.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OpenTelemetry::Instrumentation - Top-level interface for OpenTelemetry instrumentations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenTelemetry-Instrumentation-namespace - OpenTelemetry instrumentation for a namespace.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenTelemetry-SDK - An implementation of the OpenTelemetry SDK for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OpenTracing::Interface - API definition for OpenTransport implementations. But see OpenTelemetry.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OpenTracing::Implementation::DataDog - Sending traces to DataDog using Agent. But see OpenTelemetry.
    • OrgDocHandling
      • OfficeFiles - JsonStuff.
      • OrgDocHandling
      • http://orgmode.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::orgadb - An opinionated Org addressbook toolset.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-OrgUtils - Some utilities for Org documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OpenAPI::Linter - Validate and lint OpenAPI specifications.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OpenAPI-Modern - Validate HTTP requests and responses against an OpenAPI v3.1 document.
      • Contains JSON::Schema::Modern::Document::OpenAPI and JSON::Schema::Modern::Vocabulary::OpenAPI
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Org::Document - Represent an Org document.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Org-Parser - Parse Org documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Org-To-HTML - Export Org document to HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/OrgINI - OrgINI file format specification.
    • PacketCapture
      • MailingLists - EmailStuff.
      • NetworkProgramming
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-PCAP - A pure Perl library to handle libpcap based files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Image-ExifTool - Read CAP, PCAP and PCAPNG Packet Capture files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Metabrik-Repository - forensic::pcap Brik.
      • This is part of the vast Metabrik repo
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Inspect - Library for inspection of data on various network layers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Pcap - Interface to the pcap(3) LBL packet capture library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Pcap-Easy - Net::Pcap is awesome, but it's difficult to bootstrap.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sniffer-HTTP - Multi-connection sniffer driver.
    • ParallelProcessing
      • CachingStuff - For Data::*::Shared - BenchmarkingTools.
      • ChildProcesses
      • [[DFA]]
      • EventStuff
      • ExceptionHandling
      • [[Graphs]]
      • [[HTTPHandling]]
      • [[HTTPServers]]
      • PipeLines
      • PoeStuff
      • WebServices
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Consumer-NonBlock - Send data between processes without blocking.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Coro - The only real threads in perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Coro::LWP - Make LWP non-blocking - as much as possible.
      • The pod discusses AnyEvent::HTTP, AnyEvent::HTTP::LWP::UserAgent, LWP::Protocol::AnyEvent::http, LWP::Protocol::Coro::http
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Coro::Multicore - Make coro threads on multiple cores with specially supported modules.
      • This module uses AnyEvent and Coro
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Coro::Select - A (slow but coro-aware) replacement for CORE::select.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Fetch - Prime method calls for background execution using threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-PubSub-Shared - High-performance shared-memory pub/sub for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-ReqRep-Shared - High-performance shared-memory request/response IPC for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-Manager - Decentralized local IPC through various protocols.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Manager::Client - Base class for all client protocols.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-Manager-Client-SharedMem - SysV shared memory as a message store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-Manager-Client-UnixSocket - Use UNIX sockets for message transfers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-Manager-DBI - Database based clients for IPC::Manager.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-ReadpipeX - Also, see Also within that module's POD.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::Run3 - Run a subprocess with input/output redirection.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPC-System-Options - Perl's system(), readpipe()/qx, IPC::Run's run(), start() (with more options).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/IPC::System::Simple - Run commands simply, with detailed diagnostics.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Linux::Epoll - O(1) multiplexing for Linux.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MCE - Many-Core Engine for Perl providing parallel processing capabilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Minion - Job queue.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/NBI-Slurm - NBI Slurm module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Async-MCP - Async MCP (Model Context Protocol) client for IO::Async.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Waiter - Compact INET socket server.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parallel-DataPipe - Parallel data processing conveyor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parallel-ForkManager - A simple parallel processing fork manager.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parallel::Iterator - Simple parallel execution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parallel::Loops - Execute loops using parallel forked subprocesses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parallel-Pipes - Parallel processing using pipe(2) for communication and synchronization.
      • Contains Parallel::Pipes::App
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parallel::Prefork - A simple prefork server framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parallel-Subs - Simple way to run subs in parallel and process their return value in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parallel-WorkUnit/view/README.pod - Provide multi-paradigm forking with ability to pass back data.
      • There are many other Parallel::* modules on CPAN
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/POE-Loop-EV - A bridge that supports EV from POE.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/POSIX::AtFork - Hook registrations at fork(2).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/prefork - Optimized module loading for forking or non-forking processes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Protocol-DBus - D-Bus in pure Perl.
      • Includes Protocol::DBus::Client::Mojo, among others
      • See also https://dbus.freedesktop.org/doc/dbus-specification.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Resque - Redis-backed library for creating background jobs, placing them on multiple queues, and processing them later.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-Genius - Declarative concurrency planning for sequential Perl execution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/subs::parallel - Enables subroutines to seamlessly run in parallel.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Bless - Make blessed objects thread-aware.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Thread::Conveyor - Transport of any data-structure between threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Conveyor-Monitored - Monitor a belt for specific content.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-CSP - Communicating sequential processes threading for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Deadlock - Report deadlocks with stacktrace.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Exit - Provide thread-local exit(), BEGIN {}, END {} and exited().
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Needs - Remove unneeded modules from CLONEd memory.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Thread::Pool - Group of threads for performing similar jobs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Pool-Resolve - Resolve logs asynchronously.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Queue-Any - Thread-safe queues for any data-structure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Queue-Any-Monitored - Monitor a queue for any specific content.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Rand - Repeatable random sequences between threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Running - Provide non-blocking check whether threads are running.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Thread::Serialize - Serialize data-structures between threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Status - Report stack status of all running threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Subs - Execute selected subs concurrently in worker threads.
      • The POD is very sophisticated
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Synchronized - Synchronize subroutine calls between threads.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Thread-Use - Use a module inside a thread only.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/threads-farm - Group of threads for performing similar jobs.
    • ParsingStuff
      • DataTraversal - [[CPAN]].
      • [[Marpa]]
      • PerlInternals
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • http://www.kate-editor.org
      • https://lindat.mff.cuni.cz/services/udpipe/
      • https://ufal.mff.cuni.cz/udpipe
      • https://universaldependencies.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Analizo - Multi-language source code analysis toolkit.
      • analizo is a suite of source code analysis tools, aimed at being language-independent and extensible
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Attean - A Semantic Web Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DMS-Parser - Pure-Perl parser for DMS, a data syntax with strong typing, ordered maps, multi-line heredocs, and front-matter metadata.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DMS-Parser-XS - XS (C-backed) parser for DMS, a data syntax with strong typing, ordered maps, multi-line heredocs, and front-matter metadata.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DMS-XS-Parser - Thin Perl shim over the C DMS parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Grammar::Improver - A Perl module for improving grammar using LanguageTool API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Word::Parser - Parse a word into scored known and unknown parts.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::LocalDistribution - Parses local .pm files as PAUSE does.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::PMFile - Parses .pm file as PAUSE does.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::Taxonomy - Validate hierarchical data stored in CSV format.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Parser-MGC - Build simple recursive-descent parsers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pegex - Acmeist PEG Parser Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl500503Syntax-OrDie - Validate that source code is compatible with Perl 5.005_03.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Query::Tags - Raku-inspired query language for attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Highlight-Engine-Kate - A port to Perl of the syntax highlight engine of the Kate text editor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Kamelon - A versatile and fully programmable textual content parser that is extremely well suited for syntax highlighting and code folding.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Ufal::UDPipe - Bindings to UDPipe library http://ufal.mff.cuni.cz/udpipe.
    • PdfStuff
      • PDF::FromHTML - Below. Contains html2pdf.pl. Does not handle CSS - TemplateStuff.
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • WWW::Mechanize::Chrome::Webshot - Below. Converts HTML to PDF, with CSS. Uses Chrome
      • https://mupdf.com/
      • https://qpdf.sourceforge.io/ - Includes qpdf and qpdfview (a browser for PDFs)
      • https://tools.pdf24.org/en/ - Offers a huge array of free online PDF tools
      • - .
      • https://opensource.adobe.com/dc-acrobat-sdk-docs/pdfstandards/pdfreference1.7old.pdf - Versions of PDF are 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7 and now 2.0.
      • There was never a public release using version number 1.06 (from an email from Deven T. Corzine)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-PDFio - Alien distribution for PDFio.
      • See PDFio::FFI below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::DiffPDFText - Diff the text of two PDF documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Mxpress-PDF - A utility application for generating PDFs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::paperback - Copy and transform pages from a PDF into a new PDF.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PDFLibrarian - Manage a library of academic papers in PDF format with embedded BibTeX metadata.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::pdfresize - Resize each page of PDF file to a new dimension.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PDFUtils - Command-line utilities related to PDF files.
      • Includes 10 utilities
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CAM::PDF - PDF manipulation library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CtrlO-PDF - High level PDF creator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mojo::PDF - Generate PDFs with the goodness of Mojo!.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mxpress::PDF - A factory package and the entry point for Mxpress::PDF::* objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-API2 - Create, modify, and examine PDF files.
      • See PDF::API2::Resource::XObject::Image::Imager for important comments about PDF::API2
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-API2-Resource-XObject-Image-Imager - Import Imager images into PDF.
      • https://github.polettix.it/ETOOBUSY/2022/11/07/a-pdf-workaround/ - Facilitates the creation and modification of PDF files.
      • Use PDF::Builder via JSON config: https://codeberg.org/polettix/pdfunnel
      • See also https://www.catskilltech.com/FreeSW/product/PDF-Builder/title/PDF::Builder/freeSW_full
      • Example code: https://www.catskilltech.com/Examples/PDF/Builder.html
      • https://github.polettix.it/ETOOBUSY/2023/05/09/svg-text-elements/ - Layer stuff, e.g. SVG images, over PDFs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDF::Cairo - Simple API for creating PDF files using the Cairo library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDF::Create - Create PDF files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDF::CreateSimple - Create simple PDF files using a set of easy-to-use functions.
      • Can read and write in OO style - Manipulate PDF files and objects as data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Data
      • Includes pdf_data for dumping stuff
      • For a huge amount of info, see email from Deven T. Corzine, Friday 13:22 2024-09-27
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-FacturX - Generate Factur-X / ZUGFeRD-compatible PDF/A-3 invoices.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-FromHTML - Convert HTML documents to PDF.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDF::Haru - Perl interface to Haru Free PDF Library. Haru is a free, cross platform, open-sourced software library for generating PDF.
      • Supports Chinese, Japanese, Korean
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDF::Report - A wrapper written for PDF::API2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Reuse - Reuse and mass produce PDF documents.
      • This module has an important See Also
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Reuse-Barcode - Create barcodes for PDF documents with PDF::Reuse.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Reuse-OverlayChart - Produce simple or mixed charts with PDF::Reuse.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Reuse-Tutorial - How to produce PDF-files with PDF::Reuse.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Sign - Sign PDF files with CMS/CAdES signatures and RFC3161 timestamps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-Table - A utility class for building table layouts in a PDF::Builder (or PDF::API2) object.
      • See also https://www.catskilltech.com/FreeSW/product/PDF-Table/title/PDF::Table/freeSW_full
      • Example code: https://www.catskilltech.com/Examples/PDF/Table.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDF-WebKit - Use WebKit to Generate PDFs from HTML (via wkhtmltopdf).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDFio-Architect - Creating and manipulating PDF files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDFio::FFI - Perl FFI bindings for the PDFio C library.
      • It allows you to read, write, and manipulate PDF files from Perl by exposing the PDFio API
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Poppler - Bindings to the poppler PDF rendering library.
      • http://poppler.freedesktop.org
      • Could not install this under Debian bookworm even after using
      • cpanm ExtUtils::PkgConfig and
      • sudo apt-get install libpoppler-glib-dev and
      • sudo apt-get install gobject-introspection and
      • PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu and
      • PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/share and
      • cpanm Glib::Object::Introspection The latter fails with
      • Package gobject-introspection-1.0 was not found in the pkg-config search path
      • Sigh
      • But see PkgConfig - Pure-Perl Core-Only replacement for pkg-config
      • https://qpdf.sourceforge.io/ - QPDF is a command-line tool and C++ library that performs content-preserving transformations on PDF files.
      • Here is the Git repository for qpdf: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf
      • Documentation for qpdf is here: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
      • Debian-specific notes follow. I'm using bookworm
      • libqpdf29 is installed by default for some reason
      • qpdf and qpdfview and libqpdf-dev can be installed with apt-get
      • qpfdview is a very convenient multi-doc (tabbed) viewer
      • Sample code, given x.pdf is say in v 1.6 format s output by LibreOffice
      • qpdf --force-version=1.4 x.pdf x-1.4.pdf
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SVGPDF - Create PDF XObject from SVG data.
      • This module processes SVG data and produces one or more PDF XObjects to be placed in a PDF document. This module is intended to be used with PDF::Builder, PDF::API2 and compatible PDF packages
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Mechanize-Chrome-Webshot - Cheap and cheerful html2pdf converter, take a screenshot of rendered HTML, complete with CSS and Javascript.
    • PdlLanguage
      • GeographicStuff - ChartingAndPlotting.
      • MathsLibraries
      • TheLanguageGuide
      • https://pdl.perl.org/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CXC-PDL-Bin1D - One Dimensional Binning Utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL - The Perl Data Language.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Drawing-Prima - PDL-aware drawing functions for Prima widgets.
      • Object-oriented interface from perl/PDL to the PLPLOT plotting library - https://metacpan.org/pod/PDL::Graphics::PLplot.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Fit - Various fitting implementations for PDL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Fit-Levmar - Levenberg-Marquardt fit/optimization routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-GSL - PDL interface to the GNU Scientific Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-LinearAlgebra - Linear Algebra utils for PDL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Minuit - A PDL interface to the Minuit library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDL::NiceSlice - Toward a nicer slicing syntax for PDL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Opt-GLPK - PDL interface to the GNU Linear Programming Kit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Opt-Simplex-Simple - A simplex optimizer for the rest of us (who may not know PDL).
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simplex_algorithm
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Perldl2 - Simple shell (version 2) for PDL.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PDL-Stats - A collection of statistics modules in Perl Data Language, with a quick-start guide for non-PDL people.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDL::Transform::Cartography - Useful cartographic projections.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PDL::Transform::Proj4 - PDL::Transform interface to the Proj4 projection library.
    • Perl6
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl6::Form - Implements the Perl 6 'form' built-in.
      • Requires: Perl 5.8.0
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl6::Slurp - Implements the Perl 6 'slurp' built-in.
      • Requires: Perl 5.8.0
    • PerlDeltas
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Construct - This module documents which features (incl regexps) first appeared in which version of Perl.
      • Explicitly state which non-feature constructs are used in the code
    • PerlDistros
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-perl-distrolint - Distribution-wide linting tools for Perl distributions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Staticperl - Perl, libc, 100 modules, all in one standalone 500kb file .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PerlBench - Compare several perls' performances.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::Dist::APPerl - Actually Portable Perl.
      • This distro runs on several x86_64 operating systems via the same binary
      • It builds to a single binary with perl modules packed inside of it

      • - FeaturesAndPragmas.

      • https://github.com/thibaultduponchelle/perlres - List of resources about Perl

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlguts - For the Introduction to the Perl API
      • https://lists.perl.org/all.html - For Perl Mailing Lists - Alphabetical
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::sdview::Output::Tickit - Interactive terminal-based viewer for App::sdview
      • https://niceperl.blogspot.com/ - Recent modules
      • http://perldoc.jp/docs/modules/ - Japanese
      • https://man.he.net/man3/- Searchable man pages
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlsecret - For Perl secret operators and constants
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/perlpodspec
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ManUtils - Utilities related to man(page) .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sdview - A terminal document viewer for Pod and other syntaxes.
      • Contains sdview which supports POD and Markdown
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Perl-V - Structured data retrieval of perl -V output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-CoreList - What modules shipped with versions of perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/perlfaq - Frequently asked questions about Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlxs - The XS Language Reference Manual.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlxstut - Tutorial for writing XSUBs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PLS - Implements features of the Language Server Protocol for Perl 5.
      • See also https://github.com/FractalBoy/perl-language-server
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-podweaver/view/script/podweaver - Run Pod::Weaver on a file or distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Prima - a Perl graphic toolkit.
      • Contains podview
    • PerlInternals

      • https://lists.perl.org/all.html - For Perl Mailing Lists - Alphabetical

      • https://www.nntp.perl.org/group/perl.perl5.porters/2023/01/msg265583.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B - The Perl Compiler Backend.
      • Used by ogd, below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::CC - Perl compiler's optimized C translation backend.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Concise - Walk Perl syntax tree, printing concise info about ops.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Deparse - Perl compiler backend to produce perl code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Hooks::AtRuntime - Lower blocks from compile time to runtime.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/B-Hooks-AtRuntime-OnlyCoreDependencies - It is just B::Hooks::AtRuntime but with only core dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Hooks::EndOfScope - Execute code after a scope finished compilation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Hooks::EndOfScope::XS - Execute code after a scope finished compilation - XS implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Hooks::OP::Check - Wrap OP check callbacks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/B::Hooks::Parser - Interface to perl's parser variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/B-Keywords - Lists of reserved barewords and symbol names.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/B-Lint - Perl lint - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CXC::Exporter::Util - Tagged Based Exporting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-MAT - Perl Memory Analysis Tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Devel-PatchPerl - Patch perl source a la Devel::PPPort's buildperl.pl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Typemaps-Magic - Typemap for storing objects in magic.
      • See also Variable::Magic below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ExtUtils-Typemaps-Signal - A typemap bundle for dealing with signal related types.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Filter::Simple - Simplified source filtering.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Filter - Perl Source Filter Utility Module.
      • Contains 8 Filter::* modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hook::AfterRuntime - Run code at the end of the compiling scope's runtime.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Internals - Write-protect variables, manipulate refcounts.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ogd - Ordered global destruction.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Opcode - Disable named opcodes when compiling perl code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Opcodes - More Opcodes information from opnames.h and opcode.h.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PadWalker - Play with other peoples' lexical variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl5-TestEachCommit - Test each commit in a pull request to Perl core.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PPI - Parse, Analyze and Manipulate Perl (without perl).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Safe - Compile and execute code in restricted compartments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scope::OnExit - Running code on scope exit - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Exporter - A sophisticated exporter for custom-built routines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sub-Exporter-Lexical - To export lexically-available subs with Sub::Exporter.
      • Magic is Perl's way of enhancing variables - https://metacpan.org/pod/Variable::Magic.
      • This mechanism lets the user add extra data to any variable and hook syntactical operations (such as access, assignment or destruction) that can be applied to it
      • With this module, you can add your own magic to any variable without having to write a single line of XS
      • See also ExtUtils::Typemaps::Magic above

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Phone - Base class for Number::Phone::* modules.
      • This module contains dozens of sub-modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Phone-Valid-International-Loose - Loosely validate international phone numbers via a regex.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::App::File - Serve static files from root directory.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Component - Base class for PSGI endpoints.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Handler-H2 - High-performance HTTP/2 server handler for Plack.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Handler::Twiggy - Adapter for Twiggy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware - Base class for easy-to-use PSGI middleware.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-BlockHeaderInjection - Block header injections in responses .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Debug - Display information about the current request/response.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::DebugLogging - Catalyst style console debugging for plack apps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::DirIndex - Middleware to use with Plack::App::Directory and the like.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-DirIndex-Htaccess - Serve default html files for directory URLS. Includes checking of .htaccess file for DirectoryIndex.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-DirListing - Display a listing of a directory in html.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Expires - mod_expires for plack (see Apache webserver).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Headers - Modify HTTP response headers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::HTTPExceptions - Catch HTTP exceptions.
      • - Plack middleware to setup OpenTelemetry spans.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-ProofOfWork - Proof-of-Work based bot protection for Plack applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::ReverseProxy - Supports app to run as a reverse proxy backend.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Security-Simple - A simple security filter for Plack.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Session - Middleware for session management.
      • Contains Plack::Session
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Session::Cookie - Session middleware that saves session data in the cookie.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Session::SerializedCookie - Session middleware that saves session data in the customizable serialized.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Session-Simple - Make Session Simple.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Static - Serve static files with Plack.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Middleware::Static::OpenFileCache - Plack::Middleware::Static with open file cache.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Statsd - Send statistics to statsd.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Text-Minify - Remove HTML indentation on the fly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Middleware-Zitadel - Verify Bearer tokens via ZITADEL OIDC in Plack apps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Request - Portable HTTP request object from PSGI env hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plack::Response - Portable HTTP Response object for PSGI response.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PlackX-Framework - A thin framework for PSGI/Plack web apps.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebServer-DirIndex - Directory index data for web server listings.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::podtohtml - Convert POD to HTML.
      • Provides podtohtml
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sdview - A terminal-based structured document viewer for POD and other formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Abstract - Abstract document tree for Perl POD documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-AsciiDoctor - Convert from POD to AsciiDoc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-CopyrightYears - Object for copyright years changing in POD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Coverage - Checks if the documentation of a module is comprehensive.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Coverage-TrustMe - Pod::Coverage but more powerful.
      • A- https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Coverage::TrustPod - Allow a module's pod to contain Pod::Coverage hints.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Example - Module for getting example from POD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Extract - Remove pod from file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Github - Convert POD to Github markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Html - Module to convert pod files to HTML.
      • See also Pod::L10N below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-L10N - Pod with L10N.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-LOL - Parse Pod into a list of lists (LOL).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Markdown - Convert POD to Markdown.
      • Uses Pod::Simple
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Markdown-Github - Convert POD to Github's specific markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Markdown::Githubert - Convert POD to Github-flavored Markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Parser - Base class for creating POD filters and translators.
      • But see Pod::Simple
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Perldoc-ToToc - Translate Pod to a Table of Contents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Query - Query pod documents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Markdown-Githubert - Convert POD to Github-flavored Markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Readme::Brief - A short simple README with just the essentials.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Simple - Framework for parsing Pod.
      • Recommended by Pod::Parser as being preferred to the latter
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Simple::HTML - Convert Pod to HTML.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Simple::Wiki - A class for creating Pod to Wiki filters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Spell - A formatter for spellchecking Pod.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Spell::CommonMistakes - Catches common typos in POD.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Spelling - Send POD to a spelling checker.
      • See also Test::Pod::Spelling
      • Uses Pod::Text::Color - https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Text::Color::Delight.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Usage - Extracts POD documentation and shows usage information.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Weaver - Weave together a Pod document from an outline.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Weaver-Section-SourceGitHub - Add SOURCE pod section for a github repository.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pod-Webserver - Minimal web server for local Perl documentation.
      • Contains podwebserver which listens on port 8020
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pod::Wordlist - English words that come up in Perl documentation.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-Assets-Import-CSV - RT Assets Import from CSV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-AttachmentViewer - View full size attachments from the dropzone.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-AutomaticAssignment - Automatically assign tickets based on rules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-AWS-Assets - Manage AWS resources in RT assets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-BooleanCustomField - CF with checkbox to set or unset its value.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-Captcha - Use Google reCAPTCHA v3 to verify users before some actions in RT.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ChangeManagement - Change Management configuration for RT.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-CommandByMail - Change ticket metadata via email.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-CommentOnCreate - Adds an optional Comment box to Ticket Creation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ConditionalCustomFields - CF conditioned by the value of another CF.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-CSAT - Customer Satisfaction Feedback extension for Request Tracker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-DynamicWebPath - Dynamic WebPath.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-EmailReplyDelimiter - Strip text from emails after a delimiter.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/RT::Extension::ExcelFeed - Generate RT reports in MS Excel XSLX format.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ExtractCustomFieldValues - Extract CF values from email headers or body.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-FormTools - Create multi-page ticket creation wizards for RT.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-GroupSummary - Display a summary of a group.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-HelpDesk - Default Help desk configuration for Request Tracker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-Import-CSV - Import data from a comma-separated value (CSV) file, or any other sort of delimited file, into RT.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-InlineAudioVideoAttachments - Display audio/video attachments inline.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-InlineHelp - Provides the ability to add inline help to RT web pages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-JSGantt - Gantt charts for your tickets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-MandatoryOnTransition - Require core fields and ticket custom fields on status transitions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-MandatorySubject - Enforce users to fill the subject when creating a ticket.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-Memo - Add a memo widget to tickets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-MergeUsers - Merges two users into the same effective user.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ModifyUsersPrefs - Allow modification of other users' preferences.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-PreviewInSearch - Preview tickets right from search results page.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-QuickAssign - Add owner change links on ticket display page.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-QuickCalls - Quickly create tickets in specific queues with default values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-RepeatTicket - Repeat tickets based on schedule.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-RepliesToResolved - Intercept replies to resolved tickets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ResetPassword - Add "forgot your password?" link to RT instance.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ShowTransactionSquelching - Show which users were squelched per transaction.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-SwitchUsers - Switch current logged in user to others.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-Tags - Provides simple tagging using custom fields.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-TicketLocking - Enables users to place advisory locks on tickets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-ToggleTheme - Toggle elevator light and dark theme.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RT-Extension-TOTPMFA - Multi-factor authentication with time-based one-time passcodes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RTIR-Extension-MISP - Integrate RTIR with MISP.
      • https://requesttracker.com/rtir/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/RTx-Calendar - Calendar view for RT ticket dates and custom fields.

      • - .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Dynamically - Dynamically change the value of a variable.
      • Effectively
      • Except that the old value will also be restored in the case of exceptions, go to, next/last/redo or similar ways to leave the controlling block scope.

    • SecurityPolicy
      • [[FAQ]] - For AI & policy - ApacheStuff.
      • SandBoxes
      • https://citizenlab.ca/ - For The Citizen Lab
      • https://security.metacpan.org/
      • https://www.icij.org/ - For The International Consortium of Investigative Journalists
      • https://www.cvedetails.com/
      • https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr31/ - Unicode Identifiers and Syntax
      • https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr39/ - Unicode Security Mechanisms
      • https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr55/ - Unicode Source Code Handling
      • - https://security.metacpan.org/docs/guides/security-policy-for-authors.html.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::SASL - SASL Authentication framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::SASL::Cyrus - XS SASL Authentication.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::SASL::Perl - Perl implementation of the SASL Authentication framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Authen-SASL-XS - XS code to glue Perl SASL to Cyrus SASL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CAPE::Utils - A helpful library for CAPE, a MS Windows open-source malware sandbox.
      • https://capev2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/what.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPANSA-DB - The CPAN Security Advisory data as a Perl data structure, mostly for CPAN::Audit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CSAF - Common Security Advisory Framework.
      • https://www.bitsight.com/glossary/common-vulnerabilities-and-exposures-cve - Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures.
      • https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/term/common_vulnerabilities_and_exposures
      • https://www.redhat.com/en/topics/security/what-is-cve
      • CPANSec has just been authorized as a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) for Perl and modules on CPAN (2025-02-26)
      • https://security.metacpan.org/2025/02/25/cpansec-is-cna-for-perl-and-cpan.html
      • This allows us to issue and manage CVEs for that scope, and to help facilitate coordinated disclosure. If you need a CVE you can request one by emailing
      • cve-request@security.metacpan.org.
      • If a third-party requests a CVE we will ensure that they have reached out to perl-security (or the CPAN author) first and facilitate a discussion if necessary
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CVSS - Perl extension for CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System) 2.0/3.x/4.0.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::OFAC - A Perl interface to the United States Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Specially Designated Nationals List (SDN).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Validate::Sanctions - Validate a name against sanctions lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-AutomationPolicy - Generate and parse distribution automation policies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-PolicyFiles - Generate CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-CVE - Fetch CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) information from cve.org.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::OSV - Search known vulnerabilities on the Open Source Vulnerabilities Database (OSV).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Software-Security-Policy - Packages that provide templated Security Policies.
    • SeismicAnalysis
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perlsac::rwsac - A module to read and write SAC files.
    • SeismicOS
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-SeismicUnixGui - A graphical user interface for Seismic Unix.
    • SemanticWeb
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Named_graph - [[Acronyms]] - For LMDB, Quadstore, SPARQL, Triplestore.
      • AtteanX::Store::DBI [Yes (SQL), Moderate]
      • AtteanX::Store::LMDB [Yes (Disk), Very High]
      • AtteanX::Store::Memory [No (Volatile), Extreme]
      • AtteanX::Store::Simple ]No (Volatile), High] - Not on MetaCPAN
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AtteanX-Store-DBI - Database quad-store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AtteanX-Store-LDF - Linked Data Fragment RDF store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AtteanX-Store-LMDB - LMDB-based RDF store.
      • This module operates at the Data Interchange (RDF) and Query (SPARQL) layers. It provides the physical location where the "Knowledge Graph" actually lives
      • Quadstores are typically queried using SPARQL
      • Common Quadstore Implementations are Apache Jena (TDB), Blazegraph, GraphDB, Virtuoso
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AtteanX::Store::Memory - Simple in-memory RDF store.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/AtteanX-Store-SPARQL - Attean SPARQL store.
    • SetStuff
      • HashHandling - For Hash::Util::Set - [[DFA]] - For Set::FA.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Bit-Vector - Efficient bit vector, set of integers and "big int" math library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Radamsa - Perl 5 bindings for the Radamsa mutational fuzzer.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Array - Arrays as objects with lots of handy methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Bag - Bag (multiset) class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::CrossProduct - Work with the cross product of two or more sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Infinite - Sets of intervals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Set-Light - (Memory efficient) unordered set of strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Object - A set of objects and strings.
      • Slowish. See Set::Light
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Product - Generates the cartesian product of a set of lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Product::XS - Speed up Set::Product.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Set-Relation/view/lib/Set/Relation.pod - Relation data type for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Scalar - Basic set operations.
      • Slowish. See Set::Light
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Set::Select - Select common subsets from two or more sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Set-CrossProduct - Work with the cross product of two or more sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Set-Tiny - Simple sets of strings.

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::bsky - A Command-line Bluesky Client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/At - The AT Protocol for Social Networking.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Bluesky - Bluesky Client Library in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Bluesky::Poster - Simple interface for posting to Bluesky (AT Protocol).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::SocialMeta - Module to generate Social Media Meta Tags.
      • It allows you to optimize sharing on platforms such as Twitter, Facebook, Google+ and Pinerest
    • SortModules
      • Contains App::sortsub - Sort lines of text using Sort::Sub routine.
      • Uses Sort::Sub below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-subsort
      • https://sorting.cr.yp.to/ - djbsort homepage
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/File-Util-Sort - Routines related to sorting files in one or more directories.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sort-DJB - Fast sorting using Daniel J. Bernstein's djbsort bitonic sorting networks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sort::Fields - Provides a general purpose technique for efficiently sorting lists of lines that contain data separated into fields.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sort-Maker - A simple way to make efficient sort subs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sort-Naturally - Sort lexically, but sort numeral parts numerically.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sort::Sub - Collection of sort subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sort-Versions - Sorting of revision-like numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SortExample-Letter-ByFrequency-ID - Ordered list of letters by usage frequency in Indonesian words.
    • SpreadsheetStuff
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting - DataTraversal.
      • Spreadsheet::Examples - App::diff_spreadsheets.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-Examples
      • Spreadsheet::Open
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-Open
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::XLSX::Parser - Faster XLSX parser.
      • See Excel::ValueReader::XLSX
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Excel::PowerPivot::Utils - Utilities for scripting Power Pivot models within Excel workbooks.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Excel-ValueReader-XLSX - Extracting values from Excel workbooks in XLSX format, fast.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Excel-ValueWriter-XLSX - Generating data-only Excel workbooks in XLSX format, fast.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Excel::Writer::XLSX - Create a new file in the Excel 2007+ XLSX format.
      • This module has a huge number of methods
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Excel-XLSX - Read and write Excel XLSX data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Number-Base-SpreadsheetColumn - Convert spreadsheet column name (e.g. "A", "Z", "AA") to number (e.g. 0, 25, 26) and vice versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-Edit - Slice and dice spreadsheets, optionally using tied variables.
      • Contains Spreadsheet::Edit::IO. Convert between spreadsheet and csv files
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Spreadsheet::Engine::Function::PROPER - Spreadsheet funtion PROPER().
      • Used by DBIx::HTML - Just another HTML table generator.
      • Transform Excel, HTML, JSON, CSV, YAML, PNG, JPEG and GIF files instantly into HTML tables
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-ParseExcel - Read information from an Excel file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Spreadsheet::ParseXLSX - Parse XLSX files (strongly discouraged).
      • See Excel::ValueReader::XLSX
      • Contains xlsx2csv - Read the data from a spreadsheet.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-Read
      • Spreadsheet::Read offers a uniformed wrapper to Spreadsheet::ParseExcel and Spreadheet::ReadSXC
      • For OpenOffice and/or LibreOffice this module uses Spreadsheet::ParseODS or Spreadsheet::ReadSXC
      • For Microsoft Excel this module uses Spreadsheet::ParseExcel, Spreadsheet::ParseXLSX, Excel::ValueReader::XLSX, or Spreadsheet::XLSX (strongly deprecated)
      • For CSV this module uses Text::CSV_XS or Text::CSV_PP
      • For Gnumeric this module uses Spreadsheet::ReadGnumeric
      • For SquirrelCalc there is a very simplistic built-in parser
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-Write - Writer for spreadsheet files (CSV, XLS, XLSX).
      • But see the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Spreadsheet-WriteExcel - Write to a cross-platform Excel binary file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Spreadsheet::Wright - Simple spreadsheet worker.
      • Writes files in CSV, Microsoft Excel, HTML and OpenDocument formats
      • Formats - 'csv', 'xls', 'xlsx', 'html', 'xhtml', 'xml', 'ods', 'json', or 'auto' (default)

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Re - More Perlish Regular Expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Re - Regular Expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Resource-Eigen - Resource for C++ Eigen library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Resource-Libpng - The Resource of The libpng Library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Resource::RE2 - The Resource of Google/RE2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Resource-SocketUtil - Resource for Socket Utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Resource-SQLite - A resource that provides the SQLite header and source code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Resource::Utf8proc - Bundle of the utf8proc library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Resource-Zlib - Zlib Resources.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Sys - System Calls for File IO, Sockets, Time, Process, Signals, Users.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Thread - Native Thread.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Time::Local - Reverse Operations of localtime and gmtime.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/SPVM::Time::Piece - Dates and Times.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Unicode - Unicode Utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SPVM-Unicode-Normalize - Normalizing UTF-8.
    • SSH
      • NetworkProgramming - ConfigFiles.
      • TestingHelp - For Test::SFTP
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-libssh - Find or build libssh, the SSH library .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-ClusterSSH - Cluster administration tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-RemoteCommand - Simple remote command launcher via ssh.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-sshca - A minimal SSH Certificate Authority.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Libssh-Session - Support for the SSH protocol via libssh..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::FullAuto - Fully Automate any workload with *persistent* SSH/SFTP from one host.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-LibSSH - Perl binding for libssh â SSH without SFTP dependency.
      • Perl SSH client package implemented on top of OpenSSH - https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::OpenSSH.
      • POD contains this comparison: Net::OpenSSH Vs. Net::SSH::.* modules
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SFTP::Foreign - SSH File Transfer Protocol client.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SSH2 - Support for the SSH 2 protocol via libssh2.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SSH::Any - SSH client module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-SSH-Perl - Perl client Interface to SSH.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SSH2::SFTP - SSH 2 Secure FTP object.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Aphra - A simple static sitebuilder in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::sitelenmute - A static image gallery creator.
    • StatisticsStuff
      • MathsLibraries - CryptoStuff.
      • [[UUID]]
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Acme-Selection-RarestFirst - Rarest-First Selection Algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-freqtable - Print frequency table of lines/words/characters/bytes/numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::wordstat - Return word statistics of a text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Statistics-CaseResampling - Efficient resampling and calculation of medians with confidence intervals.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Math-LiveStats - Pure perl module to make mean, standard deviation, and p-values available for given window sizes in streaming data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Stats-LikeR - Get basic statistical functions, like in R, but with Perl using XS for performance.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Statistics-Descriptive - Module of basic descriptive statistical functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Statistics-Descriptive-PDL - A close to drop-in replacement for Statistics::Descriptive using PDL as the back-end.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Statistics::Distributions - Perl module for calculating critical values and upper probabilities of common statistical distributions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Statistics-Krippendorff - Calculate Krippendorff's alpha.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Statistics::Running - Basic descriptive statistics (mean/stdev/min/max/skew/kurtosis) and discrete Probability Distribution (via histogram) over data without the need to store data points.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Statistics-Running-Tiny - Basic descriptive statistics (mean/stdev/min/max/skew/kurtosis) over data without the need to store data points.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Statistics::Welford - Standard statistics using Welford's algorithm.
    • StradaStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Strada - Call compiled Strada shared libraries from Perl.
    • StreamingMedia
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-MHFS - A Media HTTP File Server. Stream your own music and video library via your browser and standard media players.
    • StringStuff
      • DataValidation - DataTraversal.
      • EmailStuff
      • [[HTMLHandling]]
      • TemplateStuff
      • TestingHelp
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Basic::Coercion::XS - Creates a coercion type that converts a string into an array reference.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Binary::Interpolation - Make it easier to interpolate binary bytes into a string.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Checker - An extensible string validation module (allowing commonly used checks on strings to be called more concisely and consistently).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-CommonPrefix - Find prefix common to all strings.
      • Also contains String::CommonSuffix
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::CRC32C - Castagnoli CRC.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Elide::Lines - Elide lines from a string, with options.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Elide::Parts - Elide a string with multiple parts of different priorities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Elide::Tiny - A very simple text truncating function, elide().
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Expand - String utility functions for expanding variables in self-referential sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Fuzzy - Python-style fuzzy string matching (fuzzywuzzy port).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Interpolate - Wrapper for builtin the Perl interpolation engine..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Interpolate::RE - Interpolate variables into strings using regular expressions.
      • - Variable interpolation, shell style.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Lookup - Convert strings to ID's authoritatively and vice-versa.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::MatchInterpolate - Named regexp capture and interpolation from the same template.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Print - printf alternative.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Random-Regexp-regxstring - Generate random strings from a regular expression.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Redactable - A string that automatically redacts itself.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Scanf - Emulate sscanf() of the C library.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Sprintf - Custom overloading of sprintf.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Super - Compactor for superstrings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Tagged - String buffers with value tags on extents.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Tagged/view/lib/String/Tagged/Formatting.pod - An API specification for simple formatted strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Tagged-HTML - Handle HTML content using String::Tagged.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Trim::More - Various string trimming utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Truncate - A module for when strings are too long to be displayed in....
      • About 22 of them - String processing utility functions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Util
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Util-Match - String utilities related to matching.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Elide - Perl module with simple "word" aware string truncating routine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Fuzzy - Partial string matching using edit distances.
      • It handles both byte strings and character strings (strings containing Unicode), treating each Unicode character as a single entity
      • The POD contains a great list of string processing modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Levenshtein - Calculate the Levenshtein edit distance between two strings.
      • Contains a marvellous list of similar modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Levenshtein-Damerau - Damerau Levenshtein edit distance.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Levenshtein::Flexible - XS Levenshtein distance calculation with bounds and costs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Levenshtein::XS - Calculate edit distance based on insertion, deletion, and substitution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::sprintfn - Drop-in replacement for sprintf(), with named parameter support.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Sprintf::Named - sprintf-like function with named conversions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Truncate - Perl module with simple string truncating routine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Wrap - Line wrapping to form simple paragraphs.
    • SwitchStatements
      • ExceptionHandling - for Syntax::Keyword::Try - ArrayHandling.
      • FeaturesAndPragmas
      • ModuleBuilding
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zombie - A reference to smartmatch
      • [[XS]]
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dispatch-Class - Dispatch on the type (class) of an argument.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Exception::Class::TryCatch - Syntactic try/catch sugar for use with Exception::Class.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Feature::Compat::Try - Make try/catch syntax available.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hustle::Table - Cached general purpose dispatch and routing table.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/match-simple - Simplified clone of smartmatch operator.
      • Obsolete. See Syntax::Operator::Matches below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Multi-Dispatch - Multiple dispatch for Perl subs and methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Nice::Try - A real Try Catch Block Implementation Using Perl Filter.
      • Very comprehensive. Works well with Want. See WantArray
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Smart::Dispatch - First-class switch statements.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Struct::Match - Exact Match (SCALAR|HASH|ARRAY)'s.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::MultiMethod - Yet another implementation of multimethods.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Switch::Again - Keyword for either generating a reusable switch statement or calling the generated switch function directly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Switch-Back - Given/when for a post-given/when Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Switch::Right - Switch and smartmatch done right this time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Feature::Try - Try/catch/finally statement for exception handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Infix-Smartmatch - Requires Module::Build::Tiny which will not install under GCC V 8.3.0.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Match - A match/case syntax for perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Try - A try/catch/finally syntax for perl.
      • Cannot do some things Nice::Try can do
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Syntax-Operator-Matches - match::simple but as a real infix operator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::MultiSub - Multiple dispatch on subroutines.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Switch::Plain - A simple switch statement for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TryCatch - First class try catch semantics for Perl, without source filters..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Try-ALRM - Try/catch-style semantics for handling timeouts using CORE::alarm.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Try::Catch - Try Catch exception handler based on Try::Tiny but faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Try::Tiny - Minimal try/catch with proper preservation of $@.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Try::Tiny::Tiny - Slim Try::Tiny down as far as reasonably possible.
    • SystemInfo
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Nix-Proc-Meminfo - Access /proc/meminfo with core classes.
      • https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/proc_meminfo.5.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::CpuLoad - Retrieve system load averages.
      • Note: On Debian I can run w, which is /usr/bin/w
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-Export - Export a subset of an OS file tree, for chroot/initrd.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-Info-Base - Base class for Sys::Info.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Info::Device::CPU - Collects and returns information about the Central Processing Unit (CPU) on the host machine.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-Info-Driver-Linux - Supplies detailed operating system information.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Hostname - Try every conceivable way to get hostname.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-Hostname-Long - Try every conceivable way to get full hostname.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Info - Extracts and collects information from the host system.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Info::Device - This is an interface to the available devices such as the CPU.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/System::CPU - Cross-platform CPU information / topology with no dependencies.
      • Pure Perl
      • Has notes about old code such as System::Info below and
      • Unix::Processors, Sys::Info, various *::Sysinfo
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/System-Info - Factory for system specific information objects.
      • The POD contains a good list of alternatives

      • AstroStuff - ApacheStuff.
