      • WebServices
      • https://lemonldap-ng.org/ - For a modular WebSSO (Single Sign On)
      • https://suricata.io/ - For open source network analysis and threat detection
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-API - A generic base class for AWS Services.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-CreatorsAPI - The Amazon Creators API Helper.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-S3 - A portable client library for working with and managing Amazon S3 buckets and keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-S3-Lite - A lightweight Amazon S3 client for common operations.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Amazon::S3::Thin - A thin, lightweight, low-level Amazon S3 client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-Signature4-Lite - Lightweight AWS Signature Version 4 signing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-Sites - A class to represent Amazon sites.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-FargateStack - A lightweight deployment framework for Amazon ECS on Fargate.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::ipinfo - A command-line tool for IPinfo.io.
      • Interface to https://ipinfo.io/ - Accurate IP address data that keeps pace with secure, specific, and forward-looking use cases
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-rdapper - A simple console-based RDAP client.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-traveller - A webserver that serves Traveller RPG maps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/AWS::Signature4 - Create a version4 signature for Amazon Web Services.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HV-Monitor - A generalized module for gathering stats for a hypervisor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/IPCamera-Reolink - Reolink API provides access to the System, Security, Network, Video input, Enc, Record, PTZ, and Alarm functions of a Reolink IP Camera or NVR via HTTP(S)/REST.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lemonldap-NG-Common - Common files for Lemonldap::NG infrastructure.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lemonldap-NG-Handler - The Apache protection module part of Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lemonldap-NG-Manager - Perl extension for managing Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system.
      • Web Single Sign On and Access Management Software
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lemonldap-NG-Portal - The authentication portal part of Lemonldap::NG Web-SSO system.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Map::Metro - Public transport graphing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Map::Metro::Graph - An entire graph.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Metro-Plugin-Map-Berlin - Map::Metro map for Berlin.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Metro-Plugin-Map-Lyon - Map::Metro map for Lyon.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Map::Tube - Lightweight Routing Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Tube-Berlin - Interface to the Berlin S- and U-Bahn map.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Tube-CLI - Command Line Interface for Map::Tube::* map.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Map::Tube::GraphViz - GraphViz output for Map::Tube.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Tube-Paris - Interface to the Paris metro, RER, Transiliens, and tram lines map.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Map::Tube::Plugin::Graph - Graph plugin for Map::Tube.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Tube-Rome - Interface to the Rome tube map.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Map-Tube-Sydney - Interface to the Sydney Rail Network.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Monitoring-Livestatus - Perl API for check_mk livestatus to access runtime data from Nagios and Icinga.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Monitoring::Sneck - A boopable LibreNMS JSON style SNMP extend for remotely running nagios style checks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Amazon::S3 - Use the Amazon S3 - Simple Storage Service.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Cloudflare-DNS - DNS API for Cloudflare API v4.
      • https://docs.fortinet.com - Fortinet FortiManager REST API client library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::LDAP - Lightweight Directory Access Protocol.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Ping - Check a remote host for reachability.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::Ping::External - Cross-platform Perl interface to "ping" utilities.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::RDAP - Provides an interface to the Registration Data Access Protocol.
      • RDAP is replacing Whois as the preferred way of obtainining information about Internet resources (IP addresses, autonymous system numbers, and domain names)
      • See also App::rdapper
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-RDAP-Server - An RDAP server framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Whois-IANA - A universal WHOIS data extractor.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Net-Whois-Raw - Get Whois information of domains and IP addresses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/OSLV-Monitor - OS level virtualization monitoring extend for LibreNMS.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Paws - A Perl SDK for AWS (Amazon Web Services) APIs.
      • The POD has a huge list of services
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Paws::S3::PutObject - Arguments for method PutObject on Paws::S3.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PawsX-S3-Uploader - Upload to S3 from a streaming source.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Suricata-Ping - Reads in a suricata config and sends a ping to the specified interface.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Monitor::Lite - Lightweight system monitoring toolkit with JSON output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sys-OsPackage - Install OS packages and determine if CPAN modules are packaged for the OS.
      • See also https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Pacman/Rosetta
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Syscall - Access system calls that Perl doesn't normally provide access to.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Task::Map::Tube - Install the Map::Tube modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-Map-Tube-Bundle - Bundles Map::Tube::* map packages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Task-Map-Tube-Metro - Install the Map::Tube concrete metro modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Task::MapMetro::Maps - Map::Metro maps in a bundle.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Routing-DE-DBRIS - Interface to the bahn.de itinerary service.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Routing-DE-VRR - Unofficial interface to EFA-based itinerary services.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Routing-DE-VRR - Unofficial interface to the efa.vrr.de German itinerary service.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Status-DE-DBRIS - Interface to bahn.de / bahnhof.de departure monitors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Status-DE-HAFAS - Interface to HAFAS-based online arrival/departure monitors.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Status-DE-IRIS - Interface to IRIS based web departure monitors.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Travel::Status::MOTIS - An interface to the MOTIS routing service.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Travel-Status-DE-VRR - Unofficial VRR departure monitor.
    • TabularData
      • LinguaStuff - DataTraversal.
      • ModuleBuilding - For Role::TinyCommons etc
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::ByClass::Adjective - Indonesian adjective words from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ArrayData-Lingua-Word-ID-KBBI-ByClass-Verb - Indonesian verb words from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ArrayData-Lingua-Word-ID-KBBI-FigureOfSpeech - Indonesian figure of speech (kiasan) entries from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia), 3e.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::Proverb - Indonesian proverb (peribahasa) entries from KBBI (Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia), 3e.
      • Contains the same data as WordList::ID::KBBI::Proverb
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData - Specification for TableData::*, modules that contains table data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TableDataBundle-Lingua-Word-EN-ByClass-Adjective - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English adjectives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TableDataBundle-Lingua-Word-EN-ByClass-Adverb - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English adverbs .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TableDataBundle-Lingua-Word-EN-ByClass-Noun - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain English nouns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableDataBundle::Perl::CPAN::Release - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain CPAN releases data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableDataBundle::Perl::Release - Collection of TableData:: modules that contain Perl releases data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableDataBundle::Software::License - Collection of TableData:: modules related to software license.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Adjective::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adjectives only, from talkenglish.com.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Adverb::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adverbs only, from talkenglish.com.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::ByClass::Adverb::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as adverbs only, from talkenglish.com.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData::Lingua::Word::EN::Noun::TalkEnglish - List of words that are used as nouns only, from talkenglish.com.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableData::Locale::US::State - US states.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TableDataRole::Spec::Basic - Basic interface for all TableData::* modules.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TableDataRoles-Standard - Standard set of roles for TableData .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WordList::ID::KBBI::ByClass::Adverb - Indonesian adverb words from Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia (KBBI), 3e.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordList-ID-KBBI-Proverb - Indonesian proverb (peribahasa) entries from Kamus Besar Bahasa Indonesia (KBBI), 3e.
      • Contains the same data as ArrayData::Lingua::Word::ID::KBBI::Proverb
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordListBundle-EN-ByCategory - Collection of English words of various categories.
      • Contains Animal, Bird, Flower, Food, Fruit, Insect, MusicalInstrument, Vegetable, WaterAnimal
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordListBundle-EN-ByClass-Adjective - Collection of English adjectives.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordListBundle-EN-ByClass-Adverb - Collection of English adverbs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordListBundle-EN-ByClass-Noun - Collection of English nouns.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WordListBundle-ID-ByCategory - Collection of Indonesian words of various categories.
      • Contains Animal, Bird, Flower, Food, Fruit, Insect, MusicalInstrument, Vegetable, WaterAnimal
    • TemplateStuff
      • [[HTMLHandling]] - DataValidation.
      • [[HTTPHandling]] - For WebDyne
      • LaTeX
      • LoggingStuff
      • MarkdownHandling
      • PdfStuff
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting - For Pandoc
      • Simple static sitebuilder - Uses Moose and YAML.
      • Uses Template::Toolkit and Markdown and Pandoc
      • https://aphra.perlhacks.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Dapper - A publishing tool for static websites.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::url - Format a URL according to a sprintf-like template.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Wikidata-Template-CS-CitaceMonografie - Base class for Wikidata command line tool wd-citace-monografie.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Business-LetterWriter - Templates in JSON format.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::JPack - Offline/Online Web Application Packer.
      • Uses Template::Plexsite below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HP-Handy - A tiny Jinja2-compatible template engine for Perl 5.5.3 and later.
      • See also HTTP::Handy
      • Contains HTML::Blitz::Builder - High-performance, selector-based, content-aware HTML template engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Blitz
      • Pod has a discussion of performance
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HTML-Blitz-Builder - Create HTML code dynamically and safely.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Template::Associate - Associate relevant packages with HTML::Template.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Template::Nest - Manipulate a nested html template structure via a perl hash.
      • See HTML::Blitz above - Can be extremely slow when processing large amounts of data.
      • say "Read @{[$#$module_list + 1]} modules from modules.sqlite" - my($module_list) = [1, 2];.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Jinja2-TT2 - .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mason - Powerful, high-performance templating for the web and beyond.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mojo::Template - Perl-ish templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Pango - Layout and render international text.
      • https://developer.gnome.org/pygtk/stable/pango-markup-language.html
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Plate - Fast templating engine with support for embedded Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Interpolate::Named - Interpolated named arguments in string.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/String-Interpolate-RE - Interpolate variables into strings using regular expressions.
      • Text::Handlebars (http://handlebarsjs.com/) - Template::Liquid.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template - Front-end module to the Template Toolkit.
      • See also Template::Tiny, Text::Template::Tiny below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::AutoFilter - Template::Toolkit with automatic filtering.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::EmbeddedPerl - A template processing engine using embedding Perl code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Nest - Manipulate a generic template structure via a perl hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Plex - (P)erl (L)exical and (EX)tendable Templating.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Plexsite - Templating for interlinked templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Plugin-Autoformat - Interface to Text::Autoformat module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Plugin::CSV - Plugin for generating CSV.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Plugin-Latex - Template Toolkit plugin for Latex.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Plugin::StringTree - Access tree-like groups of strings naturally in code and Templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Provider - Provider module for loading/compiling templates.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Provider-Pandoc - Pre-process templates with Pandoc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Template-Tiny - Template Toolkit re-implemented in as little code as possible.
      • See Template above and Text::Template::Tiny below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Layout - Pango style markup formatting.
      • This module will co-operate with PDF::API2, PDF::Builder, Cairo, and Pango
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-MustacheTemplate - Fast and specification compliant Mustache template engine for Perl.
      • See also https://mustache.github.io/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-NeatTemplate - A fast, middleweight template engine.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Template::Plugin::Number::Format - Plugin/filter interface to Number::Format.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Template - Expand template text with embedded Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Template-Tiny - Variable substituting template processor.
      • See Template and Template::Tiny above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Textile - A humane web text generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Textile2MarcdownStandalone - Standalone converter from Textile markup to Markdown.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Xslate - Scalable template engine for Perl5.
      • Even faster than HTML::Blitz
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/YATT-Lite - Template with "use strict".
    • TestingHelp
      • DataTraversal - For Data::Table::Gherkin - CpanFile.
      • DatesAndTimes
      • DebuggingStuff - For Debug::* & Devel::*
      • ExceptionHandling
      • FileHandling
      • ModuleBuilding
      • NewOperators
      • PodHandling - For various POD spelling checkers
      • StringStuff
      • Test::InDomain within DataValidation
      • test.openssl.pl - Test regexps
      • UtilityStuff - For Metabase, perlport
      • WantArray
      • https://fast2-matrix.cpantesters.org/ - For test results for any module
      • https://magpie.cpantesters.org/
      • https://perlmaven.com/devel-cover-for-markua-parser
      • https://github.com/MartijnLievaart/penv - A script to set up a new directory+cpanm for each type of testing.
      • https://github.com/FGasper/perl-github-action-tips - Tips for testing Perl modules via GitHub.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::CPANTS::Lint - Front-end to Module::CPANTS::Analyse.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::local::lib::helper - Make it easy to run code against a local-lib.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Prove - Implements the prove command.
      • Part of Test::Harness (below)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Prove-Plugin-Metrics - Emit metrics when running prove.
      • Contains TAP::Harness::Metrics, TAP::Parser::Metrics
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::Prove::Plugin::SetEnv - A prove plugin to set environment variables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::ReportPrereqs - Report prerequisite versions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Run-Command-ToFail - Base class for run-command-to-fail tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-SimpleScan - simple_scan's core code.
      • Contains simple_scan and simple_report and many examples
      • simple_scan reads test specifications from standard input and generates Perl code based on these specifications
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Test-Generator - Generate fuzz and corpus-driven test harnesses.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Yath-Plugin-Utilization - System-utilization gating for yath test runs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Yath-Script - Script initialization and utility functions for Test2::Harness.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Archive-Merged - Virtually merge two archives.
      • Meaning you can interrogate one or more archives or directories
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Assert-Refute - Unified testing and assertion tool.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/assertions - Select assertions in blocks of code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Carp::Assert - Executable comments.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Carp::Assert::More - Convenience assertions for common situations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Chicken-Ipsum - Generate random chicken noises.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/circular::require - Detect circularity in use/require statements.
      • See also Test::CircularDependencies
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Config-Model-Tester - Test framework for Config::Model.
      • https://fast2-matrix.cpantesters.org/?dist=Tree::DAG_Node - https://matrix.perl-magpie.org/dist.php?dist=Tree-DAG_Node.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Test-Dummy-Perl5-ModuleBuildTiny - Test distribution that depends on Module::Build::Tiny.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/CPAN-Test-Dummy-Perl5-ExtUtilsMakeMaker - Test distribution that depends on ExtUtils::MakeMaker.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cucumber-Messages - Library of classes to encapsulate Cucumber messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cucumber-TagExpressions - Tag expression parser.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Cucumber-Test-Release-Automation - In lib/Cucumber/Test/Release/Automation.pm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Compare - Compare Perl data structures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Maker - Simple, flexible and extensible generation of realistic data - Iincludes gender.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Person - Data object for person - Includes gender.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Random - Perl module to generate random data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::RandomPerson - Create random person data - Includes gender.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Random-Person - Random person objects.
      • Could use Mock::Person::US instead of Mock::Person:CZ
      • Uses Data::Person
      • Includes gender
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Random-String-Matches - Generate random strings matching a regex.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Random::Weighted - Get weighted random data.
      • Limited to picking 1 item
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Range - Work with a range of dates.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Date::Simple - A simple date object.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::SimplePassword - Simple random password generator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Event::Random - DateTime extension for creating random datetimes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DateTime::Event::Recurrence - DateTime::Set extension for create basic recurrence sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBD::Mock::Session::GenerateFixtures - This distribution generates fixtures for DBD-Mock-Session module.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Class-MockData - Generate mock test data for DBIx::Class schemas.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Environment-Is - Detect environments like Docker or WSL.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Fennec - A testers toolbox, and best friend - Deprecated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Gherkin - A parser and compiler for the Gherkin language.
      • See Test::BDD::Cucumber below
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Getopt-Kingpin - Command line options parser (like golang kingpin).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HealthCheck - A health check for your code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/HealthCheck-Parallel - A HealthCheck that uses parallelization for running checks.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/JMAP::Tester - A JMAP client made for testing JMAP servers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/JMAP-Tester-WebSocket - A WebSocket JMAP client made for testing JMAP servers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Keyword::DEVELOPMENT - Have code blocks which don't exist unless you ask for them..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mock-Config - Temporarily set Config or XSConfig values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Mock-Data - Extensible toolkit for generating mock data.
      • Has a dozen subclasses
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mock::Data::Regex - Generator that uses a Regex as a template to generate strings.
      • Similar to String::Random::Regexp::regxstring in StringStuff
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mock::Person - Generates random last, first and middle name of person.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mock::Populate - Mock data creation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Mockery - Mock objects for testing purposes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Module::CPANTS::Analyse - Generate Kwalitee ratings for a distribution.
      • See also App::CPANTS::Lint
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Mask - Pretend certain modules are not installed.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Noise - Pure Perl implementation of the Noise Protocol Framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Perl::MinimumVersion::Fast - Find a minimum required version of perl for Perl code.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PerlX::Assert - Yet another assertion keyword.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack - Test PSGI applications with various backends .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Plack-Test-Agent - OO interface for testing low-level Plack/PSGI apps.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Day - Class for random day generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Day::InTheFuture - Class for random day generation in the future.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Day::InThePast - Class for random day generation in the past.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Random::Set - Class for random set generation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Result::Simple - A dead simple perl-ish Result like F#, Rust, Go, etc.
      • Result represents a function's return value as success or failure, enabling safer error handling and more effective control flow management
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Scientist - Test new code against old..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/SimpleMock - A simple mocking framework for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sub::Assert - Subroutine pre- and postconditions, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Subs-Trace - Trace all calls in a package.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Syntax::Keyword::Assert - Assert keyword for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/TAP::DOM - TAP as Document Object Model.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/TAP-Formatter-HTML - TAP Test Harness output delegate for html output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Harness - Prove supports TAP::Harness plugins and has a plugin system of its own.
      • Usage: prove -Ilib [-j 9] -r t where t is the dir name
      • See "FORMATTERS" in prove, "SOURCE HANDLERS" in prove and App::Prove for more details
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tapper-Cmd - Backend functions for CLI and Web.
      • Search MetaCPAN for Tapper:: to find other components of this set
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Task::Perl::Critic - Install everything Perl::Critic.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Testcontainers - Makes it simple to create and clean up container-based dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Against-Commit - Test CPAN modules against Perl dev releases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Against-Dev - Test CPAN modules against Perl dev releases.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-BDD-Cucumber - Feature-complete Cucumber-style testing in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Builder - Back-end for building test libraries.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Changes-Strict-Simple - Strict semantic validation for CPAN Changes files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CheckDeps - Check for presence of dependencies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-CheckGitStatus - Check git status after every test.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-CircularDependencies - Make sure non of the modules depend on themselves.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Class - Easily create test classes in an xUnit/JUnit style.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Class-Moose - Serious testing for serious Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CleanNamespaces - Check for uncleaned imports.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Compile - Assert that your Perl files compile OK.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CPAN::Changes - Validation of the Changes file in a CPAN distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CPAN::Changes::ReallyStrict - Ensure a Changes file looks exactly like it would if it was machine generated.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CPAN::Meta - Validate your CPAN META.yml files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::CPAN::Meta::JSON - Validate a META.json file within a CPAN distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-CVE - Test against known CVE's.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Data - Provides utility functions to check properties and values of data and variables.
      • See also Mock::Data above
      • Create temporary test databases and run tests in all available ones - https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Database-Temp.
      • Database::Temp is also in DatabaseAndSQL
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Deep - Extremely flexible deep comparison.
      • Has over 40 sub-modules
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Deep-Hashbag - A Test::Deep hash comparator ignoring hash keys.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Dependencies - Ensure that the dependency listing is complete.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::DescribeMe - Tell test runners what kind of test you are.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Differences - Test strings and data structures and show differences if not ok.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::DistManifest - Author test that validates a package MANIFEST.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Env - Test the environment.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Exception - Test exception-based code.
      • See also Test::Fatal
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Expander - Expansion of test functionalities that appear to be frequently used while testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-ExpectAndCheck - Expect/check-style unit testing with object methods.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Fatal - Incredibly simple helpers for testing code with exceptions.
      • See also Test::Exception
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-File - Test file attributes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-File-Contents - Test routines for examining the contents of files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-File-Cmp - Test routine for file comparison independent of CRLF or LF line endings..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::File::ShareDir - Create a Fake ShareDir for your modules for testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::File::ShareDir::Dist - Simplified dist oriented ShareDir tester.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Fixme - Check code for FIXMEs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Future-IO-Impl - Acceptance tests for Future::IO implementations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Harness - Run Perl standard test scripts with statistics.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-HTML-Content - Perl extension for testing HTML output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-HTTP-LocalServer - Spawn a local HTTP server for testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-HTTP-Scenario - Deterministic record/replay of HTTP interactions for test suites.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-HTTPStatus - Check an HTTP status.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Inline - Embed your tests in your code, next to what is being tested.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Instruction - A test framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Is - Skip test in a declarative way, following the Lancaster Consensus.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-ISBN - Check International Standard Book Numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-IsAny - Check if a value is any of the given values.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-JSON-Schema - Test your data against a JSON Schema.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::JSON::Schema::Acceptance - Acceptance testing for JSON-Schema based validators.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Kit - Build custom test packages with only the features you want.
      • Similar modules: Import::Base, Toolset
      • Test::Kit largely differs from these in that it always makes your module behave like Test::More
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Kwalitee - Test the Kwalitee of a distribution before you release it.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Kwalitee::Extra - Run Kwalitee tests including optional indicators, especially, prereq_matches_use.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Manifest - Interact with a t/test_manifest file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Memcached - Memcached Runner For Tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::MinimumVersion - Does your code require newer perl than you think?.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::MinimumVersion::Fast - Does your code require newer perl than you think?.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::MixedScripts - Test text for mixed and potentially confusable Unicode scripts.
      • See also https://util.unicode.org/UnicodeJsps/confusables.jsp
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Mock-Guard - Simple mock test library using RAII.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-MockFile - Allows tests to validate code that can interact with files without touching the file system.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Mockingbird - Advanced mocking library for Perl with support for dependency injection and spies.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-MockModule - Override subroutines in a module for unit testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::MockTime - Replaces actual time with simulated time.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-MockTime-HiRes - Replaces actual time with simulated high resolution time.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Mojibake - Check your source for encoding misbehavior.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::More - See Test::Simple or Test::Simpler.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Most-Explain - Enhanced diagnostics for Test::More with structured diffs and helpful hints.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::mysqld - mysqld runner for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Needs - Skip tests when modules not available.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Nginx - Data-driven test scaffold for Nginx C module and Nginx/OpenResty-based libraries and applications.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::NoBreakpoints - Test that files do not contain soft breakpoints.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::NoTabs - Check the presence of tabs in your project.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::NoWarnings - Make sure you didn't emit any warnings while testing.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Output - Utilities to test STDOUT and STDERR messages.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Perl::Critic - Use Perl::Critic in test programs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Perl::Critic::Progressive - Gradually enforce coding standards.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-PerlTidy - Check that all your files are tidy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Pod - Check for POD errors in files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Pod::Coverage - Check for pod coverage in your distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Pod::Spelling - A Test library to spell-check POD filesTest::Pod::Spelling - A Test library to spell-check POD files.
      • See also Pod::Spelling, Test::Spelling
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Pod::Spelling::CommonMistakes - Checks POD for common spelling mistakes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Portability::Files - Check file names portability.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::PostgreSQL - PostgreSQL runner for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-PostgreSQL-v2 - A modern, isolated PostgreSQL executable runner for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Prereq - Check if Makefile.PL has the right prerequisites.
      • Supersedes the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Prereq-Meta - Test distribution prerequisites against CPAN meta data file.
      • But see Test::Prereq
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-QuickGen - Utilities for generating random test data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-RedisServer - Redis-server runner for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Regexp - Test your regular expressions.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Regexp::Pattern - Test Regexp::Pattern patterns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Reporter - Sends test results to cpan-testers@perl.org.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Reporter::Transport - Base class for Test::Reporter transports.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Reporter::Transport::Metabase - Metabase transport for Test::Reporter.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Requires - Checks to see if the module can be loaded.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Returns - Verify that a method's output agrees with its specification.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Routine - Composable units of assertion.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Script - Basic cross-platform tests for scripts.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Settings - Ask or tell when certain types of tests should be run.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::SFTP - An object to help test SFTPs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Signature - Automated SIGNATURE testing.
      • See also Module::Signature in ModuleBuilding
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Simple - Basic utilities for writing tests.
      • Has a huge array of components
      • Contains Test::More and Test2
      • But see the next module
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Simpler - Simpler than Test::Simple; more powerful than Test::More.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Smoke - The Perl core test smoke suite.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-SPDX-Coverage - Perl Test Harness to verify all matched files in Manifest have a SPDX-License-Identifier.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Spelling - Check for spelling errors in POD files.
      • See also Test::Pod::Spelling
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Spelling-Stopwords - POD spell-checking with project-specific stopwords.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::SQLite - SQLite setup/teardown for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Strict - Check syntax, presence of use strict; and test coverage.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Subunit - Subunit version 2 binary protocol support.
      • Contains Test::Subunit, Test::Subunit::Diff
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-TCP - Testing TCP program.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-TempDir-Tiny - Temporary directories that stick around when tests fail.
      • Strongly recommended over File::Temp which uses global "our" variables, which affects Capture::Tiny, since the latter also uses File::Temp. Don't do that
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::TrailingSpace - Test for trailing space in source files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Trap - Trap exit codes, exceptions, output, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Unit - The PerlUnit testing framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-URI - Check Uniform Resource Identifiers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-utf8 - Handy utf8 tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-ValkeyServer - Valkey-server runner for tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Version - Check to see that version's in modules are sane.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Warn - Perl extension to test methods for warnings.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Warnings - Test for warnings and the lack of them.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Which - Skip tests if external programs are missing from PATH (with version checks).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::Without::Module - Test fallback behaviour in absence of modules.
      • See also Devel::Hide
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-WWW-Mechanize - Testing-specific WWW::Mechanize subclass.
      • There are many similar modules on MetaCPAN
      • https://metacpan.org/search?size=20&q=Test::WWW::Mechanize
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::WWW::Simple - Test Web applications using TAP.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::XML - Compare XML in perl tests.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::XML::Easy - Test XML with XML::Easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XML::SemanticDiff - Perl extension for comparing XML documents..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test::XML::Simple - Easy testing for XML.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-YAFT - Yet another testing framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/T2 - Define the T2 namespace that can always be used to access functionality from a Test2 bundle such as Test2::V1.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::API - Primary interface for writing Test2 based testing tools.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Bundle-Numerical - Numerical Quadmath/longdouble aware Test2 bundle.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/yath. Install via App::Yath - Contains yath, which replaces test and prove (see below for usage of prove).
      • https://perladvent.org/2023/2023-12-23.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Harness2-ChildSubReaper - Tiny XS wrapper around Linux's PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER prctl..
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Plugin-MemUsage - Collect and display memory usage information.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Plugin-SubtestFilter - Filter subtests by name.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Plugin-UUID - Use REAL UUIDs in Test2.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Require-ProgramInPath - Skip test unless a program exists in the PATH.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Suite - Distribution with a rich set of tools built upon the Test2 framework.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Tools - Documentation for Tools.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-ComboObject - Combine checks and diagnostics into a single test as an object.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-DOM - Tools to test HTML/XML-based DOM representations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-LoadModule - Test whether a module can be successfully loaded.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-PerlTidy - Test2 check that all of your Perl files are tidy.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-Pod - Check that POD syntax is valid.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Tools::Spec - RSPEC implementation on top of Test2::Workflow.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Tools::Subtest - Tools for writing subtests.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-TypeTiny - Test2 tools for checking Type::Tiny types.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Tools-URL - Compare a URL in your Test2 test.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Simple/view/lib/Test2/Transition.pod - Transition notes when upgrading to Test2.
      • See also: https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Suite
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test2-Util-DistFiles - Gather files in a distribution.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Test-Simple - Inline copy of Scope::Guard.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::V1 - V1 edition of the Test2 recommended bundle.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Test2::Workflow - A test workflow is a way of structuring tests using composable units.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Time::RTM - Run-time metrics stats.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/UNIVERSAL-dump - Add dump and other methods to all classes and objects.
    • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • DataTraversal - [[Color]].
      • LaTeX
      • LinguaStuff
      • MarkdownHandling
      • PerlDocumentation
      • PipeLines - For App::PTP
      • PodHandling
      • QrCodes
      • RegexpStuff
      • TheRFCGuide
      • SpreadsheetStuff
      • StringStuff
      • TemplateStuff
      • [[UTF8]]
      • WikiStuff
      • [[XML]]
      • https://fontawesome.com/- For a huge list of icons
      • https://github.com/junegunn/fzf - For the homepage of fzf. See also fd
      • https://openrefine.org/ - OpenRefine is a powerful free, open source tool for working with messy data
      • https://pandoc.org/ - A universal document converter
      • https://github.com/sharkdp/fd - For fd aka fd-find. See also fzf
      • https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/ - Not Perl but can use reStructuredText
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Aion-Format - Perl extension for formatting numbers, coloring output, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Diff - Compute differences between two files / lists.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Algorithm::Diff::XS - Algorithm::Diff with XS core loop.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-LCS-XS - Fast (XS) implementation of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Algorithm-MLCS - Fast heuristic algorithm for finding Longest Common Subsequence of multiple sequences.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Antsy - Streaming ANSI escape sequences.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::AlgorithmDiffUtils - CLI utilities based on Algorithm::Diff.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::FzfUtils - fzf is an interactive filter program for any kind of list.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Greple-aozora - Greple module for aozora-bunko proofreading.
      • The work of comparing and contrasting the input data with the original manuscript such as the base text and the input data is called "calibration" and searching for mistakes
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PPI-Dumper - Use the PPI to dump the structure of a Perl file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-PTP - An expressive Pipelining Text Processor .
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-TextTemplatePermuteUtils - CLI utilities related to Text::Template::Permute.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Ascii-Text - Module for generating ASCII text in various fonts and styles.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Text - Class to handle text in an OO way.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Text::Simple - Data object for text in language.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Dist-Surveyor - Survey installed modules and determine the specific distribution versions they came from.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::MultipleDiff - Compare multiple files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Hash::Diff - Return difference between two hashes as a hash.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Differences - Reasonable sane HTML diffing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/HTML::Quoted - Extract structure of quoted HTML mail message.
      • Various MUAs use quite different approaches for quoting in emails
      • Purpose and returned structures are very similar to those in Text::Quoted
      • Uses HTML::Parser
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Indent - Class for indent handling.
      • See also Tags::Output::Indent
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Indent::Block - Class for block indenting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Indent::Data - Class for data indenting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Indent::String - Class for text indenting.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Indent::Word - Class for word indenting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Intellexer-API - API client for Intellexer.
      • Perl API client for the Intellexer, a webservice that, "enables developers to embed Intellexer semantics products using XML or JSON."
      • Features
      • Automatically extract topics from text
      • Parse an input text stream and extract various linguistic information
      • Automatically extracts sentiments (positivity/negativity), opinion objects (e.g., product features with associated sentiment phrases) and emotions (liking, anger, disgust, etc.) in unstructured text data
      • Identifies elements in text and classifies them into predefined categories such as personal names, names of organizations, position/occupation, nationality, geographical location, date, age, duration and names of events
      • Automatically generates a summary (short description) of a document with its main ideas
      • With Related Facts automatically generates a summary (short description) from multiple documents with their main ideas
      • Accurately compares documents of any format and determines the degree of similarity between them
      • And more
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LCS - Longest Common Subsequence.
      • Contains LCS::Tiny
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LCS-Similar - Allow differences in the compared elements of Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/LCS-XS - Fast (XS) implementation of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Lingua-TermWeight - Language-independent TermWeight calculator.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Lingua::Thesaurus - Thesaurus management.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pandoc - Wrapper for the mighty Pandoc document converter.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Parse::RecDescent - Generate Recursive-Descent Parsers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Perl-MinimumVersion - Find a minimum required version of perl for Perl code.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PPI - Parse, Analyze and Manipulate Perl (without perl).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/PPIx-Regexp - Represent a regular expression of some sort.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PPIx::Utilities - Extensions to PPI..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PPIx::Utils - Utility functions for PPI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/PPI - Pattern-based Perl Recognizer.
      • The docs compare it with PPI
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Regexp::Grammars - Add grammatical parsing features to Perl 5.10 regexes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Diff - Simple diff to String.
      • Various ways of listing the difference between 2 strings
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Escape - Backslash escapes, quoted phrase, word elision, etc.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Glob::Permute - Expand {foo,bar,baz}[2-4] style string globs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Tags - Structure oriented SGML/XML/HTML/etc. elements manipulation.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-Output-Indent - Indent class for Tags.
      • See also Indent
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-Output-LibXML - Printing 'Tags' structure by LibXML library.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Term::ANSIScreen - Terminal control using ANSI escape sequences.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Amuse - Generate HTML and LaTeX documents from Emacs Muse markup.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Amuse-Compile - Compiler for Text::Amuse.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-ANSI-Fold - Text folding library supporting ANSI terminal sequence and Asian wide characters with prohibition character handling.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::ANSI::Printf - Printf function to print string including ANSI sequence.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-ANSI-Util - Routines for text containing ANSI color codes.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Autoformat - Automatic text wrapping and reformatting.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Balanced - Extract delimited text sequences from strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Conceal - Conceal and recover interface for text processing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Diff - Perform diffs on files and record sets.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Diff::FormattedHTML - Generate a colorful HTML diff of strings/files.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Diff::HTML - XHTML format for Text::Diff::Unified.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Diff-Parser - Parse patch files containing unified and standard diffs.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Diff::Unified::XS - The fast Text::Diff module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::DiffU - Diff two sequences and print unified-style output.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Diff3 - Three way text comparison and merging.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Diff3::Text - Line number scheme free text buffer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-FindIndent - Heuristically determine the indent style.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Fragment - Manipulate fragments in text .
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Glob - Match globbing patterns against text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Lorem - Generate random Latin looking text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Glob::Expand - Permute and expand glob-like text patterns.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Minify::XS - Remove indentation and trailing whitespace from multi-line text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-OutputFilter - Enable post processing of output without fork.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::ParseWords - Parse text into an array of tokens or array of arrays.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-PrettyTable - Allow for auto-fixed-width formatting of raw data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Quoted - Extract the structure of a quoted mail message.
      • Uses Text::Autoformat
      • Uses Text::Tabs
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Stencil - Fast XS list/table renderer with escaping, formatting, and transform chaining.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Tabs - Expand and unexpand tabs like unix expand(1) and unexpand(1).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Template-Permute - Template for generating permutation of text.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Unpack-Auto - Automatically generate unpack strings from fixed-width text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::vFile::asData - Parse vFile formatted files into data structures.
      • Reads vFile format files, such as vCard (RFC 2426) and vCalendar (RFC 2445)
      • An app using it! See https://metacpan.org/pod/App::MergeCal
      • Command line program that merges iCal files into a single calendar
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc2426, rfc2445
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::VisualPrintf - Printf family functions to handle Non-ASCII characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tree-Lexicon - Object class for storing and retrieving a lexicon in a tree of affixes.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/CHOROBA/Treex-PML-2.29 - Perl implementation for the Prague Markup Language (PML)..
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Treex::Unilang - Collection of blocks parametrized by language and language independent.
      • Includes Czech
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XML::Diff - XML DOM-Tree based Diff & Patch Module.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/YAML::Diff - Diff 2 YAML Documents Semantically.

    • TheRFCGuide
      • TheISOGuide - TheEUNormGuide.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc959 - FILE TRANSFER PROTOCOL (FTP).
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1950 - ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification version 3.3.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1952 - GZIP file format specification version 4.3.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1105 - A Border Gateway Protocol (BGP).
      • See rfc2283 & rfc1136 etc
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1136 - Administrative Domains and Routing Domains A Model for Routing in the Internet.
      • See rfc2283 & rfc1267 etc
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1267 - A Border Gateway Protocol 3 (BGP-3).
      • See rfc2283 & rfc1654 etc
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1654 - A Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4).
      • See rfc2283 & rfc1771 etc
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1771 - A Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4).
      • See rfc2283 & rfc4271 etc
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1939 - Post Office Protocol - Version 3.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1951 - DEFLATE Compressed Data Format Specification version 1.3.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2045 - Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) Part One: Format of Internet Message Bodies.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2047 - MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) Part Three: Message Header Extensions for Non-ASCII Text.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2109 - HTTP State Management Mechanism.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2822 - Internet Message Format.
      • Obsoleted by rfc5322
      • Updated by rfc5335, rfc5336
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2283 - Multiprotocol Extensions for BGP-4.
      • But see rfc2858 etc
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2426 - vCard MIME Directory Profile.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2428 - FTP Extensions for IPv6 and NATs.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2445 - Internet Calendaring and Scheduling Core Object Specification (iCalendar).
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2483 - RI Resolution Services Necessary for URN Resolution.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2518 - HTTP Extensions for Distributed Authoring -- WEBDAV.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2616 - Hypertext Transfer Protocol -- HTTP/1.1.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2822 - Internet Message Format.
      • https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2858 - Multiprotocol Extensions for BGP-4.
      • See also rfc4760
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3066 - Tags for the Identification of Languages.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3161 - Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Time-Stamp Protocol (TSP).
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3164 - The BSD syslog Protocol.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3261 - SIP: Session Initiation Protocol.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339 - For Date and Time on the Internet: Timestamps.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3394 - Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) Key Wrap Algorithm.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3492 - Punycode: A Bootstring encoding of Unicode for Internationalized Domain Names in Applications (IDNA).
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3501 - INTERNET MESSAGE ACCESS PROTOCOL - VERSION 4rev1.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3548 - The Base16, Base32, and Base64 Data Encodings.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3966 - The tel URI for Telephone Numbers.

      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4217 - Securing FTP with TLS.

      • See also rfc2283
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4287 - The Atom Syndication Format.

      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6266 - Use of the Content-Disposition Header Field in the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP).

      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6902 - JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Patch.

      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9554/ - vCard Format Extensions for JSContact.
      • https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9555/ - SContact: Converting from and to vCard.

    • TheWindowGuide
      • XWindowsStuff - WindowManagement.
    • TiddlyWikis
      • https://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/syntax - Syntax reference - WikiStuff.
      • https://feather.wiki/ - For Feather Wiki, a tiny version of a TiddlyWiki
      • https://foswiki.org/Home/About - For FosWiki, a collaboration tool
      • https://tiddlywiki.com/ - The TiddlyWiki ecosystem
      • Each page must use named paragraphs - Each TiddlyWiki is restricted to a single web page.
      • The purpose of these restrictions is to impose guidelines for the naming of things
      • These guidelines help people with no expertise in data collation to index items carefully
      • So, they help prevent growth-like-topsy, a major problem will old-style wikis
      • Do yourself a favour and always include a para in every TiddlyWiki called MainMenu (or Index or TableOfContents), and put in it an alphabetical list of para names
      • Editor (can be sent commands, and can generate HTML) - Search engine.
      • The 3 double-quotes at the top of this para is one such command, but they are only visible when you click the pencil (edit) icon to enter edit mode for a para
      • Encryption (good for passwords)
      • History of paras edited per day
      • Import capability (a way of sharing)
      • Programmable in that you can list what paras are to be opened automatically when the file is loaded into the web client (browser). Hence the para called GettingStarted
      • The homepage is a TiddlyWiki, and you really should read the para GettingStarted - See tiddlywiki.com for a free download and many add-ons from the eco-system.
      • It is quite different than my GettingStarted para
      • In the eco-system there are various public hosting options giving other ways to share besides the import feature mentioned above
    • ToonLanguage
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Token-Oriented_Object_Notation - TheLanguageGuide.

    • UsernamePassword
      • [[CPAN]] - Acronyms - for 2FA.
      • DatabaseAndSQL
      • DataTraversal
      • GitStuff
      • WebServices
      • https://openid.net/developers/how-connect-works/ - for OpenID Connect
      • https://savage.net.au/misc/Digital.Security.Wiki.html
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Amazon-Credentials - Fetch Amazon credentials from file, environment or role.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/App::genpw - (Gen)erate random password/strings, with (p)atterns and (w)ordlists.
      • Contains script genpw similar to Linux's genpw
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Auth::GoogleAuth - Google Authenticator TBOT Abstraction.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::TacacsPlus - Perl extension for authentication using tacacs+ server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Authen::TOTP - Interface to RFC6238 two factor authentication (2FA).
      • See also Trog::TOTP
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Concierge - Service layer orchestrator for authentication, sessions, and user data.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Concierge-Auth - Password authentication and token generation using Crypt::Passphrase.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Concierge-Sessions - Session manager with factory pattern and multiple backend support.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Concierge-Users - User data management with multiple storage backends.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::Password::Common - Check a password against a list of common passwords.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-Password-Manager - Generate, check, manage crypt - des passwords.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/DBIx::Array::Connect - Database Connections from an INI Configuration File.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-MyPassword - Store database authentication info in a CSV file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-Password - Allows you to create a global password file for DB password.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/DBIx-PasswordIniFile - Manages DBI connections with password and other params stored in a .ini style file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/File::KDBX - Encrypted database to store secret text and files.
      • Works like KeePassXC (https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc)
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/GitHub-Config-SSH-UserData - Read user data from comments in ssh config file.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/KeePass-Reader - Interface to KeePass V4 database files.
      • See also File::KDBX above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Module-Features-PasswordManager - Password manager is a tool that lets you input, store, and retrieve individual passwords securely.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/MooX-Role-CryptedPassword - Password attribute from a encrypted file.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::SAML2 - SAML2 bindings and protocol implementation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Pass::OTP - Perl implementation of HOTP / TOTP algorithms.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Password::Policy - Make managing multiple password strength profiles easy.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Mask - Mask sensitive data.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Mask::XS - Mask sensitive data faster.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/String::Secret - Secret string wrapper to mask secret from logger.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-HTML-ChangePassword - Tags helper for change password.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Tags-HTML-Login-Access - Tags helper for login access.
      • Helps generate code for CSS and HTML Login form - Tags helper for login register.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Password::Pronounceable - Generate pronounceable passwords.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Trog-TOTP - Fork of Authen::TOTP.
      • SAML 2.0 (Security Assertion Markup Language 2.0) is an open standard that enables secure Single Sign-On (SSO) for web-based applications - Constants for urn:oasis SAML2 implementations.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/URN-OASIS-SAML2
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-HashiCorp-Vault - Perl API for HashiCorp's Vault.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Win32-Credentials - Read/Write Windows Credential Manager via Win32 API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::Zitadel - Perl client for Zitadel identity management (OIDC + Management API).
      • https://zitadel.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Zitadel - OIDC client for Zitadel - token verification, JWKS, discovery.
    • UTF8
      • FileHandling - For Path::Class, Path::Tiny - DataTraversal - For notes on UTF-8.
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • https://github.com/JuliaStrings/utf8proc - For utf8proc
      • https://github.com/tsibley/tchrist-OSCON2011-Unicode
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunicode
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perluniintro
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunitut
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/ - STDERR->binmode(":encoding(UTF-8)");
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/ - binmode STDIN, ":encoding(UTF-8)";
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/ - binmode STDOUT, ":encoding(UTF-8)";
      • https://perldoc.perl.org/ - binmode STDERR, ":encoding(UTF-8)";
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunicode#Forcing-Unicode-in-Perl-(Or-Unforcing-Unicode-in-Perl)
      • https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6162484/why-does-modern-perl-avoid-utf-8-by-default/6163129#6163129
      • https://www.unicode.org/versions/Unicode16.0.0/
      • https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr44/
      • https://www.endpointdev.com/blog/2025/04/encoding-in-perl/
      • See https://www.reddit.com/r/perl/comments/1d5k4cm/deaccentifying_characters/
      • Search::Tokenizer uses Text::Transliterator::Unaccent
      • Text::StripAccents only handles Latin1
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-safer - CLI for Text::Safer.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-uniprint - Print each Unicode character of input, along with its codepoint and name.
      • Unicode::UTF8. Its encode_utf8 is not equivalent to encode_utf8 in Encode, which should be avoided - Encode::Simple (has interesting comments, e.g. next comment).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Encode - Character encodings in Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Encode::Simple - Encode and decode text, simply.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Encode-Wide - Convert wide characters (Unicode) into HTML or XML-safe ASCII entities.
      • Try this code with and without - use 'unicode_strings';.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ODF::lpOD_Helper - Fix and enhance ODF::lpOD.
      • Associated code
      • ODF::lpOD - An OpenDocument management interface
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/ODF::lpOD
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Sys::Binmode - A fix for Perlâs system call character encoding.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Iconv - Perl interface to iconv() codeset conversion function.
      • Multi-lingual messages - Read and write PO files.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-PO
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Safer - Convert text with one of several available methods, usually to a safer/more restricted encoding, e.g. for filenames.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-StripAccents - Removes accented & special characters from strings.
      • But see Text::Unaccent
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Transliterator - Compile a transliterator from Unicode tables, to remove accents from text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::VisualPrintf - Printf family functions to handle Non-ASCII characters.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-Unaccent - Remove accents from a string.
      • Has a C dependency
      • See Text::StripAccents above
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::Unidecode - Plain ASCII transliterations of Unicode text.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::WideChar::Util - Routines for text containing wide characters.
      • Module contains a See Also
      • https://www.perl.com/pub/2012/04/perlunicook-standard-preamble.html/ - https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6162484/why-does-modern-perl-avoid-utf-8-by-default/6163129#6163129.
      • Tom's Unicode Scripts So Life is Easier - Unicode::Tussle.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Tussle
      • Sample usage
      • See also
      • Provides accsess at the information found in the Unicode Character Database.
      • perluniprops
      • unichars from Unicode-Tussle (e.g. unichars '\p{Hiragana}')
      • See also charnames
      • Sample usage
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Block::List - Access to Unicode Block names and tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::CheckUTF8 - Checks if scalar is valid UTF-8.
      • Note: Strings in @not_sorted, $a and $b are interpreted according to Perl's Unicode support. See https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunicode, https://metacpan.org/pod/perluniintro, https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunitut, https://metacpan.org/pod/perlunifaq, https://metacpan.org/pod/utf8. Otherwise you can use preprocess or should decode them before - Notes copied from the synopsis.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Escape - Escape and unescape Unicode characters other than ASCI.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::GCString - String as Sequence of UAX #29 Grapheme Clusters.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Normalize - Unicode Normalization Forms.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Precis - RFC 7564 PRECIS Framework - Enforcement and Comparison.
      • See TheRFCGuide for rfc7564
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Precis::Preparation - RFC 8264 PRECIS Framework - Preparation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Stringprep - Preparation of Internationalized Strings (RFC 3454).
      • https://metacpan.org/release/RURBAN/Unicode-Towctrans-0.04 - Generate Wide-Char Unicode Case Mapping Tables.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Unicode::Unihan - The Unihan Data Base 5.1.0.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Unicode-UTF8 - Encoding and decoding of UTF-8 encoding form.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/utf8-all - Turn on Unicode - all of it.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UTF8::R2 - Makes UTF-8 scripting easy for enterprise use.

    • UUID
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apophis - CryptoStuff - UUID uses ChaCha20 which is in CryptX.
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horus
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sekhmet
      • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier
      • http://e2fsprogs.sourceforge.net/
      • https://github.com/ulid/spec
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Apophis - Content-addressable storage with deterministic UUID v5 identifiers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::GUID - Globally unique identifiers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::GUID::Any - Generic interface for GUID/UUID creation.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::KSUID - K-Sortable Unique IDentifiers.
      • https://github.com/segmentio/ksuid
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Data-ULID - Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier.
      • See also ULID::Tiny below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::UUID - Globally/Universally Unique Identifiers (GUIDs/UUIDs).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::UUID::LibUUID - uuid.h based UUID generation (versions 2 and 4 depending on platform).
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Data::UUID::MT - Fast random UUID generator using the Mersenne Twister algorithm.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Horus - Ultra-fast XS UUID/GUID generator supporting all RFC 9562 versions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Sekhmet - Ultra-fast XS ULID generator built on Horus.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/ULID-Tiny - A lightweight ULID (Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier) generator.
      • See also Data::ULID above
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/UUID - DCE compatible Universally Unique Identifier library for Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::Random - Generate random uuid strings.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::Random::Patch::UseMRS - Make UUID::Random use Math::Random::Secure's rand().
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::Random::Secure - Like UUID::Random, but uses Math::Random::Secure for random numbers.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::Tiny - Pure Perl UUID Support With Functional Interface.
      • Warning: Low quality. But see UUID::Tiny::Patch::UseMRS below
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::Tiny::Patch::UseMRS - Make UUID::Tiny use Math::Random::Secure's rand().
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/UUID::URandom - UUIDs based on /dev/urandom or the Windows Crypto API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/UUID4-Tiny - Cryptographically secure v4 UUIDs for Linux x64.
      • https://github.com/ulid/spec

      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Version::Next - Increment module version numbers simply and correctly.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Version-Semantic - Tiny SemVer based class implementation.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Bugzilla - Perl API for Bugzilla REST API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Careerjet - Perl interface to Careerjet's public job search API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Client - A base role for quickly and easily creating web service clients.
      • POD contains a number of examples built using this module
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Dropbox - Perl interface to Dropbox API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Fastly - An interface to most facets of the Fastly API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-HashiCorp-Vault - Perl API for HashiCorp's Vault.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-IdoitAPI - A library to access the i-doit JSON RPC API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Webservice-KeyVal-API - Perl API client for the REPLACE API service, https://keyval.org/.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-OpenStates - Client for the Plural Open States API.
      • State-level legislator information for US locations, provided via the Plural Open States API
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-OurWorldInData - Connect with the Our World in Data API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Simple - Simple Interface To Web Services APIs.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-Slack-WebApi - A simple wrapper for Slack Web API.
      • New doc site: https://docs.slack.dev/
      • Old doc site: https://api.slack.com/web
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WebService::TogetherWeRemember - Together We Remember!.
      • https://togetherweremember.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WebService-YTSearch - Search YouTube.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Weenect-API - API to Weenect tracker server.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Workflow - Simple, flexible system to implement workflows.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Bizowie-API - Perl API interface to Bizowie's erp software.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Bund - Perl client for German Federal Government APIs (bund.dev).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Codeguard - Perl interface to interact with the Codeguard API.
      • https://www.codeguard.com/
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Crawl - A simple web crawler for extracting links and more from web pages.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-FetchStory - Fetch a story from a fiction website.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Hetzner - Perl client for Hetzner APIs (Cloud, Storage, Robot).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Picnic - Library to access Picnic Supermarket API.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-RobotRules - Database of robots.txt-derived permissions.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-RobotRules-Extended - Database of robots.txt-derived permissions. This is a fork of WWW::RobotRules.
      • Interface for the Shodan Computer Search Engine API - https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Shodan-API.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::SnipeIT - API Access to Snipe-IT.
      • Snipe-IT is an asset inventory management system
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Spotify - Spotify Web API Wrapper.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/WWW-Tumblr - Perl bindings for the Tumblr API.
      • https://metacpan.org/release/BYTEROCK/WWW-Vonage-API-0.01 - Accessing Vonage's REST API with Perl.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/WWW::YaCyBlacklist - A Perl module to parse and execute YaCy blacklists.
      • YaCy (pronounced "ya see") is a fascinating project: a free, distributed, peer-to-peer (P2P) search engine
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/XML-Compile - Compilation based XML processing.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XML::Compile::WSS - OASIS Web Services Security.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/XML::Compile::WSS::Signature - This extension implements cypto signatures.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Zabbix7::API - Access the JSON-RPC API of a Zabbix server.
      • Zabbix is an enterprise-class open source distributed monitoring solution
    • WeirdAndWonderful

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/perlude - Is an on-demand operator that can easily compose actions on potentially very large amount of data.

    • WikiStuff
      • TemplateStuff - MarkdownHandling.
      • TextAnalysisAndFormatting
      • TiddlyWikis - For free & fabulous TiddlyWikis like this one
      • http://www.kwiki.org
      • https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/ - This tool can list entries of some external databases, and allows users to match them against Wikidata items
      • https://openrefine.org/ - OpenRefine is a powerful free, open source tool for working with messy data
      • https://wikidata.reconci.link/ - Wikidata reconciliation for OpenRefine
      • https://wikiba.se/ - Wikibase is an open-source software suite
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/App-Wikidata-Print - Base class for Wikidata command line tool wd-print.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Kwiki - A Quickie Wiki that's not too Tricky.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/CGI::Wiki::Kwiki - An instant wiki built on CGI::Wiki.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Kwiki::Kwiki - Kwiki Kwiki is a Wiki.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Text-MediawikiFormat - Translate Wiki markup into other text formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Text::WikiFormat - Module for translating Wiki formatted text into other formats.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Toolforge::MixNMatch::Object - Toolforge Mix'n'match tool objects.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wiki-JSON - Parse wiki-like articles to a data-structure transformable to JSON.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wiki-Toolkit - A toolkit for building Wikis.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Wiki::Toolkit::Formatter::Default - A formatter for Wiki::Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Wiki::Toolkit::Formatter::Mediawiki - A Mediawiki-style formatter for Wiki::Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Wiki::Toolkit::Formatter::XHTMLMediaWiki - A Mediawiki-style formatter for Wiki::Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Wiki::Toolkit::Store::Database - Parent class for database storage backends for Wiki::Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/pod/Wiki::Toolkit::Store::Mediawiki - Mediawiki (MySQL) storage backend for Wiki::Toolkit.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wikibase-Cache-Backend-Basic - Wikibase cache backend to local static basic ids (units, common properties).
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wikibase-Datatype - Wikibase datatypes.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wikibase-Datatype-Print - Wikibase datatype print helpers.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wikibase-Datatype-Query - Query class on Wikibase item.
      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Wikibase-Datatype-Struct - Wikibase structure serialization via JSON to MediaWiki.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Alien-wxWidgets - Building, finding and using wxWidgets binaries.

      • https://metacpan.org/dist/Win32-NetAdmin - Manage network groups and users.

      • A wrapper for the wxWidgets (formerly known as wxWindows) GUI toolkit
